elements.name: lr-activity-feed
elements.description:
`<lr-activity-feed>` ŌĆö an append-only streaming log of granular agent actions ("Searching the
webŌĆ”", "Read src/index.ts"), collapsing to a localized "Completed N steps" summary once the run
is over. Entries never change state once added (a step whose status mutates in place belongs to
`<lr-task-list>` instead). Implements the shared follow (stick-to-bottom) contract: `follow`
is a component-managed, host-assignable property, released on user scroll-up and re-engaged at
the bottom, firing `lr-follow-change` on every transition (mount excluded). At/above
`virtualizeThreshold` entries, the body renders through an internal `<lr-virtual-list>`
instead of a plain keyed list ŌĆö same list semantics either way, keyed by `id`.

Each entry's `text` renders as plain text by default; a host needing richer per-entry content
(rendered markdown, a trailing tool-call chip list, etc.) sets `renderText` to fully replace it,
identically whether or not the feed is currently virtualized.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `body` | The scrollable region containing the entries (or the internal virtual-list). |
| `entry` | One entry row; carries `data-tone`. |
| `entry-icon` | The literal `icon` hint, or a tone dot when unset. |
| `entry-text` | The entry's `text`. Not rendered while `renderText` is set ŌĆö its returned content replaces this part entirely. |
| `entry-timestamp` | The formatted timestamp, only rendered while `showTimestamps` and a valid `timestamp` is set. |
| `header` | The clickable header (`<button>`). |
| `label` | The header's title text ŌĆö `label`, or its localized default when `label` is left at `'Activity'`. |
| `status-dot` | The decorative mode indicator dot; pulses while `mode="live"`. |
| `summary` | The header's one-line ticker (`live`) or completed-count summary (`post-hoc`). |
| `toggle` | The chevron indicator inside the header. |
| `tone-dot` | The tone dot rendered inside `entry-icon` when the entry sets no literal `icon`. Its own named part rather than an internal class, so it stays styleable in both the plain and virtualized rendering paths and reachable from a consumer's `::part()`. Also carries a tone-specific name, since `::part()` cannot be qualified by `[data-tone]`. |
| `tone-dot-brand` | A `brand`-tone entry's dot (also carries `tone-dot`). |
| `tone-dot-danger` | A `danger`-tone entry's dot (also carries `tone-dot`). |
| `tone-dot-neutral` | An untoned entry's dot (also carries `tone-dot`). |
| `tone-dot-success` | A `success`-tone entry's dot (also carries `tone-dot`). |
| `tone-dot-warning` | A `warning`-tone entry's dot (also carries `tone-dot`). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-activity-feed-max-height` (default: `16rem`) ŌĆö Cap on how tall the expanded body grows before it scrolls internally (non-virtualized mode); also sizes the internal virtual-list.

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Body visibility. Never self-mutated on `mode` changes ŌĆö a host wanting the finished feed
collapsed sets `mode="post-hoc"` and `expanded=false` together.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: follow
attributes.description:
Component-managed, host-assignable stick-to-bottom flag ŌĆö released on user scroll-up,
re-engaged at the bottom. Only drives scrolling in `'live'` mode.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Header text. Localized (`activityFeedLabel`) while at its default `'Activity'`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Activity'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
`'live'` follows the tail (per `follow`) and pulses; `'post-hoc'` shows the completed-count
summary and never scrolls.

Type: `ActivityFeedMode`  
Default: `'live'`

attributes.name: show-timestamps
attributes.description:
Trailing `<time datetime>` per entry, default `hour:minute` in `effectiveLocale`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: virtualize-threshold
attributes.description:
At/above this entry count, the body renders through an internal `<lr-virtual-list>`.

Type: `number`  
Default: `200`

elements.name: lr-agent-run
elements.description:
`<lr-agent-run>` ŌĆö the top-level shell for one `AgentRun`: lifecycle-status badge, elapsed
time, current step, model/cost summary, and built-in Cancel/Retry controls in a header, plus
four named composition slots (`tasks`/`tools`/`reasoning`/`output`) for the run's actual
content. This is deliberately a SHELL, not a new step-rendering surface ŌĆö every piece of
per-step or per-invocation rendering routes through an existing primitive:

- **Elapsed time**: composes `<lr-generation-status>` (`active`/`started-at`, its own built-in
  Stop button hidden via `show-stop="false"` since this component renders its own Cancel/Retry
  pair instead) for the *live, ticking* readout while the run is genuinely in progress
  (`running`/`collecting`/`waiting-input`/`waiting-approval`). `<lr-stream-status>` doesn't fit:
  its `phase` vocabulary
  (`idle`/`connecting`/`streaming`/`stalled`) models transport/connection health, not an agent
  run's nine built-in lifecycle statuses (plus application-defined extensions), and it exposes no elapsed-time readout at all ŌĆö exactly the
  distinction `<lr-generation-status>`'s own class doc already draws between the two. Once the
  run reaches a terminal state (`done`/`error`/`cancelled`) with both a `startedAt` and an
  `endedAt`, this component instead renders a small locally-formatted static duration
  (`endedAt - startedAt`): `<lr-generation-status>`'s freeze-on-`active=false` semantics only
  ever freeze at whatever it last computed *live*, so mounting it directly against a completed
  run loaded from history (e.g. `startedAt` yesterday, `endedAt` five minutes later, loaded
  today) would either show a stale zero or the wrong multi-hour span ŌĆö it has no way to render a
  fixed historical span on demand. That static fallback reuses the same small `formatDuration()`
  algorithm `<lr-usage-badge>` and `<lr-tool-call-chip>` already duplicate locally for the same
  reason (see this file's own copy).
- **Model + cost summary**: composes `<lr-usage-badge>`, fed `run.costEstimate` (formatted via
  `formatCost`, or a plain `Intl.NumberFormat` by default ŌĆö this library never assumes a
  currency, see `<lr-format-number>`'s own explicit `currency` prop) as its `cost-text`.
  `run.model` (a plain string with no analogous `<lr-usage-badge>` property) renders alongside
  as plain text.
- **Current step**: a single-line summary of whichever `run.steps` entry currently has
  `status.kind === 'running'` (the last such entry, if more than one) ŌĆö a plain text line, not a
  list, so it doesn't duplicate `<lr-task-list>`'s own per-item rendering.
- **Tasks slot default content**: when the host doesn't slot anything into `tasks` and
  `run.steps` is non-empty, this component's own `<slot>` fallback renders a `<lr-task-list>`
  populated by mapping every `AgentStep` to a `TaskItem` (see `toTaskItem()`) ŌĆö a plain data
  adapter between the two existing shapes, not new rendering.
- **Status badge**: composes `<lr-badge>`. **Empty state**: composes `<lr-empty>` when `run` is
  `null`.

`tools`/`reasoning`/`output` are plain named slots with no default content ŌĆö entirely the
host's own composition (typically `<lr-tool-call-chip>`/`<lr-tool-result-view>` rows,
reasoning/streaming text, and final output respectively). An `actions` slot adds extra header
controls alongside the built-in Cancel/Retry pair. The `header` and `summary` slots replace the
built-in lifecycle header and model/usage/metrics summary respectively. `statusLabels` and
`statusVariants` make application-defined lifecycle kinds first-class, while `metrics` renders
arbitrary labeled values such as prompt and completion token counts.

The built-in Cancel button renders while `showCancel` is true and the run's status is one of
`TICKING_KINDS` (still genuinely in progress); Retry renders while `showRetry` is true and the
status is `error` or `cancelled`. Clicking either fires `lr-cancel`/`lr-retry` with
`CancelEventDetail`/`RetryEventDetail` from `src/ai/types.ts` ŌĆö this component never cancels or
retries anything itself, it only requests. `RetryEventDetail.attempt` is a 1-based counter
local to this component, incremented on every `lr-retry` click and reset to `0` whenever
`run.id` changes (a genuinely new run replacing the old one, as opposed to the same run's status
merely updating in place).

Lifecycle transitions into an attention-needing or terminal state (`waiting-input`,
`waiting-approval`, `done`, `error`, `cancelled`) are announced through an internal
`<lr-live-region>`, mirroring `<lr-stream-status>`'s own stall/recover announcements ŌĆö
`running`/`idle` transitions are frequent and not independently actionable, so they stay
silent, and whatever status a freshly-assigned `run` (a new `run.id`) happens to already carry
is never itself treated as an eventful transition, only a later in-place change is.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | Extra header actions alongside the built-in Cancel/Retry buttons. |
| `header` | Replaces the built-in lifecycle header and its built-in actions. |
| `output` | The run's final output content. No default content. |
| `reasoning` | Reasoning/thinking content. No default content. |
| `summary` | Replaces the built-in model, usage, and metrics summary. |
| `tasks` | Task/plan content. Falls back to a `<lr-task-list>` built from `run.steps` when nothing is slotted and `run.steps` is non-empty. |
| `tools` | Tool-call content (e.g. `<lr-tool-call-chip>`/`<lr-tool-result-view>` rows). No default content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | Wrapper around the `actions` slot and the built-in Cancel/Retry buttons. |
| `base` | The root container. |
| `body` | Wrapper around the four composition slots. |
| `cancel-button` | The built-in Cancel button. Only rendered while cancelable (see the class doc). |
| `current-step` | Wrapper around the current-step icon and label. Only rendered while a step has `status.kind === 'running'`. |
| `current-step-icon` | The spinning current-step icon. |
| `current-step-label` | The current step's `label` text. |
| `elapsed` | The composed `<lr-generation-status>`, only rendered while the run is actively ticking (see the class doc). |
| `elapsed-static` | The static formatted duration for a terminal run with both `startedAt` and `endedAt`. |
| `empty` | The `<lr-empty>` shown when `run` is `null`. |
| `header` | The header row wrapping status, elapsed time, current step, summary, and actions. |
| `metric` | One arbitrary metric in the built-in summary. |
| `metric-label` | The metric's label. |
| `metric-value` | The metric's value. |
| `model` | `run.model`, when set. |
| `output` | The `output` slot. |
| `reasoning` | The `reasoning` slot. |
| `retry-button` | The built-in Retry button. Only rendered while retryable. |
| `status` | Wrapper around the status badge and optional status message. |
| `status-badge` | The resolved `<lr-badge>` lifecycle-status pill. |
| `status-message` | `run.status.message`, when set. |
| `summary` | Wrapper around the model text and the composed `<lr-usage-badge>`. Only rendered while `run.model` or a valid `run.costEstimate` is present. |
| `tasks` | The `tasks` slot. |
| `tools` | The `tools` slot. |
| `usage` | The composed `<lr-usage-badge>`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-agent-run-spin` (default: `1s linear`) ŌĆö Current-step icon spin animation duration and timing.
- `--lr-agent-run-compact-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö `[part="base"]` padding while `compact`.
- `--lr-agent-run-compact-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö Gap between `[part="base"]`'s header and body while `compact`.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled, padded box. `'plain'` removes the border, background, padding and corner
radius, so a run nested inside a host frame that already draws a border doesn't double it.
`plain` wins over `compact` when both are set (nothing left to tighten). The built-in
Cancel/Retry buttons draw their own border/background and stay visibly interactive either
way.

Type: `AgentRunAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter root padding and header/body gap for dense contexts (a run rendered as a row in a
list, a side panel) -- same convention as `lr-empty`'s `compact`. Defaults to `false`, i.e.
the full card padding. Purely a density knob: the border and background stay, so use
`appearance="plain"` instead to drop the chrome entirely.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: show-cancel
attributes.description:
Whether the built-in Cancel button can render at all -- still gated by the run's own status
being cancelable (`running`/`collecting`/`waiting-input`/`waiting-approval`). Set `false` for a read-only
viewer.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: show-retry
attributes.description:
Whether the built-in Retry button can render at all -- still gated by the run's own status
being retryable (`error`/`cancelled`).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

elements.name: lr-agent-trace
elements.description:
`<lr-agent-trace>` ŌĆö a provider-neutral agent/LLM trace view: a span-kind filter row, a
handoff quick-jump list, and the full trace hierarchy, all driven by one shared `LyraSpan[]`
array (the same shape `<lr-trace-tree>` and `<lr-span-waterfall>` already consume).

The actual trace rendering -- hierarchy, expand/collapse, roving-tabindex keyboard navigation,
duration bars, the empty state -- is entirely `<lr-trace-tree>`'s own: this component only ever
passes it a (possibly filtered) `spans` array plus a handful of pass-through properties, never
building its own row markup. `<lr-trace-tree>` was chosen over `<lr-span-waterfall>` because a
trace is fundamentally the hierarchy relationship between spans (which agent called which tool,
which handed off to which sub-agent) -- exactly what `parentId`-derived `aria-level` nesting
conveys and a flat, `startMs`-ordered timeline does not.

This component's own contribution is the multi-domain filter layer on top of that: a filter
row composing `<lr-graph-legend>` -- the same abstract type/visibility-toggle legend pattern
that component already establishes for `<lr-graph>`'s node types, reused here (it neither
reads nor writes an actual graph) for `LyraSpan.kind` visibility instead -- and a handoff
quick-jump list composing `<lr-handoff-divider>` for each visible `'agent'`-kind span (an
agent invocation is, in trace terms, a handoff of control to that agent). Hidden kinds are
simply excluded from the array handed to `<lr-trace-tree>`; a span whose parent got filtered
out is promoted to a root by `<lr-trace-tree>`'s own existing orphan handling, never dropped.

Selection is controlled end-to-end for deep-linking: `activeSpanId` flows down into
`<lr-trace-tree>` verbatim, and both activation paths -- a row click inside the tree, or a
handoff quick-jump entry -- update it and fire the identical `lr-span-select` `{ id }` shape, so
a host can encode the current span id in a URL and feed it straight back in.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `filter` | The composed `<lr-graph-legend>` filter row, only rendered while `spans` has at least one span. |
| `handoff` | One handoff quick-jump entry (a `<button>` wrapping an `<lr-handoff-divider>`); carries `data-active`. |
| `handoffs` | The handoff quick-jump list wrapper, only rendered while at least one visible span has `kind: 'agent'`. |
| `tree` | The composed `<lr-trace-tree>`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-agent-trace-handoff-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the active (`activeSpanId`) handoff quick-jump entry. Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()`, so the active entry could otherwise only be restyled by hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token.

attributes.name: active-span-id
attributes.description:
Controlled selection, forwarded verbatim into the composed `<lr-trace-tree>`. Updated
locally (and re-emitted as `lr-span-select`) whenever a span is activated from either the
tree or the handoff quick-jump list, so a host can also treat it as a two-way binding.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: hide-bars
attributes.description:
Forwarded verbatim to the composed `<lr-trace-tree>`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name forwarded to the composed `<lr-trace-tree>`. See its own `label` property.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: show-cost
attributes.description:
Forwarded verbatim to the composed `<lr-trace-tree>`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: show-tokens
attributes.description:
Forwarded verbatim to the composed `<lr-trace-tree>`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-agent-workspace
elements.description:
`<lr-agent-workspace>` ŌĆö a responsive, controlled shell for an AI conversation and its
supporting agent state. It renders the transcript and composer in the main pane, and composes
existing run, tool, retrieval, grounding, and context primitives in an optional details pane.

The component performs no network requests, model calls, retrieval, or persistence. Assign new
data to the public properties as the host application receives updates. The `messages` fallback
renders ordered `message.parts` through `<lr-message-parts>` when supplied, otherwise sanitized
Markdown from the legacy `message.text`; applications can replace the entire region with the
`messages` slot. The `details` slot similarly replaces the built-in details pane while keeping
the responsive shell.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `composer` | Replaces the built-in `<lr-chat-composer>`. |
| `details` | Replaces the built-in run/tool/retrieval/grounding/context details pane. |
| `header-actions` | Header actions such as model selection, settings, or export controls. |
| `messages` | Replaces the data-driven transcript message list. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root workspace wrapper. |
| `body` | The main conversation/details layout. |
| `composer` | The composer region. |
| `composer-input` | The built-in `<lr-chat-composer>`. |
| `conversation` | The main transcript pane. |
| `details` | The responsive details pane. |
| `details-content` | The built-in details content wrapper. |
| `header` | The workspace heading and header-actions slot. |
| `header-actions` | The header-actions slot wrapper. |
| `heading` | The visible workspace heading. |
| `messages` | The transcript message wrapper. |
| `messages-empty` | The empty transcript state. |
| `section` | A built-in run, tools, retrieval, grounding, or context section. |
| `section-heading` | A built-in details section heading. |
| `viewport` | The composed `<lr-chat-viewport>`. |

elements.name: lr-animated-image
elements.description: `` -- displays an animated GIF/APNG/WebP with a play/pause control, defaulting to a frozen first frame both at rest and automatically under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (unless the page author explicitly opts back in via `respect-reduced-motion="false"`), so motion is never forced on a user who asked for less of it. **Freeze-frame mechanism.** The live ``'s `load` event handler synchronously draws the just-loaded image to `[part="canvas"]` (a DPR-aware `drawImage()`, the same pattern `` uses for its own canvas sizing) before any animation frames have had a chance to advance. That captured frame is what pausing always reverts to -- it is not re-captured on every pause, only once per successful `src` load. Both `[part="image"]` and `[part="canvas"]` stay mounted at all times (never `display: none`/removed) so the browser's native decode loop keeps running even while visually covered by the frozen canvas; only opacity and `aria-hidden` swap between them, driven by the effective `playing` state. **`play` vs. `playing`.** `play` is the caller's intent (settable, reflects nothing on its own). `playing` is the read-only, reflected effect after reduced-motion arbitration: `play && !(respectReducedMotion && )`. A page can set `.play = true` while reduced motion still keeps the visual frozen -- `lr-play`/`lr-pause` only fire on a real transition of the resolved `playing` value, never on a `play` assignment that reduced motion blocks from taking visible effect. **Safety.** `src` is re-validated through `safeMediaSrc()` (the same allowlist `` uses) before it is ever assigned to the real ``. An empty `src` renders no `src` attribute and is not an error; a non-empty `src` that fails the check is treated exactly like a native image decode failure -- `lr-error` fires and no request is ever attempted. **Decorative-only is not supported.** An explicit `alt=""` still falls back to the localized `animatedImageDefaultAlt` string rather than staying empty/presentational -- the same deliberate tradeoff ``'s `imgAlt` getter already makes for its own `alt`/`filename` fallback chain. Deliberately no label/hint/error chrome -- this is not a form-associated control (nothing resembling a value the user submits). --- **Slots** | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | `pause-icon` | Custom icon shown on `[part="play-button"]` while playing. Defaults to a pause glyph. | | `play-icon` | Custom icon shown on `[part="play-button"]` while frozen/paused. Defaults to a play glyph. | --- **CSS Shadow Parts** | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | `base` | Root wrapper; positioning context for `control-box`. | | `canvas` | The frozen-frame ``, shown in place of `image` while not playing. | | `control-box` | The container that surrounds and backgrounds the play/pause button. Only rendered once loaded and error-free. | | `image` | The live ``. | | `play-button` | The `

attributes.name: alt
attributes.description:
A description of the image used by assistive devices. Falls back to
the localized `animatedImageDefaultAlt` when empty -- see the class doc.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible-name override for `[part="play-button"]`. Maps to the
host's `aria-label` attribute and, once set, wins verbatim over the
computed per-state Play/Pause label in both play and pause states --
it does not itself vary by state. Never touches `[part="image"]`'s
`alt` / `[part="canvas"]`'s `aria-label`, which stay independently
sourced from `alt`. A consumer wanting state-sensitive custom wording
without losing the Play/Pause distinction should override the
`playWithContext`/`pauseWithContext`/`animatedImageDefaultAlt` strings
instead.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: play
attributes.description:
Requests animated playback. Distinct from the read-only `playing`
effect -- see the class doc's "`play` vs. `playing`" section.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: respect-reduced-motion
attributes.description:
When `true` (default) and the platform reports
`prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, playback stays frozen and
`[part="play-button"]` is disabled regardless of `play`. Set to `false`
to let `play` take effect even under a reduced-motion preference -- a
deliberate, page-author-level override.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
The path to the image to load. Always re-validated against a
safe-scheme allowlist before use -- see the class doc.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-animation
elements.description:
`<lr-animation>` declaratively animates its single slotted child through the
native Web Animations API: a small curated preset catalog (`name`) or fully
custom `keyframes`, explicit WAAPI timing controls, an optional
`playOnVisible` trigger, and a `lr-start`/`lr-finish`/`lr-cancel` event
contract.

`keyframes`, when set, always wins over `name`. The `iterations` default is
`Infinity` (mirrors the upstream Web Awesome/Shoelace animation contract
verbatim) -- a named preset plays forever unless the consumer sets
`iterations="1"`.

`direction` is the Web Animations API's `PlaybackDirection`
(`'normal' | 'reverse' | 'alternate' | 'alternate-reverse'`) and is entirely
unrelated to text direction. Only the `slide-in-start`/`slide-in-end`/
`slide-out-start`/`slide-out-end` presets read the inherited text direction
(`effectiveDirection`) to resolve which physical edge "start"/"end" means,
and they do so fresh every time the animation is (re)built -- an animation
already mid-flight is not retroactively re-mirrored if an ancestor `dir`
flips while it plays; the next rebuild picks up the change.

`respectReducedMotion` (default `true`) caps playback to one iteration and
calls `finish()` immediately instead of playing, whenever the OS/browser
reports `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` -- the target snaps straight to
its resolved end state, and `lr-start`/`lr-finish` still fire in order
so a consumer sequencing further UI off those events keeps working even
though nothing visibly interpolated. Set `respectReducedMotion="false"`
only for genuine user-triggered feedback (e.g. a drag-confirm snap-back)
where a silent jump would be more confusing than a fast real animation --
ambient/decorative animation should always leave this at its default.

`timingPreset` (default `'custom'`) optionally derives `duration`/`easing`
from the shared `--lr-transition-fast`/`-base`/`-ambient` tokens instead
of the raw `duration`/`easing` property values, so an app's global motion
retiming reaches this component's animations too.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The element to animate. A second slotted element is accepted without error but ignored. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-animation-slide-distance` (default: `100%`) ŌĆö Travel distance for the slide-in/slide-out/slide-in-up/slide-in-down presets.
- `--lr-animation-zoom-scale` (default: `0.5`) ŌĆö Starting/ending scale factor for the zoom-in/zoom-out presets.
- `--lr-animation-bounce-height` (default: `25%`) ŌĆö Peak lift height of the bounce preset.
- `--lr-animation-shake-distance` (default: `4%`) ŌĆö Horizontal travel of the shake preset.

attributes.name: delay
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description:
Type: `PlaybackDirection`  
Default: `'normal'`

attributes.name: duration
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `1000`

attributes.name: easing
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `'linear'`

attributes.name: end-delay
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: fill
attributes.description:
Type: `FillMode`  
Default: `'auto'`

attributes.name: iteration-start
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: iterations
attributes.description: Default: `Infinity`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Type: `LyraAnimationPreset`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: play
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: play-on-visible
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: play-on-visible-repeat
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: playback-rate
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `1`

attributes.name: respect-reduced-motion
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: root-margin
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `'0px'`

attributes.name: timing-preset
attributes.description:
Type: `LyraAnimationTimingPreset`  
Default: `'custom'`

elements.name: lr-app-rail
elements.description:
`<lr-app-rail>` ŌĆö a responsive navigation rail that adapts across three
presentations as the *viewport* narrows (not this element's own inline
size ŌĆö see the `mode` accessor doc for why): `'full'` (nav items show
icon + label, inline), `'icon-only'` (a narrower inline rail, icons only),
and `'mobile'` (hidden behind a toggle button; opening it shows a
focus-trapped floating overlay over the page).

Breakpoints are viewport-width `matchMedia()` queries against
`icon-only-breakpoint`/`mobile-breakpoint`, not a `ResizeObserver` on this
element ŌĆö a nav rail's presentation should track the actual device/window
width the way a native OS shell's navigation does, not however much
horizontal space a particular layout happens to give it.

The `'mobile'` state participates in the library's shared overlay stack,
which supplies focus trapping, Escape/backdrop dismissal, inerting, and
focus restoration without nesting a `<lr-dialog>` in this component's
shadow template. `[part="base"]` (the inline
`'full'`/`'icon-only'` presentation) and `[part="panel"]` (the mobile
overlay) are the *same* element promoted in place across modes (mirrors
`<lr-widget>`'s fullscreen mode) ŌĆö never both at once, and never two
separate copies of the slotted content, which slot projection can't
produce anyway (a light-DOM node is only ever assigned to one `<slot>`).
It's a plain `<div>` with an explicit `role="navigation"` rather than a
literal `<nav>` tag: a `<nav>`'s implicit role can't be overridden to
`role="dialog"` while the overlay is modal without an `aria-allowed-role`
violation (verified against axe), whereas an explicit `role="navigation"`
on a generic element can be swapped for `role="dialog"` freely.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Nav items. Use `<lr-app-rail-item>` for the explicit icon/label contract that automatically hides labels in `'icon-only'` mode. Generic links and buttons remain supported, but their compact presentation is the consumer's responsibility. While the mobile overlay is open, clicking anywhere inside this slot closes it. |
| `footer` | A trailing user/settings trigger, shown below the nav items. |
| `header` | Logo/brand content, shown above the nav items in every mode. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The mobile overlay's scrim. Only rendered while open. |
| `base` | The rail root while inline (`'full'`/`'icon-only'` modes). |
| `footer` | The wrapper around the `footer` slot. |
| `header` | The wrapper around the `header` slot. |
| `nav` | The wrapper around the default (nav items) slot. |
| `panel` | The mobile overlay's floating panel ŌĆö see the class doc for why it's the same element as `base`, never both at once. |
| `resizer` | The `resizable` opt-in's drag handle -- its interactive hit target, sized to the shared minimum tappable size (`--lr-icon-button-size`), independent of the slimmer visible line rendered by its `resizer-track` child. Only rendered while `resizable` and `mode` is `'full'`. |
| `resizer-track` | The resizer's slim visible drag line, centered inside `[part="resizer"]`'s larger hit target (mirrors `<lr-swatch-picker>`'s `[part="swatch"]`/`[part="swatch-fill"]` split). Colors on hover/focus the same way the whole handle previously did. |
| `toggle` | The mobile hamburger/close toggle button. Hidden via CSS outside `'mobile'` mode, or entirely via `hideToggle`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-app-rail-width` (default: `var(--lr-size-15rem)`) ŌĆö The inline rail's width in `'full'` mode. Overridden by an inline width while a `resizable` rail has an explicit `railWidthPx`.
- `--lr-app-rail-icon-width` (default: `var(--lr-size-4rem)`) ŌĆö The inline rail's width in `'icon-only'` mode, and the maximum width of each slotted `<lr-app-rail-item>` in that mode.
- `--lr-app-rail-mobile-width` (default: `var(--lr-size-18rem)`) ŌĆö The mobile overlay panel's width, capped at `85vw`.
- `--lr-app-rail-overlay-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-overlay)`) ŌĆö The mobile overlay scrim's background.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name overriding `label` (and its localized default) for the nav landmark / dialog
role, mirroring `<lr-date-input>`'s `accessibleLabel` pattern. Reads the host's own
`aria-label` attribute -- unset (the default, `null`) reproduces today's exact
`label`/localized-default output.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: dragging
attributes.description:
`true` for the duration of an active pointer-driven resize drag (not a keyboard step) --
reflected so a consumer (or this component's own styles) can suppress `[part='base']`'s
`transition: inline-size` during the drag, which otherwise visibly "chases" the pointer
instead of tracking it 1:1. Read-only in practice (this component owns the transitions), but
a plain reactive property like every other reflected boolean here.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: hide-toggle
attributes.description:
Suppresses the built-in mobile `[part='toggle']` hamburger/close button entirely -- for a
consumer that already owns an external mobile-menu toggle wired to this rail's own `open`
property. `false` (the default) reproduces today's exact output; note `open` still has no
built-in external trigger of its own once this is set, since `lr-toggle` only fires from the
toggle button being removed.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: icon-only-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Below this viewport width, the rail switches from `'full'` to
`'icon-only'`. Any valid CSS length, used directly in a `max-width`
media query.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'960px'`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the rail's navigation landmark, and for its dialog
role while the mobile overlay is open. A host-level `aria-label`
attribute takes precedence over this when both are set -- see
`accessibleLabel`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Navigation'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-rail-width-px
attributes.description:
Maximum `railWidthPx` a drag/keyboard resize can reach.

Type: `number`  
Default: `440`

attributes.name: min-rail-width-px
attributes.description:
Minimum `railWidthPx` a drag/keyboard resize can reach.

Type: `number`  
Default: `190`

attributes.name: mobile-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Below this viewport width, the rail switches from `'icon-only'` to
`'mobile'`. Should be smaller than `iconOnlyBreakpoint` to produce all
three states as the viewport narrows.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'600px'`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
The rail's current effective presentation. Always one of the three real
modes ŌĆö never `'auto'` ŌĆö reflecting either the live breakpoint match or,
once forced, whatever was last assigned.

Assigning `'full'`/`'icon-only'`/`'mobile'` forces that mode and stops
this element from responding to `icon-only-breakpoint`/
`mobile-breakpoint` matches. Assigning the sentinel `'auto'` releases
the force and immediately re-syncs to the current viewport width,
resuming automatic tracking. `'auto'` is a write-only instruction, not a
value this getter ever returns ŌĆö there is no way to observe "currently
forced" from the property itself.

Type: `AppRailMode`

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Whether the mobile floating overlay is shown. Only meaningful while
`mode` is `'mobile'` ŌĆö the value is preserved (not reset) while another
mode is active, but no overlay chrome renders until `mode` is
`'mobile'` again. Set this directly, or use the built-in toggle button
ŌĆö there is no separate `show()`/`hide()` pair.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: persist
attributes.description:
Whitespace-separated persistence allowlist. `open width` is the backward-compatible default;
use `width preferred-mode` to retain layout preference without restoring the transient mobile
overlay. Valid tokens are `open`, `width`, and `preferred-mode`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'open width'`

attributes.name: preferred-mode
attributes.description:
Manually prefers `'full'` or `'icon-only'` for the non-mobile breakpoint axis, while the
`mobile-breakpoint` continues to be tracked automatically regardless ŌĆö e.g. a user's manual
collapse toggle that should still yield to a genuinely too-narrow-for-any-inline-rail
viewport. Only consulted while `mode` isn't force-pinned via its own accessor (see the class
doc) ŌĆö that continues to take full priority, unchanged. Unset (the default, `null`)
reproduces today's exact breakpoint-only behavior.

Type: `AppRailPreferredMode | null | undefined`

attributes.name: rail-width-px
attributes.description:
The rail's current width in px while `resizable` ŌĆö settable/gettable. Unset defers to the
`--lr-app-rail-width` CSS token's own resolved width.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: resizable
attributes.description:
Opts a continuously draggable width in for the `'full'` state ŌĆö exposes a `[part="resizer"]`
handle (pointer-drag and `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` keyboard stepping, RTL-aware) clamped to
`[minRailWidthPx, maxRailWidthPx]`. Set `storageKey` to persist the fields selected by
`persist`; otherwise listen for `lr-rail-resize` and persist `widthPx` yourself. `false` (the
default) renders no resizer and leaves the fixed-width `--lr-app-rail-width` CSS token exactly
as before this property existed.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: storage-key
attributes.description:
When set, persists the fields selected by `persist` to `localStorage` under
`lr-app-rail:${storageKey}`, restoring them on the next mount ŌĆö mirrors `lr-split`'s
`storage-key`. Effective `mode` is breakpoint-derived and is never persisted. Unset (the
default) means no persistence, exactly as before.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-app-rail-item
elements.description:
`<lr-app-rail-item>` ŌĆö an explicit icon/label navigation item for
`<lr-app-rail>`. The rail sets its `icon-only` attribute as the viewport
changes, keeping the label available to assistive technology while removing
it from the visual layout.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The visible navigation label. |
| `icon` | The leading icon. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The link or button receiving focus and activation. |
| `icon` | The icon wrapper. |
| `label` | The label wrapper; visually clipped in icon-only mode. |
| `tooltip` | The hover/focus label flyout, only rendered while `tooltip` is set, the item is `icon-only`, and it is hovered or focused. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-app-rail-item-current-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the `active`/`aria-current="page"` item. Scoped to `[aria-current='page']` only and declared as an inline `var()` fallback (never on `:host`), so setting it on the element or an ancestor recolors only the current item without hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token.
- `--lr-app-rail-item-current-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Text/icon color of the `active`/`aria-current="page"` item.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description:
Marks this as the destination for the current page/view. Reflects
`aria-current="page"` on `[part="base"]` and drives the active visual
treatment -- the rail has no built-in routing, so the consumer sets
this per item (e.g. by comparing `href` against the current location).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Prevents activation while retaining the item in the rail.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
Optional destination. Without `href`, the item renders as a button.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: target
attributes.description:
Optional link target.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: tooltip
attributes.description:
Opt-in hover/focus flyout showing this item's label text while `icon-only` (set externally by
the parent `<lr-app-rail>` as the viewport narrows) hides it from view -- an explicit,
documented property instead of an unverified cross-browser `::part()` + `::after` + `attr()`
composition. No effect outside icon-only mode, since the label is already visible there.
`false` (the default) reproduces today's exact output.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-approval-queue
elements.description:
`<lr-approval-queue>` ŌĆö a controlled queue of tool calls that need human approval, with a
keyboard-accessible request list and a single reused `<lr-tool-approval-dialog>`. It never
executes tools, applies permissions, or persists decisions; the host owns those operations.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root queue wrapper. |
| `count` | The pending-count text. |
| `empty` | The empty state. |
| `heading` | The visible queue heading. |
| `heading-row` | The heading and pending-count row. |
| `list` | The request list. |
| `request` | One selectable request row. |
| `request-id` | The stable request id. |
| `request-info` | Request name and id wrapper. |
| `status` | The request status badge. |
| `tool-name` | The proposed tool name. |

attributes.name: editable
attributes.description:
Allows argument editing in the nested approval dialog.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name and visible heading.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Whether the decision dialog is open.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: selected-id
attributes.description:
The request currently shown in the dialog.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-archive-viewer
elements.description:
Lists names and uncompressed sizes in a ZIP archive without rendering entry contents. File sizes
are measured through JSZip's public async API, so opening an archive decompresses each file once.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root container. |
| `body` | The archive listing body. |
| `entry` | An archive entry row. |
| `entry-icon` | The decorative folder or file icon. |
| `entry-name` | The entry path. |
| `entry-name-dir` | The entry path of a directory row (also carries `entry-name`). |
| `entry-size` | The human-readable file size. |
| `error` | The error region. |
| `spinner` | The loading region. |

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Display name used as the archive listing's accessible label.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
URL to fetch and parse as a ZIP archive.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-artifact-panel
elements.description:
`<lr-artifact-panel>` ŌĆö shell around one agent-generated artifact: a
title/kind header, a preview<->code toggle, version navigation with
restore, a streaming indicator, and built-in copy/download actions.
Renders none of the artifact itself ŌĆö content is slotted.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Preview-view content. |
| `actions` | Extra header controls, rendered between the version navigation and the built-in copy/download buttons. |
| `code` | Code-view content (typically a `<lr-code-block>`). The preview/code toggle only renders once this slot has assigned content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | The `actions` slot wrapper. |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `body` | The content body wrapper. |
| `copy-button` | The copy button, rendered only while `copyText` is non-empty. |
| `download-button` | The download button, rendered only while `downloadSrc` is non-empty. |
| `header` | The header row. |
| `kind` | The kind badge. |
| `label` | The artifact title. |
| `restore-button` | The restore-this-version button, rendered only while the active version isn't the latest one. |
| `streaming-indicator` | The streaming state indicator, rendered only while `streaming`. |
| `version-nav` | The version navigation group (rendered only once `versions` is non-empty). |
| `version-next` | The next-version button. |
| `version-next-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `version-next`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `version-position` | The "Version N of M" text. |
| `version-previous` | The previous-version button. |
| `version-previous-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `version-previous`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `view-button` | One toggle button; carries `data-view="preview"` or `data-view="code"`. |
| `view-toggle` | The preview/code toggle group (rendered only once the `code` slot has content). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-artifact-panel-view-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the pressed (active) preview/code toggle button.
- `--lr-artifact-panel-view-active-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Text color of the pressed (active) preview/code toggle button. Restyling the pressed state otherwise requires overriding the library-wide brand tokens, since `::part(view-button)[aria-pressed]` is invalid CSS.

attributes.name: active-version-id
attributes.description:
The currently viewed version's id, or `null` to mean "the latest version".

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: copy-text
attributes.description:
The text copied to the clipboard by the copy button. Empty hides the button.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: download-name
attributes.description:
The suggested filename reported in the `lr-download` event detail.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: download-src
attributes.description:
The download URL, sanitized through `safeMediaSrc()` before use. Empty hides the button.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: kind
attributes.description:
A short kind label (e.g. `document`, `code`), shown as a badge next to `label`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The artifact's title, shown in the header.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: streaming
attributes.description:
Whether the artifact is still being generated. Sets `aria-busy` on the body and shows a
text indicator instead of an animated one, so it stays legible under reduced motion.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: view
attributes.description:
Which slot is currently visible.

Type: `ArtifactPanelView`  
Default: `'preview'`

elements.name: lr-attachment-chip
elements.description:
`<lr-attachment-chip>` ŌĆö a compact chip representing one file queued for
(or already part of) a chat message: a composer's pre-send attachment
tray, or a sent message's `attachments` slot (see `<lr-chat-message>`).

Two independent ways to populate it, matching the two points in a message's
lifecycle this is used at:
 - Set `file` to a real `File` (fresh from a picker/drop) ŌĆö `name`, `size`,
   `mime-type` and the image thumbnail are all auto-derived from it.
 - Set the plain `name`/`size`/`mime-type`/`thumbnail-src` props instead,
   for reconstructing a chip from server-persisted attachment metadata
   (e.g. after a page reload, when no real `File` object exists any more).

`file` always wins when both are present ŌĆö see each accessor's own doc.
The image thumbnail for a real `File` is a cached `URL.createObjectURL()`
blob URL, created in the update lifecycle immediately before the thumbnail
renders and revoked automatically once `file`
changes away from the object it was created for, and on disconnect ŌĆö this
component never leaks a blob URL.

Identifying *which* attachment a `lr-remove`/`lr-retry` event is about:
this deliberately reuses the platform's own `id`/`id` attribute (every
element already has one; there's no need to shadow it with a second,
differently-named Lit property, unlike e.g. `<lr-tool-call-chip>`'s
`call-id` which identifies a *call*, a concept distinct from the chip
element itself). Set `id="..."` when you have a stable server-side
attachment id. When `id` is left unset and `file` is set, a stable id is
derived from `` `${file.name}:${file.size}:${file.lastModified}` `` ŌĆö stable
across re-renders of the *same* `File` object without requiring the
consumer to invent one. When neither is available, a generated internal id
is used as a last resort so the event always has *some* id.

i18n/locale: complete contextual messages (including filename/percentage
placement and punctuation) route through the shared localization registry.
`removeLabel`/`retryLabel`/`uploadingLabel`/`uploadFailedLabel` remain as
simple per-instance copy overrides; use the component's `.strings` map or a
registered locale when a translation needs to reorder the interpolated
values. These are plain properties, not slots ŌĆö this component still
exposes no slots.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The chip's root container. |
| `meta` | Wrapper around `name` and `size`. |
| `name` | The filename (ellipsis-truncated via CSS; the untruncated name is always available via the native `title` tooltip). |
| `preview-button` | The preview affordance, rendered when a file or `preview-src` is available. |
| `progress` | The numeric upload progress bar (`role="progressbar"`), shown only while `status="uploading"` and `progress` is a meaningful (>0) number. |
| `progress-fill` | The filled portion of `progress`. |
| `remove-button` | The remove (├Ś) affordance, only rendered while `removable`. |
| `retry-button` | The retry affordance, only rendered while `status="error"`. |
| `size` | The human-readable formatted file size. Hidden when no size is known. |
| `spinner` | The indeterminate upload spinner, shown instead of `progress` while `status="uploading"` and `progress` is unset/0. |
| `status-text` | The visible text twin of the status accent color ŌĆö carries the state in text, not just color. Empty/hidden for `pending`/`done`. Gets `role="alert"` for `status="error"` only, so a screen-reader user not already focused on the chip still hears an upload failure; the ticking `'uploading'` readout deliberately stays out of the accessibility tree the same way `<lr-generation-status>`'s per-second elapsed/token readout does ŌĆö a live region re-announcing every progress tick would be noise, not information, while a one-shot failure is exactly the kind of infrequent, actionable transition a live region exists for. |
| `thumbnail` | The leading image thumbnail / generic file glyph. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-attachment-chip-spinner-duration` (default: `0.8s`) ŌĆö Duration of one indeterminate upload-spinner rotation. The ambient loop stops under reduced motion.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-accent` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Accent color used for the status text, spinner, and progress fill. Auto-swapped per `status` (`uploading`/`error`/`done`); the default is the neutral `pending` tone.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Chip background, auto-swapped per `status` to that status's `-quiet` tint.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Chip border color. Every non-`pending` `status` sets it to `transparent`.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-compact-thumbnail-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-75rem)`) ŌĆö Thumbnail and retry/preview/remove-button minimum size while `compact`, rethemeable independently of `--lr-icon-button-size`.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-compact-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-xs)`) ŌĆö Font size of `[part="base"]` while `compact`.
- `--lr-attachment-chip-compact-gap` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-25rem)`) ŌĆö Gap between the chip's parts while `compact`.

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Renders a smaller, borderless pill presentation instead of the default bordered/chrome-heavy
chip -- for a consumer that wants an icon-only-adjacent, compact attachment affordance (e.g.
a composer's pending-attachment tray) without hand-tuning several `::part()` custom
properties individually. `false` (the default) is visually identical to today.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mime-type
attributes.description:
MIME type, used only while `file` is unset.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Filename, used only while `file` is unset.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: preview-src
attributes.description:
URL used to preview or download the attachment when `file` is unset.
A real `File` takes precedence and is previewed through a temporary blob URL.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: previewable
attributes.description:
Shows the preview action when a `file` or `preview-src` is available.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: progress
attributes.description:
Upload completion, 0-100. Only meaningful while `status="uploading"`;
a value of 0 (the default), `NaN`, or negative falls back to the indeterminate spinner
(see `hasNumericProgress`); an oversized value clamps to 100 (see `clampedProgress`).

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: removable
attributes.description:
Shows the remove (├Ś) button.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: remove-label
attributes.description:
Verb used in the remove button's accessible name. For complete control over translated
word order and punctuation, override the `removeWithContext` message instead.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Remove'`

attributes.name: retry-label
attributes.description:
Verb used in the retry button's accessible name. For complete control over translated word
order and punctuation, override the `attachmentRetryWithContext` message instead.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Retry'`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
File size in bytes, used only while `file` is unset. NaN/negative normalize to `0` (renders
as "unknown size", same as an unset size) via `effectiveSize` -- see that getter.

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: status
attributes.description:
Lifecycle state ŌĆö drives the accent tint and which of `progress`/`spinner`/`retry-button` renders.

Type: `AttachmentChipStatus`  
Default: `'pending'`

attributes.name: thumbnail-only
attributes.description:
When both this and `compact` are set, hides `[part=meta]` (the filename/size text) entirely
for an image-mime attachment, leaving only the thumbnail -- for a consumer wanting a
thumbnail-only density purely through props, with no consumer-side CSS. Has no effect for a
non-image chip (there is no thumbnail to fall back to showing on its own) or when `compact` is
unset. `false` (the default) reproduces today's exact output.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: thumbnail-src
attributes.description:
Thumbnail image URL, used only while `file` is unset (a real `File`'s
thumbnail always comes from a generated object URL instead ŌĆö see the
class doc). Unlike the other independent props this one has no
`file`-derived equivalent to defer to for a non-image file; it's simply
rendered whenever present.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: untitled-label
attributes.description:
Override for the empty-name fallback shown (and used as the `title` tooltip) when neither
`file` nor `name` supply a filename -- for i18n/locale. Defaults to `'Untitled file'`,
reproducing today's exact hardcoded text byte-for-byte.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Untitled file'`

attributes.name: upload-failed-label
attributes.description:
Visible status text shown for `status="error"`. Override for
i18n/locale. Defaults to `'Upload failed'`, reproducing today's exact
text byte-for-byte.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Upload failed'`

attributes.name: uploading-label
attributes.description:
Verb used in uploading messages. For complete control over translated word order and
punctuation, override the `attachmentUploadingWithContext`,
`attachmentUploadingProgress`, and `attachmentUploadingIndeterminate` messages instead.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Uploading'`

elements.name: lr-attachment-trigger
elements.description:
`<lr-attachment-trigger>` ŌĆö a compact attach affordance designed for a
chat composer's leading slot (see `<lr-chat-composer>`'s own `leading`
slot, which this drops straight into, though this component has no code
dependency on it). Its shape adapts to how many attachment `capabilities`
are configured:
 - Exactly one capability: a single plain icon button ([part='trigger']).
   Activating it performs that capability's action directly.
 - More than one: a small anchored menu ([part='menu'], composed from
   `<lr-menu>`/`<lr-menu-item>`) listing each capability as a row.

Two of the four capabilities (`files`, `image`) are file-picker-backed:
activating them opens a hidden native `<input type="file">` via a
synthetic `.click()`, and the resulting selection is re-emitted as
`lr-pick`. `accept` is shared across both ŌĆö `image` defaults it to
`'image/*'` unless the `accept` prop overrides it, `files` always uses
`accept` as-is (empty means "any file type", matching a bare native
`<input type="file">` with no `accept` attribute).

**`camera`/`audio` are scope-limited by design.** This component does not
implement any camera or microphone capture UI itself ŌĆö no `getUserMedia`,
no `<input capture>` ŌĆö because that's entirely a host/browser concern with
no single right answer (a desktop web app, a mobile PWA, and a native
wrapper all want different things here). Activating `camera` fires
`lr-camera-request`; activating `audio` fires `lr-audio-request`. The
host owns everything from that point on ŌĆö typically opening
`<lr-push-to-talk>` in a `<lr-overlay>`/popover for `audio`, then
handing the resulting blob to something like `<lr-attachment-chip>`.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `expand-icon` | The disclosure chevron inside the multi-capability trigger button. Only rendered when `capabilities.length > 1`. |
| `hidden-input` | The internal native `<input type="file">` that actually opens the OS file picker. Hidden (`display: none`) by default; exposed as a part only so a consumer can override that with `::part(hidden-input)` in the unlikely case their integration needs to. `triggerLabel` lets a host override the single-capability trigger button's `aria-label` (i18n) ŌĆö see that property's own doc for exactly what it does and doesn't affect. |
| `menu` | The `<lr-menu>` wrapper. Only rendered when `capabilities.length > 1`. |
| `menu-trigger` | The multi-capability button slotted into `<lr-menu>`'s own `trigger` slot. Only rendered when `capabilities.length > 1`. |
| `trigger` | The single-capability icon button. Only rendered when `capabilities.length === 1`. |

attributes.name: accept
attributes.description:
Native-file-input-style accept string (e.g. `'image/*'` or
`'.pdf,.docx'`), forwarded to the hidden file input for the
`files`/`image` capabilities ŌĆö see the class doc for how each uses it.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: multiple
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the hidden file input's own `multiple` attribute.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: trigger-label
attributes.description:
Overrides the single-capability trigger button's `aria-label`, which
otherwise comes from `this.localize()` (e.g. `'Attach files'`, localizable
via `.strings`/`registerLyraLocale()`). Set this for a one-off override;
leave it unset to keep the localized default. Only affects the
single-capability button ([part='trigger']) ŌĆö the multi-capability
menu's own trigger keeps its own `'Add attachment'` label regardless.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: trigger-title
attributes.description:
Forwards to the internal trigger button(s)' native `title` attribute ŌĆö a sighted mouse
user's hover tooltip, distinct from `triggerLabel`'s accessible-name (`aria-label`) role.
Applies to both the single-capability `[part=trigger]` button and the multi-capability
`[part=menu-trigger]` button. Unset (the default): no `title` attribute, unchanged from
before this property existed.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-audio-visualizer
elements.description:
`<lr-audio-visualizer>` ŌĆö a presentational, canvas-drawn voice-activity visualization (bars or
waveform), the LiveKit-BarVisualizer counterpart for this library. Driven by a `MediaStream`
(lazily wired to a WebAudio `AnalyserNode`), a numeric `level` for hosts that already compute
levels (e.g. `lr-push-to-talk`'s `lr-level`), or `state` alone for an ambient animation when no
real signal exists. A real signal (`stream` or `level`) always drives amplitude regardless of
`prefers-reduced-motion` ŌĆö that is live, user-controlled feedback, not decorative motion; only the
signal-less ambient animation is throttled and simplified under reduced motion.

Animation frames are only scheduled while the drawn output is actually time-varying (live analyser
data, or a non-reduced ambient pulse/sweep). Static output ŌĆö a constant `level`, idle bars, or the
flattened reduced-motion ambient patterns ŌĆö draws once and stops; any change that could alter the
next frame (properties, stream/`AudioContext` state, size, theme, motion preference) re-enters the
loop via `scheduleDraw()`. The loop is also paused while the host is scrolled off-screen (an
`IntersectionObserver`-gated `visible` flag, mirroring `<lr-chart>`'s own `draw()` gating), so a
live-signal visualizer buried behind later transcript messages doesn't keep repainting for nobody.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `canvas` | The drawing surface (`aria-hidden`; the host itself carries `role="img"` and the accessible name). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-audio-visualizer-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Active bar/waveform color.
- `--lr-audio-visualizer-quiet-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Inactive/idle color.
- `--lr-audio-visualizer-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-3rem)`) ŌĆö The host's block size, which the canvas fills at 100%.

attributes.name: bar-count
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `5`

attributes.name: gain
attributes.description:
Amplitude multiplier applied in `draw()`. NaN/non-finite falls back to `1` via `effectiveGain`.

Type: `number`  
Default: `1`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible-name override. Unset (the default) auto-generates "Voice activity: {state}".

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: level
attributes.description:
Externally-computed amplitude, `[0, 1]`, for a host that already derives its own level
(e.g. `lr-push-to-talk`'s `lr-level`). `null` (the default) means "no external signal" --
see `effectiveLevel` for how a non-null value is clamped/NaN-guarded before it feeds `draw()`.

Type: `number | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: state
attributes.description:
Type: `AudioVisualizerState`  
Default: `'idle'`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Type: `AudioVisualizerVariant`  
Default: `'bars'`

elements.name: lr-av-player
elements.description:
`<lr-av-player>` ŌĆö audio/video player built on a native `<audio>`/`<video>` element, plus a cue
transcript synced to `currentTime`, `time-range` anchor/highlight support, an optional
dependency-free waveform (peaks in, no in-component decoding), and playback-rate control. Owns
recorded-media transcript sync; distinct from `<lr-transcript-feed>` (live captions for an
in-progress voice session) and `<lr-playback>` (an index stepper over `[0, length)` for
time-series dashboards ŌĆö no media involved in either).

Adopts `DocumentAnchorTarget` with `anchorKinds: ['time-range']` only. No text selection is bound:
transcript rows render inside `<lr-virtual-list>`'s own nested shadow root, one boundary deeper
than the mixin's default selection lookup resolves.

The transcript virtualizes through `<lr-virtual-list>` the same way `pdf-viewer.class.ts`
virtualizes pages: `items`/`renderItem`/`keyFunction`/`activeId` props, and the active cue's
scroll-into-view comes for free from `activeId` rather than any custom follow logic.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `cue` | One transcript row (`aria-current`, `data-match`, `data-active-match`). |
| `cue-active-match` | Added alongside `cue`/`cue-match` on the row holding the current search match. |
| `cue-current` | Added alongside `cue` on the row the playhead is inside. A second part name rather than an attribute selector, because Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()`. |
| `cue-match` | Added alongside `cue` on every row matching the current search query. |
| `cue-speaker` | A cue's speaker label. |
| `cue-text` | A cue's text. |
| `cue-time` | A cue's timestamp label. |
| `error` | The error region. |
| `media` | The native `<audio>`/`<video>` element. |
| `rate-select` | The playback-rate `<select>`. |
| `timeline` | The waveform canvas or plain seek rail; click-to-seek and arrow-key seeking. |
| `timeline-marker` | One clickable marker per `time-range` highlight (`data-tone`, `data-active`). |
| `toolbar` | The playback-rate control row. |
| `transcript` | The virtualized cue list (`<lr-virtual-list>` itself). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-av-player-transcript-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-16rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the virtualized transcript list.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-active-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Outline color of the `[part="timeline-marker"]` matching `activeHighlightId`.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-brand) 35%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of a `[part="timeline-marker"]` with no (or an unrecognized) `data-tone`.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-success-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-success) 35%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of a `[part="timeline-marker"][data-tone="success"]`.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-warning-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-warning) 35%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of a `[part="timeline-marker"][data-tone="warning"]`.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-danger-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-danger) 35%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of a `[part="timeline-marker"][data-tone="danger"]`.
- `--lr-av-player-marker-neutral-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-text) 25%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of a `[part="timeline-marker"][data-tone="neutral"]`.
- `--lr-av-player-cue-current-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the `[part="cue"]` the playhead is currently inside.
- `--lr-av-player-cue-active-match-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-warning)`) ŌĆö Outline color of the `[part="cue"]` holding the current search match, leaving the other matches' dashed outline on the shared warning token.

attributes.name: kind
attributes.description:
Forces `audio`/`video` rendering, overriding the `mime-type`-based auto-detection.

Type: `AvKind | undefined`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: loop
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: mime-type
attributes.description:
Drives auto-detection: an `audio/*` mime type renders `<audio>`; anything else renders
`<video>`. Ignored once `kind` is set explicitly.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: muted
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Accessible name of `[part="base"]` and of the native `[part="media"]` element (the actual
keyboard tab stop, which would otherwise be nameless); a host `aria-label` wins, then the
localized `avPlayerLabel` fallback.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: playback-rate
attributes.description:
Playback-rate multiplier, reflected to the native media element. Clamped to
`[MIN_PLAYBACK_RATE, MAX_PLAYBACK_RATE]` -- a non-finite or wildly out-of-range assignment
(e.g. a bad computed value) self-heals rather than reaching `HTMLMediaElement.playbackRate`
unsanitized.

Type: `number`

attributes.name: poster
attributes.description:
Poster image for `<video>`; ignored for `<audio>`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: preload
attributes.description:
Type: `'none' | 'metadata' | 'auto'`  
Default: `'metadata'`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
Media URL; validated with `safeMediaSrc` before it ever reaches the `<audio>`/`<video>` `src`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-avatar
elements.description: `` ŌĆö a small, fixed-size identity marker: default-slotted icon/glyph content, an image, or an initials fallback, in that priority order (whichever's set takes over from the next). First-party invention (no Web Awesome equivalent) -- purely presentational, no built-in interactivity; a consumer wraps it in their own `

attributes.name: alt
attributes.description:
Alt text -- required alongside `src` for accessibility, and also used as the accessible
name (via `aria-label`) when showing icon-only slotted content, since a decorative glyph
has no text of its own for a screen reader to read. A host `aria-label` overrides this
value while leaving the visible initials/image unchanged.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: initials
attributes.description:
Fallback text (typically 1-2 characters) shown when no icon/image is set, or the image
fails to load.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description:
`'circle'` (the default) or `'square'`.

Type: `AvatarShape`  
Default: `'circle'`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size. `'lg'` matches `--lr-icon-button-size` (2.5rem); `'md'` (the default) is 2rem.

Type: `AvatarSize`  
Default: `'md'`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
Image URL. Takes priority over `initials` when set and loads successfully (but not over
slotted icon content); falls back to `initials` on a load error.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: tone
attributes.description:
Recolors the initials-fallback background/text, mirroring `lr-chip`'s `tone` vocabulary.
`'neutral'` (the default) reads as a plain, unaccented circle.

Type: `AvatarTone`  
Default: `'neutral'`

elements.name: lr-avatar-group
elements.description:
`<lr-avatar-group>` ŌĆö stacks a set of slotted `<lr-avatar>` children into a single
overlapping row (negative-margin overlap, ring border so each circle reads as distinct) and,
past a configurable `max` count, collapses the excess into a "+N" overflow badge. First-party
invention (no Web Awesome equivalent), composed over `<lr-avatar>` rather than reimplementing
it ŌĆö plain light-DOM slotted content is the group's items, the same shape `<lr-split>`'s
panels / `<lr-source-list>`'s cards / `<lr-chip-group>`'s chips already use, not a
`.items` array prop.

**`size`/`shape`/`tone` do not cascade onto slotted avatars.** They drive only this
component's own ring, overlap amount, and the overflow badge's rendering ŌĆö they cannot resize
or reshape the `<lr-avatar>` children themselves, since each avatar's own `--lr-avatar-size`
lives inside *its own* shadow-scoped `:host` block and unconditionally overrides anything of
the same custom-property name inherited from an ancestor. This mirrors every other group
component in this library (`button-group`, `checkbox-group`, `radio`): none of them cascade a
size/variant prop onto their children either. The consumer is responsible for setting a
matching `size`/`shape` on both the group and each `<lr-avatar>` child for a visually coherent
stack.

**Deliberate divergence from `<lr-chip-group>`'s overflow pattern.** Chip-group's overflow
indicator is a disclosure toggle that reveals the excess children in place (`aria-expanded`,
a "Show less" relabel). This component's overflow badge does not do that ŌĆö unstacking N more
circles back into the row would defeat the entire point of a compact identity stack. Instead,
`lr-overflow-click` is a pure notification hook: the component keeps rendering the same
collapsed stack + badge regardless of whether anyone listens, and a consumer typically wires
the event to open their own popover/dialog/tooltip listing the hidden members (out of scope for
this component ŌĆö no popover dependency is introduced here). There is no `expanded` state, no
`aria-expanded`, and the badge never changes its own text/label on click.

**No roving-tabindex / arrow-key composite-widget behavior applies here, and this is
intentional, not an oversight.** Avatars are non-interactive per `<lr-avatar>`'s own
established contract (purely presentational, no built-in interactivity), so this is not a
listbox/toolbar/grid needing `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` roving focus ŌĆö the overflow badge is the
only interactive element, and as a native `<button>` it's automatically part of the normal Tab
sequence with no custom keyboard handling required.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-avatar>` elements (or any content, though the avatar pairing is the intended usage). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer inline-flex container (holds the slot and the overflow badge). |
| `overflow-badge` | The "+N" button. Only rendered while `max` is actively causing an overflow. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-avatar-group-avatar-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-2rem)`) ŌĆö Sizes the overflow badge to match the slotted avatars. Does not resize the avatars themselves (see class doc) ŌĆö set a matching `size` on each `<lr-avatar>` child directly for that.
- `--lr-avatar-group-overlap` (default: `var(--lr-size-neg-6px)`) ŌĆö Horizontal overlap between consecutive avatars (a logical `margin-inline-start`, so it auto-mirrors under `dir="rtl"`). Setting this to `0` or a positive length is a supported escape hatch that turns the stack into normal, non-overlapping spacing.
- `--lr-avatar-group-ring-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö The cutout-style ring drawn around every avatar and the overflow badge.
- `--lr-avatar-group-ring-width` (default: `var(--lr-border-width-medium)`) ŌĆö Ring thickness.
- `--lr-avatar-group-badge-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Overflow badge background. Tone-driven; see `tone`.
- `--lr-avatar-group-badge-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Overflow badge text color. Tone-driven; see `tone`.
- `--lr-avatar-group-badge-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-sm)`) ŌĆö Font size of the "+N" badge label. `size` swaps it to `var(--lr-font-size-xs)` (`sm`) or `var(--lr-font-size-md)` (`lg`), matching `<lr-avatar>`'s own `--lr-avatar-font-size` scale so the badge and the avatars it caps read at the same optical weight.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The group's own accessible name (`role="group"`'s `aria-label`). A host-level `aria-label`
wins if both are set. Unset (the default) renders no `aria-label` at all ŌĆö a screen reader
announces "group" with no name, then reads each avatar's own accessible name in turn.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
Maximum number of assigned children shown before the rest collapse behind a "+N" badge.
Flattened slot-forwarded children count the same as direct light-DOM children. Unset (the
default) means no limit ŌĆö every child is always shown. Any explicitly assigned value is
sanitized to a finite, non-negative integer via `finiteCount` ŌĆö this feeds the
`hasOverflow`/`syncChildVisibility`/`onOverflowClick` index math below directly, so a
NaN/negative value must never reach it.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description:
`'circle'` (the default) or `'square'`, reused verbatim from `<lr-avatar>`'s own
`AvatarShape` union. Drives the overflow badge's shape ŌĆö does not reshape slotted avatars
(see class doc); each avatar's own ring already adapts to that avatar's own `shape`
attribute independently.

Type: `AvatarShape`  
Default: `'circle'`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size, reused verbatim from `<lr-avatar>`'s own `AvatarSize` union. Drives the
overflow badge's size and the overlap amount ŌĆö does not resize slotted avatars (see class
doc).

Type: `AvatarSize`  
Default: `'md'`

attributes.name: tone
attributes.description:
Recolors the overflow badge, reused verbatim from `<lr-avatar>`'s own `AvatarTone` union.
`'neutral'` (the default) reads as a plain, unaccented badge.

Type: `AvatarTone`  
Default: `'neutral'`

`<lr-branch-picker>` ŌĆö the "ŌĆ╣ 2 / 5 ŌĆ║" navigator across regenerated/edited variants of one
message. Pure controlled: it never mutates its own `index` ŌĆö the same contract `<lr-pagination>`
already establishes for `page`. The host listens for `lr-branch-change`, swaps the displayed
branch content, and applies the new `index` back.

Renders nothing at all while `count < 2`, so a host can bind it unconditionally on every message
regardless of whether that message actually has multiple branches yet.
