| `position` | The visible "2 / 5" text. |
| `previous-button` | The previous-branch chevron button. |
| `previous-glyph` | The chevron glyph wrapper inside `previous-button`. |

Total number of branches. While `count < 2` the component renders nothing at all.

elements.name: lr-button
elements.description:
`<lr-button>` ŌĆö a generic action-button primitive. Renders an internal native
`<button part="base">`. `type="submit"`/`type="reset"`
are handled by this component itself via the host's own `closest('form')` ŌĆö a shadow-internal
native `<button type="submit">` does not participate in an ancestor light-DOM form's submission
on its own, since form-submitter semantics don't cross the shadow boundary.

When `href` is set to a safe link URL (`http:`/`https:`/`blob:`/`mailto:`/relative ŌĆö see
`safeLinkHref`, or `safeDownloadHref` which drops `mailto:` when `download` is set) the root
renders as a real `<a part="base" href=ŌĆ”>` instead ŌĆö for a link styled
as a button (e.g. a CTA). Native navigation is then the anchor's own activation, so the
submit/reset click handler and `type` (submit/reset) have no effect in that mode. When the
button is disabled (its own `disabled` or an ancestor `<fieldset disabled>`) the anchor renders
with `aria-disabled="true"` and **no `href`** ŌĆö an href-less anchor is not focusable or
navigable, so a disabled link button genuinely cannot be activated (unlike a bare
`aria-disabled` on a still-navigable link). An unsafe/unparseable `href` falls back to the
native `<button>`.

`accessibleLabel` (attribute `aria-label`) is forwarded reactively to the internal button/anchor
as a literal string (for an icon-only button with no visible label); external
`aria-labelledby`/`aria-describedby` idrefs are not copied across the shadow boundary.
Host `aria-haspopup`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-controls` attributes are likewise forwarded to
the internal semantic control. When `aria-controls` names elements in the host's own root, the
element-reference relationship is resolved onto the internal control so it remains valid across
this component's shadow boundary.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Default slot: the button's label content. |
| `end` | Trailing icon/content, rendered after the label. |
| `start` | Leading icon/content, rendered before the label. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The internal native `<button>` (or an `<a>` when `href` resolves to a safe link). |
| `end` | The `end` slot wrapper. |
| `label` | The default-slot label wrapper. |
| `spinner` | The loading spinner, present only while `loading` is `true`. |
| `start` | The `start` slot wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-button-width` (default: `100%`) ŌĆö Inline size of the internal button. The host defaults it to `100%` so the native button follows the host's own width; override to `auto` (or any other value) for a compact inline composition.
- `--lr-button-hover-brightness` (default: `1.08`) ŌĆö `filter: brightness()` multiplier applied while hovering a non-disabled button.
- `--lr-button-active-scale` (default: `0.9875`) ŌĆö `transform: scale()` factor applied while a non-disabled button is pressed.
- `--lr-button-spinner-duration` (default: `1s`) ŌĆö Rotation period of the `loading` spinner.
- `--lr-button-accent` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text/glyph color for the chrome-less appearances (`outlined`, `plain`, `link`). Swapped per `variant` to that variant's semantic color.
- `--lr-button-fill` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Background of `appearance="filled"`. Swapped per `variant` to that variant's semantic color.
- `--lr-button-on-fill` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color on top of `--lr-button-fill`. Swapped per `variant` to that variant's `on-*` color.
- `--lr-button-accent-fill` (default: `var(--lr-color-neutral)`) ŌĆö Background of `appearance="accent"` (and its border color). Swapped per `variant` to that variant's semantic color.
- `--lr-button-accent-on-fill` (default: `var(--lr-color-on-neutral)`) ŌĆö Text color on top of `--lr-button-accent-fill`. Swapped per `variant` to that variant's `on-*` color.
- `--lr-button-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Border color of the internal button. Swapped per `variant` to that variant's semantic color.
- `--lr-button-outlined-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border-strong)`) ŌĆö Border color of `appearance="outlined"`, which overrides `--lr-button-border`.
- `--lr-button-outlined-fill` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Background of `appearance="outlined"`. Transparent by default; set it to tint the button (e.g. a faint surface wash behind the outline) without a `::part(base)` rule. Like `--lr-button-quiet-*`, it is deliberately *not* swapped per `variant`. Note that the `:hover` `filter: brightness()` applies to whatever fill is set, so a tinted outlined button now visibly brightens on hover where a transparent one did not.
- `--lr-button-quiet-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Border color of `appearance="quiet"`.
- `--lr-button-quiet-text` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Text color of `appearance="quiet"`.
- `--lr-button-size-2xs` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-25rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="2xs"`.
- `--lr-button-size-xs` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-5rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="xs"`.
- `--lr-button-size-s` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-875rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="s"`. Matches `lr-input`/`lr-select`/`lr-combobox`'s own `size="s"` control height.
- `--lr-button-size-m` (default: `var(--lr-size-2-5rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="m"`. Matches `lr-input`/`lr-select`/`lr-combobox`'s own default control height, so a default-size button sitting next to a default-size input/select/combobox in the same row lines up.
- `--lr-button-size-l` (default: `var(--lr-size-3rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="l"`. Matches `lr-input`/`lr-select`/`lr-combobox`'s own `size="l"` control height.
- `--lr-button-size-xl` (default: `var(--lr-size-3-5rem)`) ŌĆö `min-block-size` at `size="xl"`. Matches `lr-input`/`lr-select`/`lr-combobox`'s own `size="xl"` control height.
- `--lr-button-padding-block` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö Block padding of the internal button, re-assigned per `size` tier (the default is the `m` tier's value). Override it to retune a tier without a `::part(base)` rule; `appearance="link"` ignores it (it renders with zero padding).
- `--lr-button-padding-inline` (default: `var(--lr-space-m)`) ŌĆö Inline padding of the internal button, re-assigned per `size` tier (the default is the `m` tier's value). `appearance="link"` ignores it.
- `--lr-button-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-m)`) ŌĆö Font size of the internal button, re-assigned per `size` tier (the default is the `m` tier's value). `appearance="link"` ignores it and inherits the ambient font instead.
- `--lr-button-min-height` (default: `var(--lr-button-size-m)`) ŌĆö The active tier's `min-block-size` floor. Re-assigned per `size` tier to that tier's own `--lr-button-size-*` token, and used as the fallback when `--lr-button-height` is unset.
- `--lr-button-height` ŌĆö Exact height of the internal button. **Undeclared by default** ŌĆö so the button keeps the active tier's `min-block-size` floor and an `auto` height, exactly as before. Set it (e.g. to pin the button to a fixed toolbar row) to both floor *and* cap the height. Never declare it as `auto`: a declared value wins over the `var()` fallback arm and would make every tier's floor dead code. `appearance="link"` ignores it.
- `--lr-button-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between the icon/label and any slotted content in the internal button. Unlike the size knobs above it does not vary by `size` tier. Override it to retune without a `::part(base)` rule.
- `--lr-button-radius` (default: `var(--lr-radius)`) ŌĆö Corner radius of the internal button. Does not vary by `size` tier. `appearance="link"` ignores it (it renders with zero radius).
- `--lr-button-shadow` ŌĆö Box shadow of the internal button. **Undeclared by default**, so `box-shadow` falls back to `none` ŌĆö byte-identical to before this property existed. Set it (e.g. an elevated/floating action button) without a `::part(base)` rule.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
`'filled'` (the default) reads `--lr-button-fill`, which for `variant="neutral"` is the
ambient `--lr-color-surface` -- matching this component's own container, by design, for a
low-emphasis default. `'accent'` is the loud tier: a solid, high-contrast fill for every
variant, including `neutral`. `'link'` is zero-chrome inline text ŌĆö no padding, border, or
min-height, underlined, colored from `--lr-button-accent` (the same token `'plain'` uses)
and inheriting the surrounding font ŌĆö for a text link that flows inline in a sentence rather
than a button-shaped control. `'quiet'` is a bordered, transparent-until-hover tier for a
toolbar-style icon+label action ŌĆö its border/text read fixed `--lr-color-border`/`--lr-color-text-quiet`
tokens regardless of `variant`, unlike `'outlined'`'s variant-tinted text, so it stays
visually muted at rest.

Type: `ButtonAppearance`  
Default: `'filled'`

attributes.name: aria-controls
attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: aria-expanded
attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: aria-haspopup
attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name forwarded to the internal native button or anchor. Bound to the host's
`aria-label` content attribute so changing or removing that attribute after mount keeps the
actual focused control synchronized.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: download
attributes.description:
Native anchor `download` attribute, used only while `href` resolves to a link. Ignored in
`<button>` mode.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
When set to a safe link URL, the button's root renders as a real `<a href=ŌĆ”>` instead of a
`<button>` ŌĆö for a link styled as a button (e.g. a CTA). Unset (the default) renders a plain
`<button>`, byte-for-byte as before. Only `http:`/`https:`/`blob:`/`mailto:`/relative URLs are
honored (see `safeLinkHref`); an unsafe/unparseable value falls back to the native `<button>`.
Setting `download` narrows the allowlist to `safeDownloadHref`'s, which drops `mailto:` ŌĆö a
mail handoff names no retrievable bytes, so it cannot be a download target.
`type` (submit/reset) has no effect while the anchor renders ŌĆö an anchor has no submit/reset
concept, and native navigation is its own activation. While the button is disabled the anchor
renders with no `href` (see the class doc comment), so a disabled link button cannot navigate.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
Shows an internal spinner in place of interaction affordance and disables the button, without
clearing `disabled` ŌĆö a consumer's own `disabled` state and a transient `loading` state are
independent (mirrors `<lr-export-button>`'s own `loading`/`disabled` pair).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size, `'2xs'`ŌĆō`'xl'`. `'2xs'` is the tightest tier ŌĆö a sub-`xs` size for dense,
toolbar-embedded controls (e.g. beside a native `<input type="search">` in a compact dialog
header). `'m'` (the default) is the standard size.

Type: `ButtonSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: target
attributes.description:
Native anchor `target`, used only while `href` resolves to a link. Setting this to `'_blank'`
(or any other target) automatically derives `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on the rendered anchor
ŌĆö matching `lr-card`'s/`lr-stat`'s identical pattern; `rel` is never independently settable, to
close the reverse-tabnabbing vector. Ignored in `<button>` mode.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
Forwarded to this component's own submit/reset handling ŌĆö see the class doc comment above
for why this component (not the shadow-internal `<button>`) owns that behavior.

Type: `ButtonType`  
Default: `'button'`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Tone vocabulary shared with `<lr-chip>`/`<lr-avatar>`'s own `tone` property, named
`variant` here (not `tone`) to keep the component's semantic tone vocabulary consistent.

Type: `ButtonVariant`  
Default: `'neutral'`

elements.name: lr-button-group
elements.description:
`<lr-button-group>` ŌĆö a responsive grouping primitive for related actions.
It preserves the consumer's button elements and exposes the group semantics
on the element that owns the label.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Buttons or other action controls. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The group wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-button-group-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between grouped controls.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Type: `ButtonGroupOrientation`  
Default: `'horizontal'`

elements.name: lr-calendar
elements.description:
`<lr-calendar>` ŌĆö responsive month calendar with event markers and agenda mode.

Month-view event markers are a mouse-only quick-select affordance layered on
top of the focusable day cell: the day grid's own roving-tabindex/arrow-key
navigation already targets the day `<button>`, and nesting a second
focusable control inside that same native `<button>` is not valid (a
`<button>` element must not contain interactive/tabindex descendants, and
`role="button"` itself forbids focusable descendants regardless). Agenda
view renders each event as its own real `<button part="agenda-event">` and
is the fully keyboard-accessible way to reach `lr-event-select`.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `agenda` | Agenda list. |
| `agenda-event` | One focusable event button in agenda view (`view="agenda"` only). |
| `date` | The day-of-month number inside a day cell. |
| `day` | Day cell. |
| `event` | Event marker. |
| `grid` | Month grid. |
| `header` | Calendar header. |
| `nav` | A previous/next navigation control in the header (the previous button, and the wrapper around the next button). |
| `nav-glyph` | The previous/next chevron glyph, mirrored under RTL. |
| `title` | The header's month/year title. |
| `week` | One week row within the month grid. |
| `weekday` | One weekday header cell. |
| `weekdays` | Weekday header row. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-calendar-day-min-block-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-6rem)`) ŌĆö Minimum block size of a day cell.
- `--lr-calendar-day-min-block-size-narrow` (default: `var(--lr-size-4rem)`) ŌĆö Minimum block size of a day cell once the host is narrower than 28rem.
- `--lr-calendar-day-selected-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a selected day cell, decoupled from the shared token also driving the nav-button/agenda-event hover background.

elements.name: lr-calendar-viewer
elements.description:
Parses `.ics` calendars with the optional `ical.js` peer and renders each
VEVENT as plain text, preserving summaries, times, locations, and details.

---

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root container. |
| `body` | The scrollable calendar body. |
| `error` | The error region. |
| `event` | One calendar event. |
| `event-description` | The event description. |
| `event-list` | The event list. |
| `event-location` | The event location. |
| `event-summary` | The event title. |
| `event-time` | The formatted event time. |
| `spinner` | The loading region. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-calendar-viewer-max-height` (default: `none`) ŌĆö Maximum block size of `[part="body"]` before it scrolls internally. The `maxHeight` property sets this token inline on `[part="base"]`.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-height
attributes.description:
CSS length that caps the scrollable event body.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Display name associated with the calendar. Used as the accessible name of `[part='base']`, falling back to a host `aria-label` and then the localized `calendarViewerLabel` default, matching the `csvViewerLabel`-style sibling document viewers.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-callout
elements.description:
`<lr-callout>` ŌĆö an inline message surface for status, warning, and error content.
Set `inline` for lightweight reactive status/error text: it removes the panel chrome while
preserving the semantic role, optional leading icon, and close action.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Message content. |
| `heading` | Optional heading. |
| `icon` | Optional icon. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The callout surface. |
| `close-button` | The close button's interactive hit target, sized to the shared minimum tappable size (`--lr-icon-button-size`) in both the default panel and the compact `inline` variant. |
| `close-icon` | The close button's visible "├Ś" glyph, independent of `close-button`'s hit target size -- shrinks in the `inline` variant while the hit target stays full-size. |
| `content` | The message content. |
| `heading` | The heading wrapper. |
| `icon` | The icon wrapper. |
| `message` | The message content wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-callout-background` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö The callout surface's background. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's `-quiet` tint.
- `--lr-callout-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö The callout surface's border color. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's loud color.
- `--lr-callout-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö The callout's text color. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's loud color.
- `--lr-callout-close-hover-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö The close button's hover background, decoupled from `--lr-callout-background` so a consumer can retint one without affecting the other (e.g. keeping the hover fill visibly distinct from a `variant="brand"` panel, which shares the same default token).

elements.name: lr-card
elements.description:
`<lr-card>` ŌĆö a generic, styled bordered content container: the "small bordered surface with
padding" idiom common to hero highlights, clickable grid tiles, and management-list items. A
direct `<lr-*>` counterpart to `<wa-card>`'s contract, staying slot-compatible with
`lr-result-card` where they overlap.

The header is allocation-responsive: long or translated header content can shrink and wrap,
and the actions group moves onto another line when both no longer fit side by side.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The card body. |
| `actions` | Small header controls, rendered alongside the header content. |
| `footer` | Footer content, rendered below the body. |
| `header` | Header row content, rendered above the body. |
| `media` | Media content (e.g. an image), rendered above the header. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | Wrapper around the `actions` slot. Hidden entirely when empty. |
| `base` | The outer container (a `<div>`, or an `<a>` when `href` is set). |
| `body` | Wrapper around the default slot. |
| `footer` | Wrapper around the `footer` slot. Hidden entirely when empty. |
| `header` | Wrapper around the `header` slot and `actions`. Hidden entirely when both are empty. |
| `media` | Wrapper around the `media` slot. Hidden entirely when empty. |

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual treatment, mirroring `wa-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'outlined'` (the default)
is a bordered surface -- the common "small bordered surface with padding" idiom.

Type: `CardAppearance`  
Default: `'outlined'`

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
When set, the card's root renders as a real `<a href=...>` instead of a `<div>` -- for a
whole-card link (e.g. a wide CTA tile). Unset (the default) renders a plain `<div>`.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description:
Opt-in clickable-tile behavior: the hover/focus-visible treatment (border-color shift,
`cursor: pointer`) plus, when `href` is **not** also set, real activation semantics --
`[part='base']` becomes focusable (`tabindex="0"`), responds to Enter/Space, and emits
`lr-card-activate`. With `href` set the root is already a real `<a>`, so native navigation
stays the activation and `lr-card-activate` is never fired. `false` (the default) reproduces
today's exact static output: no `tabindex`, no listeners, no events.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: target
attributes.description:
Native anchor target, used only while `href` resolves to a link. Setting this to `'_blank'`
(or any other target) automatically derives `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on the rendered
anchor -- there is no separately-settable `rel` property, so a consumer can't forget it and
leave the opened page holding a `window.opener` back-reference (reverse-tabnabbing). Matches
`lr-stat`'s/`lr-app-rail-item`'s identical pattern.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-carousel
elements.description:
`<lr-carousel>` ŌĆö an accessible single-slide carousel for arbitrary
slotted content. The index is reflected and self-managed by navigation;
every change emits `lr-slide-change` so applications can persist or
coordinate the active slide.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Slide elements. Each assigned element becomes one slide. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The carousel landmark. |
| `controls` | Previous/next control row. |
| `indicator` | An individual slide indicator's interactive hit target, sized to the shared minimum tappable size (`--lr-icon-button-size`), independent of the smaller visible dot rendered inside it (mirrors `<lr-swatch-picker>`'s `[part="swatch"]`/`[part="swatch-fill"]` split). |
| `indicator-dot` | The individual indicator's compact visible dot. |
| `indicators` | Indicator button group. |
| `next-button` | Next slide button. |
| `next-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `next-button`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `previous-button` | Previous slide button. |
| `previous-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `previous-button`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `track` | The slotted slide wrapper. |
| `viewport` | The keyboard-focusable slide viewport. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-carousel-indicator-current-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the current slide's indicator dot (`[aria-current="true"]`). Declared as an inline `var()` fallback (never on `:host`), so setting it on the element or an ancestor recolors only the current indicator without hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token.
- `--lr-carousel-indicator-current-border-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of the current slide's indicator dot.

`<lr-carousel-item>` ŌĆö an optional semantic wrapper for one child of

Type: `string`

elements.name: lr-chart
elements.description:
`<lr-chart>` ŌĆö the core Chart.js wrapper every other `lr-*-chart` tag
subclasses. Requires the optional peer dep `chart.js`; `chartjs-plugin-zoom`
(for `zoom`) and `chartjs-plugin-datalabels` (for `data-labels`/`stack-totals`)
are further optional peers loaded only on demand.

**API mirror note:** the real `wa-chart` docs page
(https://webawesome.com/docs/components/chart/) documents a `config:
ChartJS['config']` property alongside its simplified attributes ŌĆö "a
flexible wrapper around Chart.js" supporting *both* simplified attributes
and full Chart.js configuration passthrough, not a `data`/`options` prop
pair. `lr-chart` mirrors that dual surface: the `Series`-based
`datasets`/`labels`/`type`/`legend`/`xLabel`/`yLabel`/`zoom` attributes
below are the simplified surface (compatible with WA's `type`, `xLabel`,
`yLabel`, `withoutLegend`-equivalent `legend`, etc.), and the additional
`config` property is the raw-passthrough escape hatch ŌĆö a
`Partial<ChartConfiguration>` deep-merged over the generated config in
`buildConfig()`, mirroring WA's `config` property without discarding the
`Series` shape the rest of this component family (subclasses, box-plot,
histogram) is built on.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `center` | Optional overlay content positioned at the chart area's center. Useful for doughnut and pie totals. |
| `data-table` | An optional consumer-provided accessible table alternative. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The chart wrapper. |
| `canvas` | The Chart.js canvas. |
| `center` | The chart-area-centered overlay wrapper for the `center` slot. |
| `data-table` | The optional generated or slotted data table. |
| `description` | The accessible chart summary. |
| `error` | `role="alert"` message shown instead of `canvas` when the optional `chart.js` peer dependency is not installed. |
| `reset-zoom-button` | The reset-zoom control when zoom is active. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chart-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-280px)`) ŌĆö The host's `block-size`. Set on the host element from the `height` property on every change (custom properties only cascade downward, so it cannot be set from inside the shadow root); a host-level override is overwritten the next time `height` changes.
- `--lr-chart-grid-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Grid-line color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw (Chart.js paints to canvas and cannot consume `var()`).
- `--lr-chart-tick-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Axis tick-label color; also used for the `xLabel`/`yLabel`/`y2Label` axis-title text (there is no separate title-color token). Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-legend-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Legend label color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Tooltip background color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-text` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Tooltip text color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.

`chartjs-plugin-datalabels` peer. Unset (the default) leaves labels off for
this and every other chart on the page ŌĆö the plugin registers globally, so
`buildConfig()` explicitly disables it per-chart until this is set. The
screen-reader equivalent is the always-present data table (see
`show-data-table`); labels are a purely visual, canvas-only addition.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-chat-composer
elements.description:
`<lr-chat-composer>` ŌĆö the message input for a chat/agent conversation
surface: an auto-resizing textarea plus a send/stop button. Form-
associated via the `FormAssociated` mixin (see `<lr-date-input>` for the
same shape), so it participates in native `<form>` submission/validation/
reset like any other text control ŌĆö `name`/`value`/`disabled`/`required`/
`checkValidity()`/`reportValidity()` all come from that mixin.

Auto-resize reads the textarea's own *computed* line-height/padding/
border at runtime (`resizeTextarea()`) rather than assuming a fixed
px-per-row constant, so it stays correct under a consumer's own font-size/
line-height overrides. It grows between `min-rows` and `max-rows`, then
switches to internal scrolling. A `ResizeObserver` on the textarea itself
re-runs the same fit whenever its *width* changes (a responsive
breakpoint, a sidebar toggling, a window resize, an orientation change)
even though `value`/`min-rows`/`max-rows` never did -- a narrower box
wraps the same text across more lines, so the previously-fitted height
would otherwise go stale and clip content with no scrollbar to reveal it.

Enter-to-send (only active while `submit-on-enter` is true, the default):
plain Enter submits and is prevented from inserting a newline; Shift+Enter
always inserts a newline no matter what; an IME composition step (checked
via `isComposing`, with `keyCode === 229` as a defense-in-depth fallback
for browsers that report `isComposing` inconsistently on the
compositionend-adjacent keydown) is never treated as a submit trigger;
and while `status` isn't `"idle"` Enter is left alone to insert a newline
too, rather than trying to submit again -- there is nothing meaningful to
submit while a previous message is still sending/streaming, and the
textarea deliberately stays interactive (see `disabled` below) so a user
can keep composing their next message in the meantime.

Deliberately no label/hint/error chrome -- a composite chat-input control, not a labeled form
field; wrap it in your own layout for that context. A host `aria-label` is forwarded to the
internal textarea and takes precedence over the placeholder-derived name.

`lr-submit`'s `detail.value` is always the exact, untrimmed current
value (`detail.value === value` at the moment it fires) -- trimming is
left to the consumer so it never silently diverges from what `value`
itself reports. Submitting does not clear `value`; the consumer clears it
once the submission has actually been accepted (so e.g. a failed send can
leave the text in place for retry).
`submitDisabled` lets the consumer apply its own validation policy without
disabling the textarea or the busy-state Stop action.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `chips` | An attachment tray rendered above the input row (e.g. files queued for this message). |
| `leading` | Content rendered before the textarea (e.g. an attach-file trigger button). |
| `trailing` | Overrides the built-in send/stop button entirely when it has assigned content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `action-button` | The built-in send/stop button. Absent from the accessibility tree's meaningful content whenever `trailing` has assigned content. Style its busy treatment via `:host([status='sending'])`/`:host([status='streaming'])`, or the dedicated `--lr-chat-composer-busy-bg` cssprop below. |
| `base` | The bordered root container. Drops its card chrome (border, background, padding, radius) under `appearance="plain"`, where the focus affordance becomes an underline on this same part instead of the border-color shift. |
| `chips` | The wrapper around the `chips` slot. Hidden entirely when the slot is empty. |
| `leading` | The wrapper around the `leading` slot. Hidden entirely when the slot is empty. |
| `row` | The row holding the leading slot, textarea, and trailing slot/button. |
| `textarea` | The auto-resizing `<textarea>` itself. |
| `trailing` | The wrapper around the `trailing` slot and the built-in `action-button`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chat-composer-busy-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö `action-button` background while `status` is `"sending"` or `"streaming"`. Scoped separately from the shared `--lr-color-text-quiet` token, which the `textarea` part's placeholder also reads -- overriding this recolors only the busy button, not the placeholder text too.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled, padded box. `'plain'` removes the border, background, padding and corner
radius, so a composer docked inside a chat panel, dialog footer or toolbar that already draws
its own border doesn't double the frame. Focus stays visible either way: `plain` swaps the
border-color shift for an underline across the input row, since there is no border left to
recolor.

Type: `ChatComposerAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the internal textarea. Takes precedence over the placeholder-derived name.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the internal `<textarea>`'s own `autocapitalize`. Empty string omits the
attribute (browser default).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the internal `<textarea>`'s own `autocorrect` (Safari/WebKit-specific). Empty
string omits the attribute (browser default).

Named `autoCorrect` (capital `C`), not `autocorrect`, purely to dodge a TS `lib.dom.d.ts`
collision: newer DOM typings declare a `boolean`-typed `HTMLElement.autocorrect` IDL member,
which a same-named `string`-typed reactive property here would override unsoundly from the
type checker's point of view (even though the browser itself would happily let this class's
own accessor win at runtime). The explicit attribute mapping preserves the standard
lowercase `autocorrect` wire name in both Lit and generated component metadata.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: enterkeyhint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-rows
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `8`

attributes.name: min-rows
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `1`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Type: `string`

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: status
attributes.description:
Type: `ChatComposerStatus`  
Default: `'idle'`

attributes.name: stoppable
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: submit-disabled
attributes.description:
Consumer-controlled validation gate for submission. While idle, disables the built-in Send
button and suppresses Enter/click submission without disabling the textarea. Busy Stop behavior
remains governed by `status` and `stoppable`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: submit-on-enter
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: wrap
attributes.description:
Native editing-assistance attributes forwarded to the internal textarea.

Type: `ChatComposerWrap`  
Default: `'soft'`

elements.name: lr-chat-message
elements.description:
`<lr-chat-message>` ŌĆö a role-based message bubble *shell* for a chat/
agent conversation surface. It renders none of the message content itself:
the default slot carries whatever a consumer wants to display (plain
text, a `<lr-markdown>`, a custom template, anything at all) and this
component only supplies the surrounding chrome ŌĆö alignment/coloring by
`role`, an avatar/badges header row, an optional collapse toggle, an
attachments strip, and a status-aware footer (a live-updating status dot +
text, the formatted `timestamp`, a built-in retry affordance for
`status="failed"`, and an `actions` slot for everything else).

No built-in copy button is rendered. Deciding what "the copyable text" of
an arbitrary slotted message even means (plain text? the rendered
markdown source? something else?) is exactly the kind of content
interpretation this shell deliberately stays out of ŌĆö slot a copy control
into `actions` instead. Firing `lr-copy` (`detail: { text }`) from that
control keeps the event name consistent with `<lr-json-viewer>`'s own
copy affordance, for anything listening at the conversation-surface level.

Accessibility of `status`: the current status is always available as
plain visible text (`[part="status-text"]`), never color alone. A
transition *to* `"failed"`, or *from* `"streaming"` to `"sent"` (a stream
finishing), is additionally announced through an internal
`<lr-live-region>` (see that component's header for the throttled-
announcement wiring this composes) so a screen-reader user who isn't
currently focused on this message still learns about it ŌĆö *unless* the
`failure` slot has content, in which case this internal announcement is
skipped: the host's own `role="alert"` failure content is expected to
announce itself, and firing both would double-announce the same failure
with two different (and differently specific) messages. This differs
from `<lr-typing-indicator>`'s deliberately simpler `role="status"`
approach ŌĆö that component only ever has one thing to announce (its own
mount); this one has a `status` that can flip between several values
across a single element's lifetime, which is exactly the coalescing job
`<lr-live-region>` exists for.

`role` is a message-author role (`user`/`assistant`/`system`, matching
the vocabulary of every chat/completion API), *not* a WAI-ARIA role, so
it reflects to a `data-role` attribute rather than the bare `role`
attribute ŌĆö `role="user"` would collide with `Element`'s own ARIA `role`
accessor and is not a valid ARIA role token to begin with.

`actionsOutsideBubble` allows the `actions` slot to render as a sibling
immediately after the message bubble instead of nested inside the footer ŌĆö
useful for consumers whose action row (e.g., a hover-reveal copy button)
must sit visually outside the bubble's chrome.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The message body. |
| `actions` | Action controls (e.g. copy, retry), rendered at the end of the footer. |
| `attachments` | File/image attachment chips, rendered below the message body by default; see `attachments-position`. |
| `avatar` | An avatar/icon for the message author. |
| `badges` | Small status/metric chips (e.g. token count, latency, model name) ŌĆö entirely app-supplied; this component computes none of that itself. |
| `failure` | Only ever rendered while `status="failed"`. Empty (the default), the footer keeps its built-in `[part="status-text"]`/`[part="retry-button"]` exactly as before. The moment this slot has assigned content, that built-in status text and retry button are suppressed ŌĆö the host is now fully responsible for presenting its own failure UI, and the built-in `chatFailedAnnounce` live-region announcement is suppressed too (see `@event lr-retry` below for the effect on that event, and the "Accessibility of `status`" paragraph above for the built-in announcement this replaces). Content assigned here should carry `role="alert"` itself when it represents an actionable send failure ŌĆö this component does not add that role on the host's behalf, since it has no way to know what markup the host puts in this slot. This mirrors `lr-flow-node`'s `header` slot, which replaces that component's own built-in heading row the same way. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | The wrapper around the `actions` slot. Rendered inside the footer by default; a sibling immediately after `bubble` when `actionsOutsideBubble` is set. |
| `attachments` | The wrapper around the `attachments` slot. |
| `avatar` | The wrapper around the `avatar` slot. |
| `badges` | The wrapper around the `badges` slot. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the default slot (the message content). Hidden while `collapsed`. |
| `bubble` | The message bubble root. Programmatically focusable (`tabindex="-1"`) so focus has a stable place to land when the built-in retry button is removed (e.g. a `lr-retry` listener flipping `status` away from `"failed"`). Its fill/text derive from `--lr-chat-message-bubble-bg`/`--lr-chat-message-bubble-color` for every role except `user`, which derives from `--lr-chat-message-user-bubble-bg`/`--lr-chat-message-user-bubble-color` instead ŌĆö override those cssprops rather than the shared `--lr-color-*` tokens they default to. Its geometry comes from `--lr-chat-message-bubble-padding`/`--lr-chat-message-bubble-radius` for the same reason ŌĆö a `::part(bubble)` override from the consumer's tree outranks the per-`status` rules in this shadow tree and silently erases the `failed`/`streaming` treatments. |
| `collapse-button` | The built-in collapse/expand toggle (only rendered when `collapsible`). |
| `failure` | The `failure` slot itself (`display: contents` ŌĆö it contributes no box of its own, so the host's own content lays out exactly as if it were a direct child of `bubble`, with no `::part(failure)` override needed to get there). Only present in the DOM while `status="failed"`. |
| `footer` | The row below the message body ŌĆö status, timestamp, retry, and actions. Hidden entirely when none of those have anything to show. |
| `header` | The row above the message body ŌĆö avatar, badges, and the collapse toggle. Hidden entirely when none of those have anything to show. |
| `retry-button` | The built-in retry button (only rendered when `status="failed"`). |
| `status-indicator` | A small decorative (`aria-hidden`) dot reflecting `status`; absent while `status="sent"`. |
| `status-text` | The visible text twin of `status-indicator` ŌĆö carries the state in text, not just color. |
| `timestamp` | The formatted `timestamp`, rendered in a `<time>` element. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chat-message-max-width` (default: `80%`) ŌĆö Maximum inline size of the message bubble.
- `--lr-chat-message-bubble-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Bubble fill for every role except `user`.
- `--lr-chat-message-bubble-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Bubble text color for every role except `user`.
- `--lr-chat-message-user-bubble-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Bubble fill for `data-role="user"`.
- `--lr-chat-message-user-bubble-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Bubble text color for `data-role="user"`.
- `--lr-chat-message-bubble-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-m)`) ŌĆö Bubble padding. Prefer this over a `::part(bubble)` padding override: an outer-tree `::part` declaration outranks every rule in this shadow tree, which silently suppresses the per-`status` and per-role bubble styling below it.
- `--lr-chat-message-bubble-radius` (default: `var(--lr-radius)`) ŌĆö Bubble corner radius. Bubble-only by design ŌĆö `collapse-button` and `retry-button` keep reading the shared `--lr-radius`, so a rounder bubble never desyncs the controls from the rest of the library.
- `--lr-transition-ambient` (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Streaming-indicator animation duration and timing function.

attributes.name: actions-outside-bubble
attributes.description:
Renders the `actions` slot's content as a sibling immediately after `[part="bubble"]` instead of
nested inside `[part="footer"]`'s own padding/background box ŌĆö for a consumer whose action row
(e.g. a hover-reveal copy button) must sit visually outside the bubble's chrome. `false` (the
default) keeps today's exact DOM: actions render inside the footer, inside the bubble.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: attachments-position
attributes.description:
Where the `attachments` slot renders relative to the message body.
`'after'` (the default) reproduces today's exact DOM order. `'before'`
renders attachments immediately above the body -- both DOM and visual
order move together (no CSS `order` trick), so reading/focus order
always matches what's on screen.

Type: `'before' | 'after'`  
Default: `'after'`

attributes.name: collapsed
attributes.description:
Whether the message body is currently hidden. Effective whenever set,
independent of `collapsible` ŌĆö `collapsible` only controls whether the
built-in toggle button is rendered, mirroring `lr-widget`'s identical
`collapsible`/`collapsed` pair.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: collapsible
attributes.description:
Shows the built-in collapse/expand toggle in the header.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: data-role
attributes.description:
Who authored the message. Reflects to `data-role` ŌĆö see the class doc.

Type: `ChatMessageRole`  
Default: `'assistant'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: message-id
attributes.description:
Optional stable application-defined identifier for this message. Included in `lr-retry`
detail when set.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: status
attributes.description:
Delivery/generation state. Drives the footer's status dot/text and,
for `"failed"`, the bubble's danger treatment and the built-in retry
button. Declared via `static properties` above with a hand-written
accessor (see `previousStatus`).

Type: `ChatMessageStatus`

elements.name: lr-chat-viewport
elements.description:
`<lr-chat-viewport>` ŌĆö the transcript scroll container: owns stick-to-bottom behavior while an
answer streams, the "jump to latest" pill, and the unread divider.

**Two supported content shapes, auto-detected:** ordinary element children (typically
`<lr-chat-message>`s -- *slotted mode*), or exactly one `<lr-virtual-list>` (*virtual mode*,
detected via `instanceof` against the imported class so custom prefixes keep working). In virtual
mode this component defers all scrolling to the slotted list's own `scrollToIndex()`, and sizes
that list to its own height -- without which the list would scroll inside `lr-virtual-list`'s
24rem `--lr-virtual-list-height` default no matter how tall this viewport is. That sizing is a
percentage, so virtual mode needs a height-bounded parent, the same requirement slotted mode's
own scroll container already has; a consumer's own rule or inline style setting
`--lr-virtual-list-height` on the list still wins.

**Follow/release state machine.** While `follow` is engaged, content growth re-scrolls to the end.
Release happens only on a *user-intent* gesture (wheel, touchmove, scrollbar-drag, or
PageUp/ArrowUp/Home while the log region has focus) that leaves the view more than
`bottomThreshold` from the end -- a scroll caused by this component's own programmatic scrolling,
or by a layout shift, never releases it. Reaching the bottom again by any means re-engages `follow`.
The internal log defaults to `live="off"`, which avoids announcing every streaming token. Consumers
that append complete messages at an announcement-safe cadence can opt into `polite` or `assertive`.

**`scrollToUnread()` in virtual mode.** The target row is scrolled with `align: 'start'` so the
divider boundary lands at the top of the view with the unread content visible below it -- the
only alignment that matches what "scroll to the divider" means here. The underlying virtual list
only issues a corrective re-scroll for an initially-unmeasured target when that target's *own*
height is what was uncertain (`align: 'end'`/a downward `align: 'auto'`); a `'start'`-aligned
target's position is a function of the rows *before* it, so it has no such self-correction. In
practice the rows leading up to an unread boundary are usually ones the reader already scrolled
past (and so already measured), which keeps this accurate in the common case; a still-unmeasured
long-distance jump can land approximately rather than pixel-exact. Fixing that fully would mean
changing how the underlying list resolves offsets, which is out of scope here -- `align: 'end'`
was considered and rejected because it changes the visible outcome (it would put the *bottom* of
the boundary row at the viewport's bottom edge, hiding the unread content the jump is meant to
reveal, not just changing how precisely it lands).

Renders no messages and computes no unread state itself -- the host supplies `unreadStartIndex`;
no virtualization of its own (`<lr-virtual-list>`); not a generic overflow surface
(`<lr-scroller>`); no message semantics (`<lr-chat-message>`).

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The transcript: ordinary element children, or exactly one `<lr-virtual-list>`. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The positioning root. |
| `content` | The slotted-content wrapper the growth observers watch. |
| `jump-pill` | The built-in jump-to-latest button, absent while `follow` is engaged. |
| `scroll` | The scroll container (`role="log"`, `tabindex="0"`). In virtual mode it stops scrolling itself (the slotted list scrolls) but keeps the role. |
| `unread-divider` | The "New messages" separator (slotted mode only). |

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Host `aria-label`, forwarded to the internal `role="log"` element -- an `aria-label` left
on the custom-element host itself names nothing, because the log role lives inside the
shadow root. Wins over `label` and the localized default.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: bottom-threshold
attributes.description:
Px distance from the end still counted as "at bottom."

Type: `number`  
Default: `24`

attributes.name: follow
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the log region. Defaults to the localized `chatViewportLabel`;
a host `aria-label` (see `accessibleLabel`) wins over both.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: live
attributes.description:
Live-region policy forwarded to the internal `role="log"`. Keep `off` for token-by-token
streaming; use `polite` or `assertive` only when messages are appended at an announcement-safe
cadence.

Type: `ChatViewportLive`  
Default: `'off'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: unread-start-index
attributes.description:
Index of the first unread item -- element-child index in slotted mode, `items` index in virtual
mode. Host-owned unread bookkeeping in, divider/pill count out. `null` disables both.

Type: `number | null`  
Default: `null`

elements.name: lr-checkbox
elements.description:
`<lr-checkbox>` ŌĆö a boolean form control. Structurally the same idea as
`<lr-switch>` (form-associated via `ElementInternals`, click and
Space both toggle) but with checkbox semantics: `role="checkbox"` +
an `aria-checked` that can also be `"mixed"`, and a visual box/checkmark
instead of a track/thumb.

`checked` is not a plain string, so this attaches `ElementInternals`
directly and implements its own `updateValidity()` rather than using the
`FormAssociated` mixin ŌĆö see `<lr-combobox>` for the same
direct-`ElementInternals` shape with a non-string value.

Deliberately no hint/error chrome of its own -- the default slot already carries real, visible
label text (see `@slot` below), so a labeled-field frame built around `label`/`hint`/`errorText`
props has nothing to add here. A consumer needing hint/error messaging composes it in their own
wrapper (e.g. `<lr-tool-param-form>` folds a boolean field's validation error into adjacent
description text / `aria-label` rather than a `<lr-checkbox>`-owned slot), the same way a
native `<input type="checkbox">` plus `<label>` pairs with an externally-owned error node.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Label text, rendered next to the box. Clicking it toggles the checkbox, the same as clicking a native checkbox's associated `<label>`. If left empty, set `aria-label` on the host so the control still has an accessible name. A host `aria-describedby` attribute is forwarded to the internal `role="checkbox"` so externally-owned descriptions can be associated with this individual control. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The whole interactive control (`role="checkbox"`); wraps the box and label. |
| `box` | The small square that shows the checkmark/indeterminate dash. |
| `checkmark` | The checkmark (or indeterminate dash) glyph inside the box. |
| `label` | The wrapper around the default slot. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-checkbox-label-indent` (default: `calc(min(var(--lr-icon-button-size), 1.75rem) + var(--lr-space-s))`) ŌĆö The inline distance from the control's start edge to the start of the label text, i.e. the box's own floor plus the gap next to it. Published so a consumer composing per-option hint text under the label can align it without re-deriving that formula from the shadow styles, and used as the source of the real gap so the two cannot drift. Setting it on the element (or on `lr-checkbox` in your own stylesheet) moves the label; because custom properties inherit down and not sideways, it is *not* readable from a sibling node in your tree ŌĆö align a sibling by computing the same formula from `--lr-theme-icon-button-size` and `--lr-theme-space-s`, which you control.
- `--lr-checkbox-checked-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Background of `[part='box']` while `checked` or `indeterminate`. Retint just this control's checked fill without touching the shared `--lr-color-brand` token every other component also reads.
- `--lr-checkbox-checked-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of `[part='box']` while `checked` or `indeterminate`.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: indeterminate
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The form submission key, reflected synchronously for native form APIs.

Type: `string`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Type: `string`

elements.name: lr-checkbox-group
elements.description:
`<lr-checkbox-group>` ŌĆö a form-associated group of `<lr-checkbox>` elements.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-checkbox>` children. |
| `error` | Custom validation message. |
| `hint` | Supporting text. |
| `label` | Visible group label. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `error` | Validation message. |
| `form-control` | Group wrapper. |
| `form-control-label` | Label. |
| `hint` | Supporting text. |
| `options` | Checkbox collection. |

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: error-text
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The form submission key each checked child checkbox's value is grouped under in the group's
own `FormData` entry (see `sync()`). Reflected synchronously for native form APIs; renaming
rebuilds that `FormData` in the same tick -- mirrors `<lr-token-input>`'s identical `name` setter.

Type: `string`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

elements.name: lr-checkpoint
elements.description:
`<lr-checkpoint>` ŌĆö an inline conversation restore point: a labeled marker between messages
whose Restore affordance confirms inline, then hands the host a `lr-restore` event. This
component persists and restores nothing itself ŌĆö host state in, events out.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Optional supplemental content under the marker row (e.g. what changed since this point). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The marker root (`role="group"`). |
| `cancel-button` | Cancels, reverting to `restore-button`. |
| `confirm-button` | Confirms the restore, firing `lr-restore`. |
| `confirm-group` | The inline confirm prompt, swapped in for `restore-button` while confirming. |
| `confirm-prompt` | The confirm prompt text. |
| `icon` | The bookmark glyph. |
| `label` | The computed label text. |
| `line` | Each of the two flanking rules. |
| `restore-button` | The Restore button. Only rendered while `restorable`. |
| `timestamp` | The formatted `timestamp`, when set. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-checkpoint-spin-duration` (default: `1s`) ŌĆö Duration of one restoring-spinner rotation (stopped under reduced motion).

attributes.name: checkpoint-id
attributes.description:
Opaque id echoed in the `lr-restore` event detail.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: confirm-restore
attributes.description:
Gates the `lr-restore` event behind the inline confirm step.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Checkpoint name. The generic `'Checkpoint'` fallback renders while empty.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: restorable
attributes.description:
When `false`, renders a plain marker with no button ŌĆö for read-only views, or the currently-
restored checkpoint.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: restoring
attributes.description:
Host-set busy state: the Restore button becomes `aria-disabled="true"` with a spinner beside
the localized "RestoringŌĆ”" text.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-chip
elements.description:
`<lr-chip>` ŌĆö a small, content-agnostic pill for a short label: a tag, an
active-filter/scope indicator, etc. Distinct from `<lr-attachment-chip>`
(specifically file-shaped, with a thumbnail/size/upload-progress) ŌĆö this
one carries no domain assumptions at all, just a label and an optional
leading icon/dot.

`tone` tints the whole pill using the same loud-color-on-quiet-tint
convention `<lr-tool-call-chip>`/`<lr-citation-badge>` already
establish for status coloring: background is the tone's `-quiet` tint,
text/icon is the tone's loud color. `neutral` (the default) has no
dedicated token pair of its own, so it falls back to a plain
bordered-surface look ŌĆö the same "no signal" treatment
`<lr-citation-badge>`'s `default` status and `<lr-tool-call-chip>`'s
`pending` status already use.

This is a controlled component: clicking the remove (├Ś) button only fires
`lr-remove` ŌĆö the chip never removes itself from the DOM on its own
interaction, the same contract `<lr-attachment-chip>`/
`<lr-conversation-item>` already follow. A consumer owns the underlying
list and decides whether/how the click actually removes anything.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The chip's label content. |
| `icon` | Optional leading icon or status dot. Nothing is reserved for it (no extra gap) when left empty. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The pill's root container. |
| `icon` | Wrapper around the `icon` slot. Hidden entirely while empty. |
| `label` | Wrapper around the default slot. |
| `remove-button` | The remove (├Ś) affordance, only rendered while `removable`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chip-accent` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text/icon color of the pill. Each `tone` sets it to that tone's loud color.
- `--lr-chip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Background of the pill. Each `tone` sets it to that tone's `-quiet` tint.
- `--lr-chip-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Border color of the pill. Every non-neutral `tone` sets it to `transparent`.
- `--lr-chip-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-sm)`) ŌĆö Label font size. Each `size` sets it to that step's font size.
- `--lr-chip-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between the icon, label, and remove button. Each `size` sets it to that step's gap.
- `--lr-chip-radius` (default: `var(--lr-radius-pill)`) ŌĆö Corner radius of the pill and of the remove button, kept in sync so retuning one retunes both. Does not vary by `size` tier.
- `--lr-chip-icon-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-sm)`) ŌĆö Font size of the `icon` slot wrapper. Each `size` sets it to that step's icon size.
- `--lr-chip-padding-block` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-25rem)`) ŌĆö Block padding of the pill. Each `size` sets it to that step's block padding.
- `--lr-chip-padding-inline` (default: `var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö Inline padding of the pill. Each `size` sets it to that step's inline padding.
- `--lr-chip-min-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-5rem)`) ŌĆö Minimum block size of an interactive (`removable`/`toggleable`) chip's tap target. `size` keeps `3xs`/`2xs`/`xs`/`s`/`m` at the `1.5rem` (24px) WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 minimum and raises `l` to `var(--lr-size-1-75rem)` and `xl` to `var(--lr-size-2rem)`. Non-interactive display chips are unaffected by this floor.
- `--lr-chip-height` ŌĆö Exact block size of the chip. Undeclared by default, so the chip grows to fit its content (floored by `--lr-chip-min-height` when interactive). Set it to pin a fixed height. A value below the 24px interactive target is for non-interactive chips only.
- `--lr-chip-pressed-bg` (default: `var(--lr-chip-bg)`) ŌĆö Background while a toggleable chip is selected, independently themeable from its resting background.
- `--lr-chip-pressed-border` (default: `var(--lr-chip-accent)`) ŌĆö Border color while a toggleable chip is selected, independently themeable from the label/icon color.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: removable
attributes.description:
Shows the remove (├Ś) button.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description:
Opt-in toggle/pressed mode -- the current pressed value. Setting `selected` (to `true`, the
common way to start a chip already pressed) opts the chip into toggle mode automatically, so
`<lr-chip selected>` alone is enough: `[part='base']` becomes focusable and
keyboard-activatable (Enter/Space, mirroring native `<button>` behavior), reflects
`aria-pressed`, and toggles on click/activation, emitting `lr-chip-select`. That opt-in
(tracked by `toggleable`, see below) persists once made, so toggling `selected` back to
`false` never strips the chip's interactivity -- a chip a user has clicked "off" must stay
clickable to turn it back "on". Has no effect (no interactive semantics added to
`[part='base']`) when combined with `removable`, since the remove button already nests inside
`[part='base']` -- axe-core's `nested-interactive` rule forbids a focusable descendant of a
`role="button"` ancestor, and this component's two real use cases (a chart-series visibility
toggle, a category filter chip) never need both at once. `false` (the default, with
`toggleable` also left at its default) reproduces today's exact passive-label-pill output.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual density. `m` preserves the original chip dimensions.

Type: `ChipSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: toggleable
attributes.description:
Explicit opt-in into `selected`'s toggle/pressed interactive mode, independent of the
*current* value of `selected`. Setting `selected` to `true` at any point opts in
automatically (see its doc comment) and keeps this `true` from then on, which is enough for
a chip that starts already pressed. Set `toggleable` directly for a chip that must be
clickable from the outset while starting **unselected** -- e.g. an initially-inactive
category filter chip -- since `selected`'s own default (`false`) can't be distinguished from
"never opted in" on its own.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: tone
attributes.description:
Status/emphasis color. `neutral` (the default) reads as plain/unstyled.

Type: `ChipTone`  
Default: `'neutral'`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
Opaque consumer bookkeeping value ŌĆö never read, validated, or rendered
by this component itself, only ever echoed back verbatim (including
`undefined` if never set) in `lr-remove`'s detail.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-chip-group
elements.description:
`<lr-chip-group>` ŌĆö a flex-wrap container for a set of `<lr-chip>`
children (plain light-DOM composition ŌĆö direct children are the chips,
the same shape `<lr-split>`'s panels / `<lr-source-list>`'s cards
take ŌĆö no `.items` array prop).

`max-visible` is entirely optional. When unset, every child is always
shown and this component does nothing beyond flex-wrap layout. When set
and the group has more chip children than that, the excess children are
hidden (via their own `hidden` property ŌĆö CSS alone can't parameterize
`:nth-child` on a runtime prop, so this reaches into the light DOM the
same way `<lr-split>` sets each panel's inline `flex`/`order`) and a
"+N" overflow-indicator pill takes their place. Clicking it is a toggle:
it reveals the rest (and relabels itself "Show less"); clicking again
re-collapses back to `max-visible`. `lr-overflow-toggle` fires only from
that click ŌĆö i.e. only when `max-visible` is actually causing an overflow
state ŌĆö never as a side effect of `max-visible`/children changing on
their own.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-chip>` elements (or any content, though the chip pairing is the intended usage). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The flex-wrap container (holds both the slot and the overflow indicator). |
| `overflow-indicator` | The "+N" / "Show less" toggle button. Only rendered while `max-visible` is actively causing an overflow. |

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-visible
attributes.description:
Maximum number of assigned children shown before the rest collapse behind a "+N"
indicator. Flattened slot-forwarded children count the same as direct light-DOM children.
Unset (the default) means no limit ŌĆö every child is always shown. Any explicitly assigned
value is sanitized to a finite, non-negative integer via `finiteCount` ŌĆö this feeds the
`hasOverflow`/`syncChildVisibility` index comparisons below directly, so a NaN/negative value
must never reach them.

Type: `number | undefined`

elements.name: lr-chunk-inspector
elements.description:
`<lr-chunk-inspector>` ŌĆö a ranked retrieved-chunks list: relevance score bars with tier tones,
expandable chunk text, and the deep-link event that lands a chunk in `lr-document-viewer`.
Never fetches, ranks, or dedupes; never opens documents itself.

Every row-level part is reachable through `::part()` in both rendering paths: above
`virtualize-at` a row lives in the internal `<lr-virtual-list>`'s shadow root, and its parts are
re-exported from there under the same names. Row *state* is exposed as an additional part name
(`chunk-current`, `score-current`, `score-fill-<tone>`, `text-clamped`) rather than as an
attribute on the part, because Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()` --
`::part(chunk)[aria-current='true']` is invalid CSS. The equivalent attributes are still present
on the elements. A state part is a second token in the same `part` attribute, so a `[part~="..."]`
(not `[part="..."]`) selector is the one that matches inside a tree.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The `role="group"` wrapper. |
| `chunk` | One chunk row. Carries `role="listitem"` only in the non-virtualized path; while virtualized the surrounding `<lr-virtual-list>` row supplies that role instead. |
| `chunk-current` | Additional part on the `chunk` row matching `activeId`. |
| `empty` | The empty-state message, shown when `chunks` is empty. |
| `open-button` | The chunk's title/open `<button>`. |
| `score` | The visible percent-score text. |
| `score-bar` | The `aria-hidden` score bar track. |
| `score-current` | Additional part on the current row's `score` line. |
| `score-fill` | The score bar's tone-mapped fill. |
| `score-fill-danger` | Additional part on a `score-fill` in the low-score tier. |
| `score-fill-success` | Additional part on a `score-fill` in the high-score tier. |
| `score-fill-warning` | Additional part on a `score-fill` in the medium-score tier. |
| `text` | The chunk's text preview. Omitted when `compact`. |
| `text-clamped` | Additional part on a `text` preview that is still collapsed (line-clamped); dropped once that chunk is expanded. |
| `title` | The `<span>` inside `open-button` carrying the visible title text. Split from `open-button` (rather than a dual part name on one element) because an exact-match `[part="..."]` CSS attribute selector -- as used by this component's own tests -- cannot match a multi-token `part` attribute value. |
| `toggle` | The "Show more"/"Show less" button. Omitted when `compact`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chunk-inspector-current-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the chunk matching `activeId`. **Contrast-sensitive:** paired with `--lr-chunk-inspector-current-color`, which has to keep a 4.5:1 ratio against it.
- `--lr-chunk-inspector-current-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color of the current chunk's `[part="score"]` line. **Contrast-sensitive:** the quiet token it replaces only reaches ~4.24:1 against the current background, so override this together with `--lr-chunk-inspector-current-bg`, never alone.

attributes.name: active-id
attributes.description:
Marks the chunk currently open in the viewer.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Compact rows render title + score bar + open button only.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: sort
attributes.description:
Type: `'score' | 'none'`  
Default: `'score'`

attributes.name: virtualize-at
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `50`

elements.name: lr-citation-badge
elements.description:
`<lr-citation-badge>` ŌĆö an inline `[n]` citation marker with a hover/
focus preview popover and confidence/verification-status coloring. Used
for an agent response's inline citations, each carrying a `source-id`
that matches a corresponding `<lr-source-card>` shown elsewhere on the
page (a sibling component in this same family ŌĆö this component doesn't
import or know anything about it, only carries the id through its
events).

The default slot is *not* the badge's visible content ŌĆö the badge always
renders `[index]` ŌĆö it's reserved for optional rich preview content (e.g.
a filename + short excerpt) shown in a floating popover on hover/focus,
positioned with `internal/positioner.js`'s `place()` the same way
`<lr-tool-call-chip>` positions its own detail tooltip. No popover shows
at all when the slot carries no content, and the popover never traps
focus ŌĆö it's supplementary preview content, not a modal, so Tab continues
past the badge normally even while it happens to be visible from a mouse
hover. When preview content exists, the button keeps a stable
`aria-describedby` relationship to the `role="tooltip"` panel while it is
both hidden and visible, so assistive technology can resolve the preview
as soon as focus causes it to open.

Two distinct signals fire from the same badge: `lr-citation-activate`
(click, or Enter while focused ŌĆö native `<button>` behavior, no listener
needed for the Enter case) is the lightweight "jump to this source"
signal a host wires to scrolling/highlighting the matching
`<lr-source-card>`. `lr-citation-open` (dblclick, or Space while
focused) is a distinct "full preview" signal ŌĆö `href` in its detail is
`undefined` when the `href` prop isn't set; the consumer decides what
"open" means (a new tab, a dialog, etc). A double-click still fires two
`lr-citation-activate` events (one per constituent click ŌĆö standard
browser `dblclick` behavior) in addition to the one `lr-citation-open`;
a consumer that only cares about the richer signal on a double-click
should ignore the paired activate events in that case.

Status coloring: `verified`/`high` use the success tones (a claim that's
been checked out, or the model is confident in). `medium`/`low` use
warning (progressively less certain, but still a real citation).
`unverified` uses danger ŌĆö deliberately distinct from `low`: "hasn't been
checked at all" is a different (arguably riskier) claim than "checked but
uncertain". `default` renders as plain neutral text with no background
tint, for citations that carry no confidence/verification signal at all.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Rich preview/tooltip content (e.g. a filename + excerpt), shown in a floating popover on hover/focus. Nothing renders (no hover affordance at all) when this slot is empty. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The clickable badge (`<button>`). |
| `bracket` | Each of the two literal `[`/`]` glyphs. |
| `index` | The citation number. |
| `popover` | The floating preview panel (only meaningful while open). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-citation-badge-accent` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Text color of `base`, and its hover border color. Swapped by the reflected `status`: `var(--lr-color-success)` for `high`/`verified`, `var(--lr-color-warning)` for `medium`/`low`, `var(--lr-color-danger)` for `unverified`.
- `--lr-citation-badge-bg` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Background of `base`. Swapped by the reflected `status` to the matching `-quiet` tone (`success`/`warning`/`danger`).
- `--lr-citation-badge-border` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Resting border color of `base`; no `status` changes it, so it stays transparent unless a host overrides it.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
Optional direct link target for the citation's source. Carried into
`lr-citation-open`'s detail as-is; this component never navigates.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: index
attributes.description:
The citation number shown, e.g. `3` renders as `[3]`. Renders directly as visible text (and
feeds `lr-citation-activate`/`lr-citation-open`'s `detail.index`), so a NaN/non-integer
value would be a real user-facing rendering bug ŌĆö clamped to a finite, 1-indexed integer
(citation numbers are conventionally 1-indexed, matching this property's own default).
