attributes.description:
Upper counterpart of `minlength` (native `maxlength`/`tooLong`), with the same parsing and the
same default of `undefined`. Note that native `maxlength` also *prevents* typing beyond the
limit; it reports `tooLong` for values that arrive some other way (paste of a longer value,
a programmatic assignment).

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
`type="number"` only ŌĆö forwarded to the internal native `<input>`'s own `min`/`max`/`step`
and consulted by that same native input's constraint validation (see `updateValidity()`).
Defaults to `undefined` (no lower bound). The `min` attribute is parsed as a number here; the
declared type also admits a string so a subclass bound to a non-numeric native input type can
narrow the attribute parsing to that type's own literal form (`<lr-time-input>`'s `09:00`)
without redeclaring the whole property surface.

Type: `number | string | undefined`

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description:
Minimum text length, forwarded to the internal native `<input>`'s own `minlength` and
consulted by that same native input's constraint validation (`tooShort`, see
`updateValidity()`). Defaults to `undefined` (no lower bound). Like native `minlength`, an
empty value never violates it ŌĆö pair it with `required` to also reject empty. Ignored by the
native input for `type="number"`/`type="time"`, exactly as the platform specifies.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: password-visible
attributes.description:
`type="password"` only ŌĆö whether the field currently reveals its raw text. Toggled by the
built-in `password-toggle` button; also settable by a consumer up front.

Default: `false`

attributes.name: pattern
attributes.description:
A regular expression the value must match in full, forwarded to the internal native
`<input>`'s own `pattern` and validated by it (`patternMismatch`). Defaults to `undefined`
(no pattern). Compiled by the browser with the `v` flag and anchored to the whole value, so
no `^`/`$` is needed; an empty value never violates it.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-page-rail
elements.description:
`<lr-page-rail>` ŌĆö a virtualized vertical thumbnail rail for page-addressed documents, with
per-page highlight heat markers. Two modes: **wired** (`viewer`/`for` supply a live
`PageThumbnailSource`, e.g. `lr-pdf-viewer` -- thumbnails render lazily as rows materialize, and
the rail tracks page/count from the viewer's own events) and **mediated** (`page-count`/`page` are
host-bound directly, rows render a placeholder glyph -- still a fully functional pager). In wired
mode the viewer's `page` is the single source of truth.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The rail. |
| `heat` | The heat-marker cluster. |
| `heat-dot` | One tone-colored heat marker (or the `+n` overflow marker). |
| `heat-dot-accent` | An accent-tone heat marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `heat-dot-danger` | A danger-tone heat marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `heat-dot-neutral` | A neutral-tone heat marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `heat-dot-overflow` | The `+n` overflow marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `heat-dot-success` | A success-tone heat marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `heat-dot-warning` | A warning-tone heat marker (also carries `heat-dot`). |
| `page` | One page button. |
| `page-current` | The page button for the current `page` (also carries `page`). |
| `page-number` | The visible page number. |
| `pages` | The embedded `lr-virtual-list`. |
| `thumbnail` | The thumbnail canvas wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-page-rail-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-24rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the virtualized rail.
- `--lr-page-rail-current-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the `[part="page-current"]` button for the current `page`.

`viewer`.

Default: `''`

elements.description:
`<lr-pagination>` ŌĆö controlled, server-friendly page navigation with an
editable page jump and optional item-range summary.

The component never mutates `page`. Activating a control emits
`lr-page-change`; the consumer applies the requested page after its own
routing or data-fetch decision. Once the `page` property changes, a polite
live region announces the applied page.

---

| `next-button` | The next-page button. |
| `next-icon` | The next-page directional icon. |
| `page-count` | The total page count shown after the input. |
| `page-field` | The current-page input and page-count wrapper. |
| `page-input` | The validated numeric page-jump input. |
| `previous-button` | The previous-page button. |
| `previous-icon` | The previous-page directional icon. |
| `summary` | The item-range summary. |

- `--lr-pagination-font-size` ŌĆö Control font size; defaults from the `size` variant.
- `--lr-pagination-control-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö Inner padding of the nav buttons and the page input. Uniform across every `size` (the control footprint is fixed by `--lr-pagination-control-size`, so this only adjusts the icon/digit inset).

elements.name: lr-path-strip
elements.description:
`<lr-path-strip>` ŌĆö a linear node -> relation -> node chain rendering "why A connects to B"
(GraphRAG local-search reasoning paths) as a compact, horizontally scrollable strip.
One-dimensional and presentational: no path finding, no branching, no per-element popovers.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `arrow` | The `aria-hidden` directed-edge arrow glyph, logical (mirrors under RTL). |
| `base` | The root wrapper, hosting the delegated roving-tabindex keydown handler. |
| `empty` | The empty-state message, shown when `path` is empty. |
| `node` | One `node`-kind element (a `<button>`). |
| `relation` | One `edge`-kind element's relation label (a `<button>`). |

Accessible name; falls back to the localized `pathStripLabel`.

elements.name: lr-pdf-viewer
elements.description:
Fetches PDF bytes and renders their pages with the optional `pdfjs-dist` peer. Pages are composed
through `lr-virtual-list`, while a PDF.js text layer keeps rendered text selectable and copyable.
Adopts `DocumentAnchorTarget`: `page`, `text-quote`, and `region` anchors resolve; highlights paint
via one `<lr-highlight-layer>` per page, stacked beneath the text layer (canvas -> highlights ->
text layer) so starting a text selection over a cited passage keeps working. Pointer activation of
a highlight is hit-tested at the page-wrapper level (`onPageClick`) since the text layer sitting on
top intercepts most direct pointer events; keyboard activation reaches the highlight layer's own
roving-tabindex rects directly, since z-stacking doesn't affect tab order. Accepted residual: a
click that ends a text-selection drag over a highlighted passage never activates it (the
selection-in-progress check in `onPageClick` exists precisely to distinguish that case from a
genuine activation click).

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root viewer container. |
| `error` | The error message region. |
| `page` | One rendered page wrapper. |
| `page-canvas` | The canvas a page's content is painted onto. |
| `page-indicator` | The current page text. |
| `pages` | The virtualized page list. |
| `search-match` | A painted in-document search match. |
| `search-match-active` | The currently active search match (also carries `search-match`). |
| `spinner` | The loading status region. |
| `text-layer` | Selectable text positioned over a page canvas. |
| `text-span` | One generated text run inside a page's text layer. |
| `toolbar` | Pagination and zoom controls. |
| `zoom-indicator` | The current zoom percentage. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-pdf-viewer-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-24rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the virtualized page list. Also settable via the `max-height` property.

attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:
A CSS length (e.g. `"30rem"`); once set, overrides `--lr-pdf-viewer-height` -- the block size
of the virtualized page list -- declaratively, the same `max-height` attribute
`<lr-notebook-viewer>`/`<lr-svg-viewer>`/`<lr-xml-viewer>` expose, rather than requiring a
consumer to set the differently-named CSS custom property inline.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:
Display name used as the document's accessible label fallback.

Type: `number`

elements.name: lr-phone-input
elements.description:
`<lr-phone-input>` ŌĆö a country-aware telephone field whose form value is
canonical E.164. National formatting and numbering-plan validation are
supplied through `adapter`; without one, already-international E.164 input
remains useful and national input stays editable with `incomplete` validity.

User edits emit native-style `input` and `change` events. Event detail
exposes both the canonical `value` and editable `inputValue`; programmatic
property changes are silent. Phone-number text is deliberately LTR while
the form chrome and country selector follow the inherited direction. A host
`aria-label` names the internal telephone input and wins over every derived
or component-specific fallback.

The country selector keeps the real, fully accessible native `<select>`
(full country names in its popup, native mobile pickers, type-ahead) but
renders it invisibly over a compact visual trigger ŌĆö selected alpha-2 code
plus a design-system chevron ŌĆö so long localized country names never clip
the closed control and the calling code isn't shown twice. With `flags`
set, the trigger also shows the selected country's `<lr-flag>`; actual flag
artwork still comes from the optional `@aceshooting/lyra-flags` peer,
registered by the consumer via `components/media/flag/flag-peer.js`
exactly as for a standalone `<lr-flag>` (without that registration the
trigger simply omits the image). Native `<option>`s cannot contain
elements, so the open popup remains text-only by platform design.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `country-prefix` | Optional visual displayed before the country selector, such as a flag. |
| `error` | Custom error content. |
| `hint` | Custom hint content. |
| `label` | Custom label content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `calling-code` | The selected country's calling code. |
| `country` | The country selector region (invisible native select over the visual trigger). |
| `country-code` | The selected alpha-2 code (or placeholder text) inside the trigger. |
| `country-prefix` | Optional country adornment slot wrapper. |
| `country-select` | The native country selector, stretched invisibly over the trigger. |
| `country-trigger` | The visible, decorative closed-state trigger. |
| `error` | The error or validation message. |
| `expand-icon` | The dropdown indicator inside the trigger. |
| `flag` | The selected country's `<lr-flag>` inside the trigger (only with `flags`). |
| `form-control` | The outer form-control wrapper. |
| `form-control-label` | The visible label. |
| `hint` | The hint message. |
| `input` | The native telephone input. |
| `input-wrapper` | The country selector and telephone input wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-phone-input-padding-block` ŌĆö Input block-padding, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-phone-input-font-size` ŌĆö Input/flag/country-code/calling-code font size, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-phone-input-control-min-height` ŌĆö Input-wrapper block-size floor, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-phone-input-control-height` ŌĆö Exact input-wrapper height. Unset by default, which leaves `--lr-phone-input-control-min-height` as a floor only; set it to a length to both floor and cap the row (e.g. to pixel-match a sibling field in the same toolbar row). Because it is never declared by the component itself, it can be set from an ancestor or an outer-tree rule as well as inline on the element.

attributes.name: spellcheck

Forwarded to the internal `<input>`'s own `spellcheck`. Defaults to `true`, matching the
native element's own default. Uses spellcheckConverter rather than Lit's default
presence-based boolean converter so an explicit `spellcheck="false"` attribute is honored; a
`.spellcheck=${false}` property binding can still turn this off directly.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

elements.name: lr-pie-chart
elements.description:
`<lr-pie-chart>` ŌĆö `<lr-chart>` with `type` locked to `"pie"`. Single-series:
one `Series` with `data: number[]` and `color: string[]` as the slice palette.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `center` | Optional overlay content positioned at the chart area's center. Useful for doughnut and pie totals. |
| `data-table` | An optional consumer-provided accessible table alternative. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The chart wrapper. |
| `canvas` | The Chart.js canvas. |
| `center` | The chart-area-centered overlay wrapper for the `center` slot. |
| `data-table` | The optional generated or slotted data table. |
| `description` | The accessible chart summary. |
| `error` | `role="alert"` message shown instead of `canvas` when the optional `chart.js` peer dependency is not installed. |
| `reset-zoom-button` | The reset-zoom control when zoom is active. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chart-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-280px)`) ŌĆö The host's `block-size`. Set on the host element from the `height` property on every change (custom properties only cascade downward, so it cannot be set from inside the shadow root); a host-level override is overwritten the next time `height` changes.
- `--lr-chart-grid-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Grid-line color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw (Chart.js paints to canvas and cannot consume `var()`).
- `--lr-chart-tick-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Axis tick-label color; also used for the `xLabel`/`yLabel`/`y2Label` axis-title text (there is no separate title-color token). Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-legend-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Legend label color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Tooltip background color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-text` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Tooltip text color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.

interval (play/pause), the common building block behind ad-hoc

attributes.description:
Re-declared as a Lit reactive property (shadowing the inherited plain
`HTMLElement.hidden` IDL property) so that setting `el.hidden = true` ŌĆö
or the `hidden` attribute ŌĆö actually enters Lit's change-tracking system
and the `willUpdate` auto-pause guard below can see it via `changed`.

Type: `number`

attributes.name: length

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

elements.name: lr-policy-summary
elements.description:
`<lr-policy-summary>` ŌĆö a read-only list of guardrail, permission, privacy, and tool-policy
decisions, each carrying an `allow` / `deny` / `needs-review` state and an always-visible,
accessible explanation of why that decision was made -- never conveyed by color alone.

Composes `<lr-badge>` for the compact per-decision state indicator and `<lr-callout inline>`
for the explanation text: the callout's own `role="alert"`/`role="status"` semantics already
carry the right urgency per state (`deny` renders as an alert, `allow`/`needs-review` as
status), so this component only needs to pick the matching `variant` rather than re-implement
that wiring. `<lr-details>` renders a decision's optional richer `detail` (matched rule text,
policy id, cited evidence) behind progressive disclosure, collapsed by default, instead of
always showing it alongside the shorter `explanation`.

`decisions` is controlled and never mutated by this component -- pass a new array (e.g. as a
guardrail pipeline resolves) to update it. This is a summary surface, not an approval gate:
there is no per-decision action here, and a decision's `state` is fixed data, not something a
viewer can change from this component.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper, only rendered while `decisions` is non-empty. |
| `category` | The decision's localized category text. |
| `count` | One state's localized count text; carries `data-state`. |
| `decision` | One decision row (`role="listitem"`); carries `data-state` and `data-category`. |
| `decision-header` | The row's category/label/state-badge line. |
| `detail` | The `<lr-details>` progressive-disclosure panel for `detail`, only rendered when a decision defines one. |
| `empty` | The `<lr-empty>` shown when `decisions` is empty. |
| `explanation` | The `<lr-callout inline>` wrapping the always-visible `explanation` text. |
| `label` | The decision's `label` text. |
| `list` | The `role="list"` wrapper around every decision row. Its accessible name defaults to the localized `policySummaryLabel`, but a host-level `aria-label` on `<lr-policy-summary>` wins over that default. |
| `state-badge` | The resolved `<lr-badge>` state indicator. |
| `summary` | The always-visible allow/deny/needs-review count row. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-policy-summary-count-allow-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-success)`) ŌĆö Text color of the `allow` count.
- `--lr-policy-summary-count-deny-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-danger)`) ŌĆö Text color of the `deny` count.
- `--lr-policy-summary-count-needs-review-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-warning)`) ŌĆö Text color of the `needs-review` count. Restyling a state count otherwise requires overriding the library-wide status tokens, since `::part(count)[data-state]` is invalid CSS.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-poll-status
elements.description:
`<lr-poll-status>` ŌĆö a "next scheduled refresh" countdown with a built-in pause control: a
ticking `M:SS` display counting down to the next scheduled action, a "RefreshingŌĆ”" state at
zero, a "Paused" state while `paused` (instead of freezing on a stale value), and a
pause/resume toggle. First-party invention (no Web Awesome equivalent); the
closest existing component, `lr-stream-status`, is scoped to transport/connection-health
phases, a different concern from a scheduled-interval countdown -- this mirrors its internal
`<lr-live-region>` composition for accessible phase-transition announcements.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `countdown` | The `M:SS` text (or "RefreshingŌĆ”" once due, or "Paused" while `paused`). |
| `indicator` | The pulsing status dot. |
| `pause-button` | The built-in pause/resume toggle. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-poll-status-due-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-success)`) ŌĆö Background of `indicator` while `data-due` is set, without repainting every other component that reuses the shared success token.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description:
Whether the poll cycle is running at all. `true` (the default).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: next-in-ms
attributes.description:
Milliseconds until the next scheduled action, as of whenever this was last set -- setting it
(re)starts the countdown from "now." Unset (the default) shows no countdown.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: paused
attributes.description:
User-toggled pause -- while `true`, the countdown display freezes and `lr-poll-due` never
fires. `false` (the default).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-push-to-talk
elements.description:
`<lr-push-to-talk>` ŌĆö a mic capture button owning the full `getUserMedia` + `MediaRecorder`
lifecycle: permission request, recording, optional chunked streaming, teardown. The one place in
this library that touches the microphone ŌĆö no SDK, no LiveKit/ElevenLabs import, native browser
APIs only.

`mode="hold"` (the default) is a press-and-hold gesture: pointerdown/Enter-or-Space-keydown starts,
pointerup/keyup/blur stops. `mode="toggle"` is click-to-start/click-to-stop with `aria-pressed`.
Escape cancels the in-progress take in either mode (discarding it ŌĆö `lr-record-cancel`, never
`lr-record-stop`). `state` is a read-only lifecycle reflected to the `data-state` attribute (not
`state`, avoiding any ambiguity with a native form-control `state`): `'idle' | 'requesting' |
'denied' | 'recording' | 'error'`. A host-level `aria-label` (set on `<lr-push-to-talk>` itself)
overrides the computed trigger label.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `icon` | Replaces the default mic glyph. |
| `recording-icon` | Replaces the default recording-state pulse glyph. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `icon` | Wrapper around the `icon` slot / default mic glyph. |
| `pulse` | Wrapper around the `recording-icon` slot / default pulse glyph, rendered only while recording. |
| `status` | Visible status text for the `requesting`/`denied`/`error`/unsupported states. |
| `timer` | The `M:SS` elapsed-time readout, rendered only while recording and `show-timer`. |
| `trigger` | The capture button. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-push-to-talk-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-3rem)`) ŌĆö Inline and block size of the circular `trigger` button.
- `--lr-push-to-talk-recording-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-danger)`) ŌĆö Border and text color of `[part="trigger"]` while `state` is `recording`. Recolors only the recording treatment, leaving every other danger-toned surface on the page untouched.

attributes.name: timeslice-ms

`> 0` requests periodic `lr-record-chunk` slices from `MediaRecorder` every this-many
milliseconds; `0` (the default) requests one slice at stop. Clamped to
`[1, MAX_TIMEOUT_MS]` at the point it's handed to `MediaRecorder.start()` -- see `start()` --
so a non-finite/oversized value can't reach that native API unsanitized.

Type: `number`

elements.name: lr-qr-code
elements.description: `` -- encodes `value` as a QR symbol with the optional `qrcode` peer dependency (Reed-Solomon error correction and every other algorithmic step of the QR spec is delegated to that library, never hand-rolled) and draws the resulting module matrix onto a canvas itself, one square (optionally rounded) cell per module, with a light quiet-zone margin baked in around the symbol so it stays scannable out of the box with zero consumer configuration. Extra host CSS `padding` around the element adds further breathing room on top of that baked-in margin, but is not required for baseline scannability. The canvas -- not the host -- owns `role="img"` and the accessible name, since it is the one meaningful descendant here (mirrors `lr-file-icon`'s single-image pattern rather than the composite-group pattern used by `lr-heatmap`/`lr-word-cloud`). The accessible name resolves, in order: `label`, then a host `aria-label` attribute (forwarded onto the canvas), then `value` itself. An empty `value` renders `[part="empty"]` instead of an `img`-role element -- there is nothing to encode or name. `--lr-qr-code-fill`/`--lr-qr-code-background` (dark/light modules) default to `--lr-color-text`/`--lr-color-surface`, which -- like every semantic token in this library -- flip under a dark theme. That means the *default* rendering under a dark theme is a polarity-inverted QR code (light modules on a dark background) rather than the conventional dark-on-light. Contrast itself stays strong in both themes (these are the app's own high-contrast text/surface pair), so human legibility is unaffected -- only third-party barcode *scanner* robustness across less-tolerant scanning apps is the residual, consumer-overridable risk. A consumer needing guaranteed cross-scanner compatibility regardless of page theme should pin `--lr-qr-code-fill: #000` / `--lr-qr-code-background: #fff` explicitly at the point of use. Deliberately out of scope for this component, not oversights: logo/image embedding; a finder-pattern-corner accent color; auto-shrinking to fit a narrow container (this component's `size` is a direct request for a specific rendered pixel density, like `` -- the consumer picks a size that fits their layout, this component never second-guesses it, though it still renders correctly at its default size inside a narrow allocation); form association (`value` is caller-supplied display data the user doesn't edit through this component -- no `FormAssociated` mixin, no label/hint/error chrome, no `ElementInternals`); and any motion/`prefers-reduced-motion` branch (nothing here animates). Keyboard focus is likewise intentionally absent -- the canvas is a static image standing in for `value`, structurally like ``, not an interactive grid. --- **CSS Shadow Parts** | Name | Description | | --- | --- | | `base` | The outer wrapper, sized to `size`├Ś`size` CSS px in every state. | | `canvas` | The rendered QR code canvas. | | `empty` | Shown when `value` is empty. | | `error` | Shown when the peer is missing, or `value` failed to encode. | | `loading` | Shown while the optional `qrcode` peer is loading, the first time it's needed. | --- **CSS Custom Properties** - `--lr-qr-code-fill` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Dark/foreground module color. - `--lr-qr-code-background` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Light/background module color, including the quiet zone.

attributes.name: error-correction
attributes.description:
QR error-correction level. Normalized (upper-cased, validated against `L`/`M`/`Q`/`H`,
falling back to `H`) on every assignment, attribute or property, so it's never a transient
garbage value.

Type: `LyraQrCodeErrorCorrection`

elements.name: lr-query-builder
elements.description:
`<lr-query-builder>` ŌĆö a composable structured-query builder for tabular/dashboard data: a
flat list of field/operator/value condition rows combined with one AND/OR combinator.

Distinct from this package's `<lr-graph-query-builder>`: that component builds typed
relationship/path queries over a knowledge graph, a genuinely different data model from this
one's flat tabular field/operator/value conditions ŌĆö they never share a file or a value type.

Fully controlled: a host supplies `fields` (the available columns, each with a
`QueryBuilderFieldType` that determines its offered operators and value control) and `value`
(a plain, serializable `{ combinator, conditions }` object ŌĆö safe to persist or send to a
backend as-is, the same shape convention as this package's `<lr-rubric-form>`/`<lr-filter-bar>`).
This component never mutates `fields`/`value` in place or calls out to storage/network itself;
every change is surfaced through `value`/`lr-input` for the host to own.

Each row composes `<lr-select>` for the field and operator pickers, and a value control chosen
from the selected field's `type`: `<lr-input type="text">` (`string`), `<lr-input
type="number">` (`number`), `<lr-select>` with `True`/`False` options (`boolean`),
`<lr-date-input>` (`date`), `<lr-select>` (`enum`, `eq`/`neq`) or a multi-select
`<lr-combobox>` (`enum`, `in`/`notIn`). A unary operator (`isEmpty`/`isNotEmpty`) renders no
value control. `<lr-icon-button icon="trash">` removes a row; `<lr-button>` appends one.

This is a composite query-definition control, not a single submittable form field ŌĆö it
deliberately ships no `label`/`hint`/`errorText` chrome or native form association (the
`label`/`hint`/`error` triad those controls share doesn't fit a multi-row, multi-field
composite the way it fits one value). A host names the whole control via a plain `aria-label`
attribute, applied to the element that owns `role="group"`.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `add-button` | The "Add condition" `lr-button`. |
| `base` | The outer wrapper. |
| `combinator` | The AND/OR combinator `lr-select`, rendered only when there are 2+ conditions. |
| `condition` | One field/operator/value row. |
| `conditions` | The wrapper around the condition rows. |
| `empty` | The message shown when there are no fields, or no conditions yet. |
| `field-select` | A row's field `lr-select`. |
| `operator-select` | A row's operator `lr-select`. |
| `remove-button` | A row's remove `lr-icon-button`. |
| `value` | A row's value control (whichever of `lr-input`/`lr-select`/`lr-date-input`/ `lr-combobox` applies, or an empty placeholder for a unary operator or an incomplete row). |

| Name | Description |

| `canvas` | The Chart.js canvas. |

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chart-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-280px)`) ŌĆö The host's `block-size`. Set on the host element from the `height` property on every change (custom properties only cascade downward, so it cannot be set from inside the shadow root); a host-level override is overwritten the next time `height` changes.
- `--lr-chart-grid-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Grid-line color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw (Chart.js paints to canvas and cannot consume `var()`).
- `--lr-chart-tick-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Axis tick-label color; also used for the `xLabel`/`yLabel`/`y2Label` axis-title text (there is no separate title-color token). Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-legend-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Legend label color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Tooltip background color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-text` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Tooltip text color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.

elements.name: lr-radio
elements.description:
`<lr-radio>` ŌĆö a form-associated single-choice control. Radios can be used
alone or inside `<lr-radio-group>`.

Deliberately no hint/error chrome of its own -- the default slot already carries real, visible
label text (see `@slot` below), so a labeled-field frame built around `label`/`hint`/`errorText`
props has nothing to add here. A consumer needing shared hint/error messaging for a set of
options composes it once on the owning `<lr-radio-group>` (which does carry `hint`/`errorText`),
the same way a native radio `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` pairs with one externally-owned error node
shared across all its `<input type="radio">` children rather than one per option.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Label text. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The interactive radio control. |
| `circle` | The circular radio indicator. |
| `dot` | The selected indicator. |
| `label` | The default slot wrapper. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-radio-label-indent` (default: `calc(min(var(--lr-icon-button-size), 1.75rem) + var(--lr-space-s))`) ŌĆö The inline distance from the control's start edge to the start of the label text, i.e. the circle's own floor plus the gap next to it. Published so a consumer composing per-option hint text under the label can align it without re-deriving that formula from the shadow styles, and used as the source of the real gap so the two cannot drift. Setting it on the element (or on `lr-radio` in your own stylesheet) moves the label; because custom properties inherit down and not sideways, it is *not* readable from a sibling node in your tree ŌĆö align a sibling by computing the same formula from `--lr-theme-icon-button-size` and `--lr-theme-space-s`, which you control.

elements.name: lr-random-content
elements.description:
`<lr-random-content>` ŌĆö displays a randomly (or sequentially) chosen
subset of its slotted children and hides the rest, for A/B copy testing,
testimonial/quote rotation, or varying marketing copy on each render or
interval without any custom JS beyond slotting the candidates.

Not a form-associated control and has no textual chrome of its own ŌĆö a
pure content-rotation primitive over caller-supplied children, so the
label/hint/error frame doesn't apply.

The host renders `display: block` by default, like the rest of this
family. A consumer needing an inline text-fragment swap inside a sentence
can override `lr-random-content { display: inline; }` from outside ŌĆö
that isn't baked in here, since `display: contents` on the host risks
accessibility-tree inconsistencies across engines.

There is no built-in trigger UI (no next/previous/shuffle button):
selection is driven only by `autoplay`/`autoplayInterval` and the public
`randomize()` method. Consequently there is no keyboard interaction and no
RTL-aware arrow-key handling to implement here ŌĆö that guarantee only
applies to components with roving/arrow-key navigation, which this one
structurally lacks.

`fade-left`/`fade-right` are physical-direction transforms (matching the
upstream naming this component mirrors), not "previous/next" navigational
semantics like a carousel chevron, so they are deliberately **not**
mirrored under `:host(:dir(rtl))`.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The pool of candidate children. Only direct element children are eligible. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The wrapping element around the default slot. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-random-content-animation-duration` (default: `300ms`) ŌĆö Duration of the entrance animation.
- `--lr-random-content-animation-easing` (default: `ease`) ŌĆö Easing function for the entrance animation.
- `--lr-random-content-animation-translate` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-5em)`) ŌĆö Translation distance for directional animations.

attributes.name: animation
attributes.description:
Entrance effect applied to a child the instant it becomes shown.

Type: `LyraRandomContentAnimation`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: autoplay
attributes.description:
Whether the displayed selection automatically re-rolls on an interval.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: autoplay-interval
attributes.description:
Milliseconds between autoplay ticks. Clamped to a 1000ms floor.

Type: `number`  
Default: `3000`

attributes.name: items
attributes.description:
How many children are shown simultaneously -- a count, not the pool itself. NaN/negative/
fractional/oversized all normalize through `finiteInteger`, clamped to at least 1 and at
most the pool size -- see `clampedCount()`.

Type: `number`  
Default: `1`

attributes.name: locale

Default: `''`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
Selection algorithm ŌĆö see `randomize()`.

Type: `LyraRandomContentMode`  
Default: `'unique'`

- `--lr-rating-empty-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Unfilled-star color.

elements.name: lr-reorder-item
elements.description:
`<lr-reorder-item>` ŌĆö one row inside `<lr-reorder-list>`. Renders arbitrary slotted content plus
move-up/move-down buttons. This item alone doesn't know whether it's first or last in the list,
so its boundary-disabled state (`atStart`/`atEnd`), list-level cascade (`listDisabled`), and
held-move state (`pending`) are all pushed down by the parent `<lr-reorder-list>` ŌĆö normally
set internally, not by consumers.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Arbitrary row content (a label, a mini-form, anything). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The row's root wrapper. |
| `content` | Wrapper around the default slot. |
| `move-down-button` | The move-down button. |
| `move-up-button` | The move-up button. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-reorder-item-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between the move buttons and content.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Disables this row's own move-up/move-down buttons without removing it or its slotted content
from the DOM. Does not gate the default slot's own content.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: pending
attributes.description:
Whether this item's move is currently held pending host resolution, after an `lr-reorder`
listener on the parent `<lr-reorder-list>` called `preventDefault()`. Purely informational ŌĆö
it does not itself disable this item's move buttons (the list already refuses to start any
further move while one is pending, so a second click here is already a no-op); style it via
`lr-reorder-item[pending]` if desired. Pushed down by `<lr-reorder-list>`; normally set
internally, not by consumers.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
Stable identifier included in the parent list's emitted `lr-reorder` order array. Falls back
to this item's live DOM-position index (stringified) when unset ŌĆö a consumer that cares about
stable identity across moves should set this explicitly, since the fallback string changes as
the item's position changes.

Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-reorder-list
elements.description:
`<lr-reorder-list>` ŌĆö a generic vertical list of `<lr-reorder-item>` rows, reorderable via
per-row move-up/move-down buttons (always available) or Ctrl/Cmd+ArrowUp/ArrowDown from focus
anywhere inside a row ŌĆö the same modifier convention `<lr-tree>`'s `reorderable` and
`<lr-dashboard-grid>`'s `cells-draggable` already establish.

Unlike `<lr-tree>`'s `reorderable` mode (a *controlled* request ŌĆö `data` is host-owned and
nothing moves until the host reassigns it), this list physically moves its own slotted
`<lr-reorder-item>` light-DOM nodes itself. That is a deliberate difference, not an
inconsistency: `<lr-tree>` re-derives its child elements from a `data` array prop on every
render, so a direct DOM move there would just be overwritten on the next render ŌĆö it must stay
controlled. This list has no such data-array prop; its children are plain author-authored
slotted content with nothing to reconcile against, so DOM order genuinely is the source of
truth (the same principle `<lr-tree>` relies on for its own children). The `lr-reorder` event
still tells the host the resulting order, so it can persist it without hand-rolling its own
splice/resort logic.

An `lr-reorder` listener can call `preventDefault()` to hold a move open while its own async
work (e.g. a network call persisting the new order) is in flight -- the same
cancelable-event-plus-host-resolvable-pending-state pattern `<lr-confirm-bar>` and
`<lr-tool-approval-dialog>` already establish for their own approve/deny decisions.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-reorder-item>` elements. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The list's root wrapper (`role="list"`). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-reorder-list-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between rows.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Disables every item's move-up/move-down buttons and the Ctrl/Cmd+Arrow shortcut, without
removing any item from the DOM or mutating any item's own `disabled` attribute.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the list, set as `aria-label` on the internal `role="list"` element. A
plain `aria-label` attribute on the host itself is honored as a fallback when this is left
unset, matching `<lr-control-group>`. Native lists don't require an accessible name, so this
has no forced fallback string when both are left unset.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-resize-observer
elements.description:
`<lr-resize-observer>` ŌĆö observes the first-party or consumer-owned
elements in its default slot and emits typed resize entries. The wrapper has
no visual layout of its own (`display: contents`).

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Elements to observe. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The non-layout wrapper around the observed slot. |

attributes.name: box
attributes.description:
Type: `ResizeObserverBox`  
Default: `'content-box'`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-responsive-panel
elements.description:
`<lr-responsive-panel>` ŌĆö the same slotted content either docked inline
in the page's normal layout flow (desktop) or presented as a full-screen/
bottom-sheet overlay (mobile), depending on viewport width. Typical uses:
a settings panel or a conversation-history sidebar that's a permanent
docked pane on a wide screen but a modal on a phone.

Breakpoint detection uses `matchMedia('(max-width: ' + mobileBreakpoint +
')')`, re-evaluated live while connected -- resizing/rotating a device
that crosses the breakpoint while `mode="auto"` (the default) updates the
effective presentation without unmounting or re-creating the slotted
content: inline and overlay presentation share the exact same shadow DOM
structure (only a css class, and the overlay-only `role`/`aria-modal`
attributes and backdrop element, differ), so lit-html's diffing keeps
`[part="body"]` and its `<slot>` as the same DOM node across the
transition -- scroll position and focus inside the slotted content survive
it for free. When an open inline panel becomes modal, focus already inside
is preserved and outside focus moves to the first available target. Closing
still returns to the opener captured by the original inline open.

The overlay presentation uses the library's shared overlay coordinator for
focus trapping, Escape/backdrop dismissal, inerting, and stack ordering,
while retaining this component's own responsive rendering and close event.

Accessible name (overlay presentation only -- the inline presentation has
no dialog semantics to name), in priority order: if the host element itself
has an `aria-label` attribute set, its value wins outright, overriding
every source below -- the standard ARIA convention for a consumer that
wants full control over the announced name, matching lr-dialog's
`accessibleLabel` pattern. Otherwise `label`, when set, is used verbatim.
When both are empty, this falls back to the `header` slot's content -- a
heading element (`h1`ŌĆō`h6` or `[role="heading"]`) among the slotted header
content wins if present, otherwise the header slot's full text content is
used, mirroring lr-dialog's `detectHeading()`/`headingText` fallback (see
dialog.ts's module doc for why this uses `aria-label`, a copied string,
rather than `aria-labelledby`: the header content is light DOM while
`[part="panel"]` lives in this element's shadow tree, and an ID-reference
attribute can't resolve across that boundary). A panel opened without a
host `aria-label`, `label`, or header content still renders `role="dialog"`
with no accessible name -- set one of those to avoid that.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The panel body. |
| `footer` | Optional footer content (e.g. action buttons), rendered below the body. |
| `header` | Optional header content, rendered above the body. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The full-viewport scrim behind the panel -- only rendered in the overlay presentation. |
| `base` | The root wrapper; `display: none` while closed, positioned `fixed` while open and in the overlay presentation. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the default slot. |
| `footer` | The wrapper around the `footer` slot. |
| `header` | The wrapper around the `header` slot. |
| `panel` | The panel surface itself (`role="dialog"` while open and in the overlay presentation). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-responsive-panel-overlay-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-overlay)`) ŌĆö The overlay presentation's scrim color, applied to `[part="backdrop"]`.
- `--lr-responsive-panel-sheet-max-block-size` (default: `85dvh`) ŌĆö Maximum height of the `variant="bottom-sheet"` overlay panel (falls back to `85vh` where `dvh` is unsupported).

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Host-level `aria-label` override for the overlay presentation's accessible name -- wins over
every other source (`label`, the header-slot fallback), matching `<lr-dialog>`'s
`accessibleLabel` pattern. See the class doc for the full precedence order. Set as a plain
`aria-label` attribute on `<lr-responsive-panel>` itself, not a public JS property.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the overlay presentation's `role="dialog"`. Unused in the inline
presentation, which has no dialog semantics to name. When empty, falls back to the `header`
slot's content -- see the class doc for the full fallback order.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mobile-breakpoint
attributes.description:
CSS length passed to `matchMedia` as `(max-width: <this>)` to decide, in `mode="auto"`,
whether the effective presentation is `'overlay'` (below/at this width) or `'inline'`
(above it).

Type: `string`  
Default: `'768px'`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
`'auto'` (default) tracks `mobile-breakpoint` live; `'inline'`/`'overlay'` force that
presentation regardless of viewport width.

Type: `ResponsivePanelMode`  
Default: `'auto'`

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Whether the panel is open. In the inline presentation this just means visible/mounted; in
the overlay presentation this is the actual modal open/closed state.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Only affects the overlay presentation's visual treatment -- `'fullscreen'` (default) covers
the whole viewport; `'bottom-sheet'` slides up from the bottom and doesn't cover the full
height.

Type: `ResponsivePanelVariant`  
Default: `'fullscreen'`

elements.name: lr-result-card
elements.description:
`<lr-result-card>` ŌĆö a small bordered card shell for a custom tool-result
renderer's body (see `<lr-tool-result-view>`'s `registerToolRenderer()`
in `../tool-result-view/registry.js`). Purely visual, with no state of its
own beyond slot-presence tracking: it gives every custom renderer the same
"small card" language (border, radius, optional title + header actions)
without each one hand-rolling its own box.

Pairs with `<lr-result-field>` for the label/value rows that typically
make up the body, though the default slot accepts any content ŌĆö nothing
here requires a `<lr-result-field>` specifically.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The card body ŌĆö typically one or more `<lr-result-field>` rows. |
| `actions` | Small header controls (e.g. a copy button), rendered alongside the title. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | The wrapper around the `actions` slot. `hidden` whenever the slot has no assigned content. |
| `base` | The outer bordered container. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the default slot. |
| `header` | The header row wrapping the title and the `actions` slot. Present in the DOM at all times (so a later `slotchange` on `actions` is still observed) but `hidden` whenever there is no `title` and no `actions` content ŌĆö an untitled, action-less card has no visible header bar at all. |
| `title` | The title text. Truncates with an ellipsis when it overflows; carries its own native `title` attribute (the full string) so hovering the truncated text reveals it via the browser's default tooltip, scoped to just this element rather than the whole card. The host's own `title` attribute is stripped once Lit has synced it into the `title` property (see `StripHostTitleAttribute` in `internal/strip-host-title.ts`), so the native tooltip never also covers the rest of the card. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-result-card-compact-header-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö `[part="header"]` block/inline padding while `compact`.
- `--lr-result-card-compact-body-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö `[part="body"]` padding while `compact`.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s/`lr-agent-run`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the
default) keeps the bordered, filled box. `'plain'` removes the border, background, and corner
radius, so a card nested inside a host frame that already draws a border (e.g.
`<lr-tool-result-view>`'s own chrome) doesn't double it. `plain` wins over `compact` when
both are set (nothing left to tighten).

Type: `ResultCardAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter header/body padding for dense contexts (a card rendered as a row in a transcript or
result list) -- same convention as `lr-agent-run`'s `compact`. Defaults to `false`, i.e. the
full card padding. Purely a density knob: the border and background stay, so use
`appearance="plain"` instead to drop the chrome entirely.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
Small heading for the card. Leave unset for an untitled card (e.g. a
bare block of `<lr-result-field>` rows with no natural heading).
Rendered into the truncating `[part="title"]` span, which also carries
this value as its own `title` attribute so the disclosure tooltip is
scoped to that element rather than the whole host -- a bare host-level
`title` attribute (the browser's global tooltip attribute) is actively
stripped once Lit has synced it into this property, so the card never
grows an unsolicited native tooltip repeating the same text. See
`StripHostTitleAttribute` (`internal/strip-host-title.ts`).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-result-field
elements.description:
`<lr-result-field>` ŌĆö a single label/value row, meant for use inside a
`<lr-result-card>` body (though it works standalone too). Renders as a
dense "label: value" line by default ŌĆö e.g. "Status: 200 OK" or
"Duration: 340ms" ŌĆö matching the compact, small-card presentation this
pair exists for.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Rich value content (e.g. a `<lr-chip>` status badge, or a plain text override), taking precedence over the `value` prop whenever it has any assigned content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The row container. |
| `label` | The label text (including its trailing colon). |
| `value` | The wrapper around the value ŌĆö either the slotted content or the plain `value` prop text. |

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
The field name, e.g. "Status". Leave unset to render a value with no label.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
Plain-text value, e.g. "200 OK". Ignored once the default slot carries
real content ŌĆö see the `@slot` doc.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-retrieval-compare
elements.description:
`<lr-retrieval-compare>` ŌĆö a side-by-side retrieval/reranking workbench that makes rank,
overlap, and dense/sparse/rerank/final score changes inspectable. It never performs retrieval.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The named comparison region. |
| `chunk` | A selectable chunk row. |
| `chunk-rank` | The effective rank. |
| `chunk-selected` | The selected chunk row. |
| `chunk-text` | Retrieved text. |
| `chunk-title` | The source title. |
| `chunks` | One ranked list. |
| `empty` | The empty state. |
| `overlap` | The pairwise top-k Jaccard overlap summary. |
| `score` | One named score. |
| `scores` | Score-breakdown list. |
| `set` | One result-set column. |
| `set-heading` | A caller-supplied result-set label. |
| `sets` | Horizontally scrollable set grid. |

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: selected-chunk-id
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: top-k
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `10`

elements.name: lr-retrieval-results
elements.description:
`<lr-retrieval-results>` ŌĆö the orchestration-level ranked-chunk-list surface: takes raw
`RetrievalChunk[]` (the shared retrieval-and-grounding type from `src/ai/types.ts`) and adds
everything a single retrieval call's result set needs beyond what one chunk's own rendering
provides -- deduplication, optional grouping by source, multi-selection, pagination/infinite
loading, and a compact/expanded presentation switch -- while composing existing primitives for
every part that already has one, never re-implementing chunk/score/source rendering itself.

**Composition, not reinvention.** Each rendered row wraps exactly one chunk in an internal
`<lr-chunk-inspector>` (fed a single-element `chunks` array), reusing its score bar/tier
coloring, title+page rendering, expandable text, and `compact` mode verbatim -- this component
never hand-rolls chunk-card markup. `metadata` (arbitrary `Record<string, unknown>`, which no
existing primitive renders) is the one genuinely new bit of presentation here, shown as a plain
key/value list in `expanded` presentation only. Large result sets are windowed through an
internal `<lr-virtual-list>`, exactly like `<lr-thread-list>`'s own data-mode rendering -- each
row's rendered content therefore lives inside `<lr-virtual-list>`'s own shadow root, not this
component's, whenever virtualization is active (see that component's own doc for why).

**Controlled component.** `chunks`/`selectedIds`/`loading`/`error`/`hasMore` are all host-owned;
this component never fetches, retries, or mutates its own copy of `chunks`. Selecting a row
updates `selectedIds` locally *then* emits `lr-select` (the same "update own copy, then emit;
reassign to control" convention `<lr-source-picker>` already uses) so a host can either accept
the update as-is or override it before the next render.

**Deduplication** keeps, per duplicate `id`, whichever chunk has the higher `score` -- set
`dedupe="false"` to see every raw entry `chunks` contains, duplicates included. **Grouping**
(`grouping="source"`) buckets the deduplicated, score-sorted list by `source.id`, each bucket
ordered by its own best-scoring chunk first, and always renders through the internal
`<lr-virtual-list>` (regardless of `virtualize-at`) so group headers have a single rendering path
ŌĆö `<lr-thread-list>`'s own date-bucket grouping takes the identical approach.

**Pagination.** While virtualized, `has-more`/`loading` are forwarded straight to the internal
`<lr-virtual-list>`, which fires `lr-load-more` itself on scroll-near-bottom (re-emitted here
unchanged). Below the virtualization threshold (a short, non-grouped list), scrolling near the
bottom isn't a meaningful gesture, so a `[part="load-more"]` button takes its place instead,
showing a spinner in place of the button while `loading` is true.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `chunk` | The per-row `<lr-chunk-inspector>`'s own `chunk` row. |
| `chunk-current` | The row `<lr-chunk-inspector>`'s current-chunk state part. |
| `chunk-open-button` | The row chunk's title/open `<button>`. |
| `chunk-score` | The row chunk's percent-score line. |
| `chunk-score-bar` | The row chunk's score bar track. |
| `chunk-score-current` | The current row chunk's score line. |
| `chunk-score-fill` | The row chunk's score bar fill. |
| `chunk-score-fill-danger` | The row chunk's score fill in the low-score tier. |
| `chunk-score-fill-success` | The row chunk's score fill in the high-score tier. |
| `chunk-score-fill-warning` | The row chunk's score fill in the medium-score tier. |
| `chunk-text` | The row chunk's text preview (`presentation="expanded"` only). |
| `chunk-text-clamped` | The row chunk's text preview while still collapsed. |
| `chunk-title` | The row chunk's visible title text. |
| `chunk-toggle` | The row chunk's "Show more"/"Show less" button. |
| `empty` | The `<lr-empty>` wrapper, shown when `chunks` is empty and neither `error` nor `loading` is set. |
| `error` | The error message region (`role="alert"`), shown while `error` is non-empty. |
| `group-header` | Exported from the internal `<lr-virtual-list>`'s `group` part ŌĆö grouped/virtualized mode only. |
| `load-more` | The "Load more" button itself (non-virtualized mode, `loading` false). |
| `load-more-row` | The wrapper around the non-virtualized-mode pagination footer. |
| `metadata` | The `<dl>` of a chunk's `metadata` entries ŌĆö omitted entirely when a chunk has no `metadata`, or while `presentation="compact"`. |
| `metadata-entry` | One metadata key/value pair's wrapper. |
| `metadata-term` | The `<dt>` carrying a metadata key. Named separately because `::part()` matches one element and cannot be followed into its subtree. |
| `metadata-value` | The `<dd>` carrying a metadata value. |
| `row` | One result row's wrapper. Below the virtualization threshold this is a plain, directly-styleable element in this component's own shadow root; while virtualized it is exported from the internal `<lr-virtual-list>`'s own `row` part instead (`::part(row)` still reaches it either way). |
| `row-body` | The wrapper around a row's `<lr-chunk-inspector>` plus its optional metadata list; carries `data-selected` while that row is selected. |
| `row-body-selected` | Additional part on a selected `row-body`. State is exposed as a second part name because Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()` ŌĆö `::part(row-body)[data-selected]` is invalid CSS, and while virtualized `::part()` is the only way in. A state part is a second token in the same `part` attribute, so a `[part~="ŌĆ”"]` (not `[part="ŌĆ”"]`) selector is the one that matches inside a tree. |
| `select` | The per-row `<lr-checkbox>`, omitted entirely when `selectable` is false. |
| `spinner` | The initial-load `<lr-spinner>`, shown while `loading` is true and `chunks` is still empty. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-retrieval-results-selected-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Inline-start border color marking a selected `[part="row-body"]`. A border rather than a fill by design (see the styles file), so recoloring it carries no contrast risk for the row's own text.

attributes.name: active-id
attributes.description:
Marks the chunk currently open in a viewer -- forwarded to each per-row `<lr-chunk-inspector>`
and to the internal `<lr-virtual-list>` (which scrolls the matching row into view).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: dedupe
attributes.description:
Drops duplicate `id`s before rendering, keeping whichever duplicate has the higher `score`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: error
attributes.description:
Non-empty replaces the entire result view with a `role="alert"` error message -- caller-
supplied text, not routed through `localize()` (the same stance `<lr-document-preview>`'s own
`error-message` takes for the same reason: this is app/network data, not library copy).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: grouping
attributes.description:
`'source'` buckets rows under a header per `source.id` (always rendered through the internal
`<lr-virtual-list>`, regardless of `virtualize-at`); `'none'` (default) is a flat ranked list.

Type: `RetrievalResultsGrouping`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: has-more
attributes.description:
While virtualized, forwarded to the internal `<lr-virtual-list>` so scrolling near the bottom
fires `lr-load-more`; otherwise shows the built-in `[part="load-more"]` footer.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the results region. Defaults to the localized `chunkInspectorLabel`
("Retrieved chunks") -- reused rather than a new key, since it already says exactly what this
region is.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: presentation
attributes.description:
`'expanded'` (default) shows each chunk's full `<lr-chunk-inspector>` row (score bar, text
preview with its own expand toggle) plus any `metadata`; `'compact'` shows title + score bar
only, on both.

Type: `RetrievalResultsPresentation`  
Default: `'expanded'`

attributes.name: selectable
attributes.description:
Shows a per-row `<lr-checkbox>`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: sort
attributes.description:
`'score'` (default) sorts the deduplicated list descending by `score`; `'none'` preserves
`chunks`' own given order.

Type: `'score' | 'none'`  
Default: `'score'`

attributes.name: virtualize-at
attributes.description:
Above this many rows (after dedup, before grouping), rendering switches from a plain list to
the internal `<lr-virtual-list>`. Grouped mode always virtualizes regardless of this value.

Type: `number`  
Default: `50`

elements.name: lr-retrieval-search
elements.description:
`<lr-retrieval-search>` -- the query bar for a retrieval/RAG surface: query text, an active-
filter/scope chip row, a vector/keyword/hybrid mode selector, and loading/error/empty status
feedback. Consumes `RetrievalQuery` (`src/ai/types.ts`) as the shape emitted on submit.

Fully controlled, like every other Lyra input: `query`/`mode`/`filters`/`scope` are host-owned
properties. This component never performs retrieval itself -- it only emits `lr-search`; the
host owns the actual fetch and toggles `loading` around it. Because this component has no way
to know when a request resolves (only `loading`, set from outside), submitting again (Enter, or
clicking the button) while `loading` is already `true` is treated as **superseding** the
in-flight request: `lr-cancel` fires immediately before the new `lr-search`. The submit button
itself doubles as an explicit Cancel affordance while `loading` -- clicking it only emits
`lr-cancel`, without resubmitting, the same "just stop" action `<lr-chat-composer>`'s Stop
button offers for its own `stoppable` busy state.

Composes `<lr-input type="search">` for the query field, `<lr-segmented>` for the mode
selector (the same small-closed-set-choice-in-a-toolbar role it already fills, sized to sit
flush beside `<lr-input>`), `<lr-chip>`/`<lr-chip-group>` for removable active-filter/scope
chips, `<lr-spinner>` for the loading state, and `<lr-empty>` (compact) for the empty state.
`filters`/`scope` chip removal updates this component's own copy first, then emits
`lr-filters-change` with the complete next state -- the same "update, then emit; reassign to
control" round-trip `<lr-source-picker>`'s `selectedIds` already establishes. `empty` is a
host-driven flag (the last completed search returned zero results); this component holds no
results data of its own -- see `<lr-retrieval-results>` for rendering the actual chunk list.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The `role="search"` root landmark. |
| `empty` | The compact `<lr-empty>`, shown only when `empty` is `true` and neither `loading` nor `errorText` is set. |
| `error` | The `role="alert"` error message, shown only when `errorText` is non-empty and not `loading`. |
| `filters` | The active-filter/scope `<lr-chip-group>`. Omitted entirely when both `filters` and `scope` are empty. |
| `mode` | The vector/keyword/hybrid `<lr-segmented>`. |
| `query` | The query `<lr-input type="search">`. |
| `row` | The row holding the query field, mode selector, and submit/cancel button. |
| `spinner` | The busy `<lr-spinner>`, shown only while `loading`. |
| `submit` | The submit/cancel `<button>`. Reads "Search" while idle, "Cancel" while `loading`. |

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Overrides the computed accessible name for the `role="search"` landmark. Wins over `label`
and the localized default. Attribute-reflects from a host-level `aria-label`.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: empty
attributes.description:
Host-driven flag: the last completed search returned zero results. Renders a compact
`<lr-empty>` beneath the search row. Never inferred by this component itself -- see the
class doc; it holds no results data of its own.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: error-text
attributes.description:
Host-supplied error message from the last failed search, shown verbatim (caller-owned text,
not localized) in a `role="alert"` region. Empty string (the default) shows nothing.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the `role="search"` landmark. Falls back to the localized default; a
host `aria-label` (via `accessibleLabel`) wins over both.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
Host-driven busy flag. This component never performs retrieval itself and has no way to know
when a request resolves, so the host toggles this explicitly around its own fetch -- see the
class doc for the resulting submit/cancel/supersede behavior.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
Retrieval mode. Defaults to `'hybrid'`, the common default for a search bar combining both
vector and keyword retrieval.

Type: `LyraRetrievalMode`  
Default: `'hybrid'`

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description:
Placeholder for the query field. Empty string (the default) falls back to the localized
generic "Search" placeholder, which also becomes that field's accessible name (mirroring
`<lr-input>`'s own placeholder-as-label fallback).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: query
attributes.description:
The current query text. Controlled -- the internal `lr-input` updates this optimistically as
the user types (mirroring every other Lyra input's controlled-value convention), and a host
reassignment always wins.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-retrieval-trace
elements.description:
`<lr-retrieval-trace>` ŌĆö a retrieval pipeline's stage timeline (query rewriting, embedding,
retrieval, reranking, filtering), rendered through `<lr-span-waterfall>`'s existing
time-scaled bar rendering, plus a disclosure list below it exposing each stage's evidence:
free-form text, retrieved/reranked/filtered chunks via `<lr-chunk-inspector>`, and/or arbitrary
stage metadata. Never fetches, ranks, or computes retrieval results itself.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `evidence-body` | A stage's evidence content wrapper, hidden while collapsed. |
| `evidence-list` | The wrapper around every stage's evidence disclosure row. Omitted when no stage has evidence. |
| `evidence-metadata` | A stage's key/value metadata list (a `<dl>`). |
| `evidence-metadata-key` | One metadata entry's key (a `<dt>`). |
| `evidence-metadata-row` | One metadata entry's `<dt>`/`<dd>` pair wrapper, inside `evidence-metadata`. |
| `evidence-metadata-value` | One metadata entry's value (a `<dd>`). |
| `evidence-row` | One stage's evidence disclosure row. Omitted for a stage with no evidence. |
| `evidence-text` | A stage's free-form text evidence. |
| `evidence-toggle` | A stage's evidence disclosure `<button>`. |
| `evidence-toggle-icon` | The disclosure button's chevron glyph. |
| `timeline` | The internal `<lr-span-waterfall>` element. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-retrieval-trace-active-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of the `[part="evidence-row"]` whose stage matches `activeStageId`.

attributes.name: active-stage-id
attributes.description:
Controlled selection, forwarded verbatim to the internal `<lr-span-waterfall>`'s `activeSpanId`.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the internal timeline. Falls back to a host `aria-label`, then the timeline's own localized default.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-rubric-form
elements.description:
`<lr-rubric-form>` ŌĆö a configurable annotation rubric (LangSmith
annotation-queue style): score, category, and freeform-comment keys with
a submit-and-next flow for working through an eval queue.

Each `RubricKey.type` routes to an existing sibling control: `score`
renders `<lr-segmented>` when its `[min, max]`/`step` domain has 10 or
fewer integer steps, or `<lr-slider>` otherwise; `category` renders
`<lr-select>` (single) or `<lr-checkbox-group>` (`multiple`); `comment`
renders `<lr-textarea>`. A key whose `type` is none of the three renders
a visible "Unsupported field type" note instead of silently dropping it,
and marks the form invalid ŌĆö the same defensive shape as
`<lr-tool-param-form>`'s own unsupported-property fallback.

Optional native `<form>` participation is implemented via `ElementInternals`
attached directly (this component's value is a whole object, not a plain
string, so the `FormAssociated` string-value mixin doesn't fit) ŌĆö the same
shape `<lr-tool-param-form>` uses. This is a nice-to-have layered on top
of the primary integration contract (`value` +
`lr-input`/`lr-validity-change`/`lr-submit`/`lr-skip`), not a
requirement: a consumer that never puts this inside a `<form>` loses
nothing.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | Extra host controls rendered in the footer beside Submit/Skip. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer wrapper around all fields. |
| `description` | A field's helper text. |
| `empty` | The message shown when `keys` has no entries. |
| `error` | A field-level validation message. |
| `field` | One key's wrapper (label + control + description + error). |
| `footer` | The row containing the actions slot and Submit/Skip buttons. |
| `label` | A field's label. |
| `scale` | The rendered score/category/comment control's wrapper. |
| `skip` | The Skip button (only rendered when `skippable`). |
| `submit` | The Submit button. |
| `unsupported` | The fallback note for a key whose `type` is outside the three supported ones. |

attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: has-next
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: item-id
attributes.description: Type: `string`

attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`

elements.name: lr-scatter-chart
elements.description:
`<lr-scatter-chart>` ŌĆö `<lr-chart>` with `type` locked to `"scatter"`. Feed
points via `Series.points`.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `center` | Optional overlay content positioned at the chart area's center. Useful for doughnut and pie totals. |
| `data-table` | An optional consumer-provided accessible table alternative. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The chart wrapper. |
| `canvas` | The Chart.js canvas. |
| `center` | The chart-area-centered overlay wrapper for the `center` slot. |
| `data-table` | The optional generated or slotted data table. |
| `description` | The accessible chart summary. |
| `error` | `role="alert"` message shown instead of `canvas` when the optional `chart.js` peer dependency is not installed. |
| `reset-zoom-button` | The reset-zoom control when zoom is active. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-chart-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-280px)`) ŌĆö The host's `block-size`. Set on the host element from the `height` property on every change (custom properties only cascade downward, so it cannot be set from inside the shadow root); a host-level override is overwritten the next time `height` changes.
- `--lr-chart-grid-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö Grid-line color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw (Chart.js paints to canvas and cannot consume `var()`).
- `--lr-chart-tick-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö Axis tick-label color; also used for the `xLabel`/`yLabel`/`y2Label` axis-title text (there is no separate title-color token). Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-legend-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Legend label color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Tooltip background color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.
- `--lr-chart-tooltip-text` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Tooltip text color. Resolved via `getComputedStyle` on every draw.

attributes.name: accessible-description

Accessible description for the canvas. When unset, a concise data/trend summary is generated.

attributes.name: accessible-label

`chartjs-plugin-datalabels` peer. Unset (the default) leaves labels off for
this and every other chart on the page ŌĆö the plugin registers globally, so
`buildConfig()` explicitly disables it per-chart until this is set. The
screen-reader equivalent is the always-present data table (see
`show-data-table`); labels are a purely visual, canvas-only addition.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: height
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `'280px'`

Sets `options.indexAxis = 'y'`, Chart.js's own mechanism for horizontal bars (also applies to line/area types).

elements.name: lr-scroller
elements.description:
`<lr-scroller>` ŌĆö a responsive overflow surface with optional previous
and next controls. Content remains in the default slot, so cards, tabs, and
any consumer-owned interactive elements retain their own semantics.

| --- | --- |
| (default) | Scrollable content. |

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |

| `base` | The overall scroller layout. |
| `content` | The slotted content wrapper. |
| `control` | Shared part on both `previous` and `next`. |
| `next` | The next/end control. |
| `next-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `next`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `previous` | The previous/start control. |
| `previous-glyph` | The chevron glyph inside `previous`, mirrored under RTL. |
| `viewport` | The native scroll container. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-scroller-control-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-2rem)`) ŌĆö Control size.
- `--lr-scroller-min-block-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-10rem)`) ŌĆö Minimum vertical scroller size.

attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`
