elements.name: lr-segmented
elements.description:
`<lr-segmented>` ŌĆö a single-select button row with the WAI-ARIA APG `radiogroup` contract
built in: `role="radiogroup"`/`role="radio"`, roving tabindex, automatic activation (click or
arrow-key move both select immediately, like a native radio group), cyclic Arrow/Home/End
navigation among non-disabled items. First-party invention --
"choose exactly one of N labeled options, rendered as a button row" is ubiquitous
settings/filter-panel UI. Supports the same `2xs`-`xl` compact-form-control `size` scale as
`<lr-select>`/`<lr-combobox>`/`<lr-input>`, so it can sit flush beside those controls in a
toolbar at a matching height.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The `role="radiogroup"` root. |
| `segment` | A single `role="radio"` button. |
| `segment-icon` | Optional leading visual supplied by the item's `icon` field; content may have a natural aspect ratio and is not restricted to a square icon. |
| `segment-label` | The segment's label text. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-scroll-fade-size` (default: `2rem`) ŌĆö Width of the static fade at each horizontal scroll edge.
- `--lr-segmented-track-min-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-2-5rem)`) ŌĆö Minimum height of the `base` track. Re-set per `size` (`2xs` through `xl`); the `2.5rem` (40px) default applies at the unset/`m` size, matching `<lr-input>`/`<lr-select>`/`<lr-combobox>`'s own shared default-tier floor. Because it is declared on `:host` per tier, override it on the element itself, not on an ancestor.
- `--lr-segmented-track-height` ŌĆö Exact height of the `base` track, pinning it at every `size` tier (sets both `block-size` and `min-block-size`) so the row can sit flush beside a hard-sized toolbar control. **Genuinely unset by default** ŌĆö while unset each tier keeps its own `--lr-segmented-track-min-height` floor and the track grows with its content.
- `--lr-segmented-selected-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Background of the checked segment. Scoped to `[aria-checked='true']` only, so it never repaints a hovered unselected segment (which is what hijacking `--lr-color-surface` library-wide used to do).
- `--lr-segmented-selected-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color of the checked segment.
- `--lr-segmented-selected-font-weight` (default: `var(--lr-font-weight-semibold)`) ŌĆö Font weight of the checked segment.
- `--lr-segmented-selected-shadow` (default: `var(--lr-shadow)`) ŌĆö Box shadow lifting the checked segment off the track.
- `--lr-segmented-hover-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color of a hovered segment that is neither checked nor disabled. Independent of the selected-state props above ŌĆö recoloring the checked pill leaves this untouched.
- `--lr-segmented-segment-padding` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-125rem) var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö Each segment's padding. Re-set per `size`; this default applies at the unset/`m` size.
- `--lr-segmented-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-sm)`) ŌĆö Each segment's font size. Re-set per `size`; this default applies at the unset/`m` size.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the radiogroup, used when no visible label context
exists around it (e.g. no wrapping `<label>` or adjacent heading). Set
as `aria-label` on the `role="radiogroup"` element. A plain `aria-label`
attribute on the host itself is honored as a fallback when this is left
unset, matching `<lr-slider>`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size ŌĆö same `2xs`-`xl` scale as `<lr-select>`/`<lr-combobox>` (`s` through `xl`)
and `<lr-input>` (`2xs`). Reflects as the `size` attribute. The default `m` tier is
identical to this component's pre-`size` rendering, so leaving it unset never changes
output.

Type: `LyraSegmentedSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The currently selected item's `value`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-select
elements.description:
`<lr-select>` ŌĆö a plain closed-list dropdown: a direct `<lr-*>`
counterpart to `<wa-select>`/`<wa-option>`. Trigger is a button (not a text
input) -- click/Enter/Space/ArrowDown opens it, there's no typing-to-filter.
A printable keypress instead jumps (or, while closed, directly selects) the
next option whose label starts with what's been typed, like a native
`<select>`'s type-ahead.

Options are `<lr-option value>` children, the same element `<lr-combobox>`
uses. Unlike `lr-combobox` this is single-select only, with no filter/
source/with-clear/max-options-visible/empty-text/max-render/multiple surface
-- see `<lr-combobox>` for the filterable/multi-select case.

Reuses `lr-combobox`'s popup positioning (`internal/positioner.js`) and
click-outside/Escape/Home/End/Arrow-key listbox navigation patterns,
adapted to a trigger button that keeps DOM focus throughout (the listbox's
"active" row is conveyed via `aria-activedescendant`, never actual focus),
matching the WAI-ARIA "select-only combobox" pattern.

When `autoCommitSingleOption` is set and exactly one option is enabled
(regardless of how many disabled ones exist alongside it), the popup never
opens at all: a click, Enter, Space, ArrowDown, or ArrowUp on the trigger
commits that sole option directly, and the trigger renders as a plain
`role="button"` with no chevron/`aria-haspopup`/`aria-expanded` rather than
a combobox with a permanently inert popup state ŌĆö opening a one-row list to
pick the only available choice is pure friction with no real decision
behind it. This never changes `value`/validity defaults on its own ŌĆö an
unselected single-option select stays unselected (and a `required` one
stays invalid) exactly like the multi-option case, until the trigger is
actually activated. `autoCommitSingleOption` defaults to `false`: by
default a select always renders the normal combobox/listbox/chevron
trigger no matter how many options are enabled, matching pre-1.3.0
behavior ŌĆö opt in explicitly if a narrowing-to-one option list should
auto-commit.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-option>` elements. |
| `end` | Adornment after the selected-value label and before the expand icon. |
| `error` | Custom error content. |
| `hint` | Custom hint content. |
| `label` | Custom label content. |
| `start` | Adornment at the inline-start of the trigger row, before the selected-value label. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `end` | Wrapper around the `end` adornment slot; `hidden` while nothing is slotted. |
| `error` | The error message. |
| `expand-icon` | The dropdown indicator. |
| `form-control` | The outer wrapper around label, trigger, listbox, error and hint. |
| `form-control-label` | The `<label>` element. |
| `group-label` | An option group's heading row (shown when any option declares a `group`). |
| `hint` | The hint message. |
| `listbox` | The options popover. |
| `option` | An option row. |
| `option-dot` | An option row's leading status dot (when `dot-color` is set). |
| `option-label` | An option row's label/sub wrapper. |
| `option-sub` | An option row's secondary line (when `sub` is set). |
| `start` | Wrapper around the `start` adornment slot; `hidden` while nothing is slotted. |
| `trigger` | The trigger button (positioning anchor). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-select-expand-size` ŌĆö Decorative expand-icon box size, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-select-gap` ŌĆö Gap between the trigger's start adornment, label, end adornment, and expand icon. Doesn't vary by `size`.
- `--lr-select-radius` ŌĆö Trigger corner radius. Doesn't vary by `size`.
- `--lr-select-trigger-padding` ŌĆö Trigger padding shorthand, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-select-trigger-min-height` ŌĆö Trigger block-size floor, scaled by `size`, and live at every tier including the default `m` (`2.5rem`, matching `<lr-input>`/`<lr-combobox>` at that tier).
- `--lr-select-font-size` ŌĆö Trigger font size, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-select-option-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the hovered/keyboard-active option row. Not declared on `:host`, so a value set on any ancestor is never shadowed -- retheme just this row state without hijacking the shared `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token used by every other component's own hover/active state.
- `--lr-select-option-selected-bg` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Background of the currently-selected option row. Not declared on `:host`; retheme just the selected row without hijacking `--lr-color-brand`.
- `--lr-select-option-selected-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of the selected option row.
- `--lr-select-option-selected-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Text color of the selected option row.
- `--lr-select-option-selected-font-weight` (default: `var(--lr-font-weight-semibold)`) ŌĆö Font weight of the selected option row.
- `--lr-select-trigger-height` ŌĆö Exact trigger height. Unset by default, which leaves `--lr-select-trigger-min-height` as a floor only; set it to a length to both floor and cap the trigger (e.g. to pixel-match a sibling field in the same toolbar row). Because it is never declared by the component itself, it can be set from an ancestor or an outer-tree rule as well as inline on the element.

attributes.name: auto-commit-single-option
attributes.description:
Opt-in: when `true` and exactly one `<lr-option>` is enabled, the
trigger commits that option directly (click/Enter/Space/ArrowDown/
ArrowUp) instead of opening the listbox, and renders as a plain
`role="button"` with no chevron. Defaults to `false`, which always
renders the normal combobox/listbox/chevron trigger regardless of how
many options are enabled ŌĆö the pre-1.3.0 behavior. See the class doc
above and `onlyOption` below for the full rationale.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-selection-toolbar

`<lr-selection-toolbar>` ŌĆö a nonmodal action toolbar positioned above selected text. It carries
the selected text and a format-neutral document anchor into ask, quote, cite, or copy actions.

---

| `action` | Every action button. |
| `action-ask` | The ask action. |
| `action-cite` | The cite action. |
| `action-copy` | The copy action. |
| `action-quote` | The quote action. |
| `toolbar` | The floating `role="toolbar"` surface. |

attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

elements.name: lr-sequence-strip
elements.description:
`<lr-sequence-strip>` ŌĆö a compact, one-thin-cell-per-item strip visualizing a sequence of
categorical states, with an optional secondary per-cell marker. Pure CSS/flex, no chart.js/SVG/
canvas ŌĆö sized/named consistently with the sparkline/heatmap family, but a glanceable aggregate
visualization (`role="img"`, one summarizing `aria-label`), not a `role="list"` of
separately-operable items: no per-cell keyboard focus, no per-cell click event, matching
`<lr-sparkline>`'s accessibility model rather than `<lr-heatmap>`'s heavier canvas +
keyboard-roving one.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root strip wrapper (`role="img"`). |
| `cell` | Each item's cell, background-colored by its category. |
| `legend` | The static category key rendered below the strip when `showLegend` is set (`aria-hidden` ŌĆö it repeats the strip's own `aria-label` visually). |
| `legend-item` | One swatch + label pair in the legend, one per `categories` entry (plus one trailing marker row when `markerLabel` is set). |
| `legend-label` | The text of a legend item (the category's `label`, or its `key`). |
| `legend-marker-swatch` | The chip of the `markerLabel` legend row: a neutral chip carrying the same bottom bar a `marker: true` cell paints, in the same `--lr-sequence-strip-marker-color`. |
| `legend-swatch` | The color chip of a legend item, matching that category's cell color. |
| `marker` | The small bottom marker on a cell whose item sets `marker: true`. |
| `tooltip` | The hover tooltip showing the hovered item's label. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-sequence-strip-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-5rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the strip.
- `--lr-sequence-strip-marker-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Color of the bottom marker on a `marker: true` cell, and of the marker legend row's bar.
- `--lr-sequence-strip-legend-swatch-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-625rem)`) ŌĆö Inline and block size of a legend swatch (category and marker rows alike).
- `--lr-sequence-strip-legend-marker-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface-raised)`) ŌĆö Neutral chip background behind the marker legend row's bar; it stands in for "any cell", so it deliberately matches no category color.

attributes.name: accessible-label
attributes.description:
Overrides the auto-generated `aria-label` (a per-category "label: count" summary). Unset
computes the summary from `items`/`categories`.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: marker-label
attributes.description:
Names what a cell's `marker` means (e.g. `"Subagent"`). Set it to key the marker in the legend
ŌĆö with `showLegend` on it adds one trailing `[part="legend-item"]` whose swatch reproduces the
cell's own marker treatment ŌĆö and to have the marker counted in the auto-generated summary,
which is otherwise per-category only. Unset (the default) nothing changes: no extra legend row
and no extra summary clause.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Only `'horizontal'` (the default) is supported today ŌĆö vertical is plausible future scope, not
built speculatively without a motivating case.

Type: `'horizontal'`  
Default: `'horizontal'`

attributes.name: show-legend
attributes.description:
Renders a static `[part="legend"]` key of every `categories` entry below the strip, so the
color-to-category mapping is readable without hovering each cell. Deliberately
non-interactive: unlike `<lr-graph-legend>` this toggles nothing and emits nothing ŌĆö the
strip is a presentational aggregate, and the key describes the scheme, not the current data
(a category with no matching item still gets a row).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-skeleton
elements.description:
`<lr-skeleton>` ŌĆö a loading placeholder. First-party invention, standing
in for the bespoke `animate-pulse` div most dashboards hand-roll.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The placeholder shape. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-transition-ambient` (default: `1.8s ease-in-out`) ŌĆö Animation duration and timing function shared by the pulse and sheen effects.
- `--lr-skeleton-w` (default: `100%`) ŌĆö Inline size of the placeholder.
- `--lr-skeleton-h` (default: `var(--lr-size-1em)`) ŌĆö Block size of the placeholder.

attributes.name: announce
attributes.description:
Whether this placeholder exposes a localized status announcement. Disable for decorative
members of a group whose loading state is announced once by a parent or sibling.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: effect
attributes.description:
Type: `SkeletonEffect`  
Default: `'pulse'`

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name announced via `role="status"`. Override with a
description of what is actually loading (e.g. "Loading chart").

Type: `string`  
Default: `'LoadingŌĆ”'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Type: `SkeletonVariant`  
Default: `'text'`

attributes.name: width
attributes.description: Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-slider
elements.description:
`<lr-slider>` ŌĆö a numeric range control (e.g. an LLM "temperature"
setting), form-associated. Mirrors native `<input type="range">`
semantics: `value` is the string form-submitted via `FormAssociated`,
`valueAsNumber` is the ergonomic numeric accessor (mirroring the native
`<input type=range>` IDL attribute of the same name) kept in sync with it
in both directions ŌĆö reads parse `value`, writes stringify back to it.

Unlike the mixin's other consumers, an unset `value` is eagerly defaulted
ŌĆö on connect, and again after `form.reset()` ŌĆö to the midpoint of
`[min, max]` snapped to `step`, the same "range sanitization algorithm"
default a native range input applies. A slider always represents *some*
number, so `required` (inherited from `FormAssociated`) only has a window
to block submission before that default lands, matching how `required`
isn't a meaningful constraint on a native range input either.

Clicking anywhere on the track (not just the 16px thumb) jumps the thumb
to that point and continues the same gesture as a drag, matching native
`<input type=range>` click-to-seek.

Deliberately no label/hint/error chrome -- `label` here is an accessible-name override, not
visible label text; a labeled-field consumer wraps this element in their own layout.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The row wrapping the track and the optional value readout. |
| `fill` | The filled portion of the track from `min` up to the current value. |
| `thumb` | The draggable handle (`role="slider"`). |
| `track` | The full-width background line. |
| `value` | The visible numeric readout, rendered when `show-value` is true. |

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the slider, used when no visible label context
exists around it (e.g. no wrapping `<label>` or adjacent heading). Set
as `aria-label` on the interactive `role="slider"` element. A plain
`aria-label` attribute on the host itself is honored as a fallback when
this is left unset, matching `<lr-checkbox>`/`<lr-switch>`; with
neither, the localized generic `sliderLabel` message applies so the
focusable thumb is never nameless (the same pattern as
`<lr-input>`/`<lr-textarea>`'s built-in generic labels).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max
attributes.description: Type: `number`

attributes.name: min
attributes.description: Type: `number`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Type: `string`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: show-value
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: step
attributes.description: Type: `number`

elements.name: lr-source-card
elements.description:
`<lr-source-card>` ŌĆö one citation/source entry, meant to be a direct
light-DOM child of `<lr-source-list>` (though it renders and functions
fine standalone). Shows a title/page heading, an always-visible `excerpt`
slot, and an optional `full` slot revealed behind its own independent
"Show more" toggle ŌĆö unrelated to the parent `<lr-source-list>`'s own
expand/collapse, which only ever hides/shows the *set* of cards, never a
single card's own content.

`source-id` is this card's stable identity, meant to match a
`<lr-citation-badge>` (a sibling component) elsewhere on the page. This
component doesn't implement any scroll-to/highlight behavior itself ŌĆö that
lives at the app level, wiring a citation badge's activation event to this
card's `id`/`source-id`. See the `@example` below.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `excerpt` | A short preview. When left empty, the `excerpt` part collapses away entirely rather than leaving an empty gap in the card. |
| `full` | The complete source text/chunk, hidden behind the "Show more"/"Show less" toggle. When left empty, no toggle renders at all ŌĆö a card with no `full` content simply has no expand affordance. Removing all `full`-slotted content while expanded automatically collapses it back. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `excerpt` | The wrapper around the `excerpt` slot, `hidden` when the slot has no assigned content. |
| `full` | The wrapper around the `full` slot, `hidden` while collapsed. |
| `title` | The clickable title/page heading (`<button>`). |
| `toggle` | The "Show more"/"Show less" button. Only rendered when the `full` slot has content. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-source-card-compact-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö `[part="base"]` padding while `compact`.
- `--lr-source-card-compact-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between `[part="base"]`'s rows while `compact`.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled, padded box. `'plain'` removes the border, background, padding and corner
radius, so a card inside a `<lr-source-list>` (or any container already drawing its own
border/dividers) doesn't double the frame. `plain` wins over `compact` when both are set
(nothing left to tighten); the title and toggle keep their brand color and hover underline,
which never depended on the card chrome.

Type: `SourceCardAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter root padding and row gap, for the dense citation lists these cards usually render in
-- same convention as `lr-empty`'s `compact`. Defaults to `false`, i.e. the full card
padding. Purely a density knob: the border and background stay, so use `appearance="plain"`
to drop the chrome entirely.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
Optional URL, echoed back (unopened) in `lr-open`'s detail.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: page
attributes.description:
Optional page reference, e.g. `12` or `"iv"` ŌĆö rendered as-is (never
parsed/validated as a number), so a non-numeric page label works too.

Type: `string | number | undefined`

attributes.name: source-id
attributes.description:
Stable identifier matching a `<lr-citation-badge>` elsewhere on the page.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: title
attributes.description:
The source's display title, e.g. a filename. Rendered only as the
title button's own text -- a bare host-level `title` attribute (the
browser's global tooltip attribute) is actively stripped once Lit has
synced it into this property, so the card never grows an unsolicited
native tooltip repeating the same text. See `StripHostTitleAttribute`
(`internal/strip-host-title.ts`).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-source-list
elements.description:
`<lr-source-list>` ŌĆö a collapsible "Sources" panel for one chat message,
grouping a set of `<lr-source-card>` children (its default-slot light-DOM
children, plain composition ŌĆö no `.items` array prop, the same shape
`<lr-split>`'s panels take) behind a single clickable header.

This library has no built-in pluralization (see `<lr-empty>`'s plain
`description` prop for a similar stance), so anything beyond the final
fallback is entirely consumer-supplied: `label-plural` (e.g. `"3 sources"`)
wins when set, falling back to `label`, falling back to a localized
`"Sources"` (via `this.localize()`) ŌĆö see each property's own doc.
`sourceCount` (a read-only, live-updated count of the currently-slotted
children) is exposed for a consumer who wants to build that string
reactively instead of hand-counting DOM children.

The card list is removed from the accessibility tree (not just visually
hidden) while collapsed, via the native `hidden` attribute on
`[part="list"]` ŌĆö a screen reader user tabbing past the header never lands
on off-screen source cards they can't currently see.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | `<lr-source-card>` elements (or any content, though the card pairing is the intended usage). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `header` | The clickable header (`<button>`) toggling `expanded`. |
| `list` | The wrapper around the default slot, `hidden` while collapsed. |
| `toggle` | The chevron indicator inside the header. |

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Whether the card list is currently shown. Starts collapsed by default
so a message's sources don't eat vertical space until asked for.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Header text used when `label-plural` isn't set, e.g. `"Sources"`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label-plural
attributes.description:
Fully consumer-built, already-pluralized header summary, e.g. `"3 sources"`
or `"1 source"` ŌĆö this component never counts or pluralizes on its own
(see the class doc). Takes precedence over `label` when both are set.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-source-picker
elements.description:
`<lr-source-picker>` ŌĆö a checkbox tree/list scoping which sources ground the next answer:
tri-state folders, select-all, type icons, search. **Not `FormAssociated`, deliberately**: this
is a scoping panel, not a form control ŌĆö the selection is immediate app state consumed by the
next retrieval call, exactly the stance `lr-tool-select-dialog` already takes.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `checkbox` | The tri-state checkbox glyph. |
| `empty` | The empty state (`noData` when `sources` is empty, `noMatches` when a filter empties the tree). |
| `icon` | The `lr-file-icon` type badge. |
| `item` | One `role="treeitem"` row. |
| `label` | The entry's label text. |
| `search` | The built-in filter `lr-input`, only rendered when `searchable`. |
| `select-all` | The header select-all row, only rendered when `showSelectAll`. |
| `summary` | The "{selected} of {total} selected" text. |
| `tree` | The `role="tree"` container. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-source-picker-checked-bg` ŌĆö Background of a fully-checked selection control: the `select-all` pill (defaults to `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) and a fully-selected entry's `[part="checkbox"]` (defaults to `var(--lr-color-brand)`). The two keep their distinct resting defaults; setting this prop unifies both.
- `--lr-source-picker-checked-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of every checked or mixed selection control.
- `--lr-source-picker-mixed-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-brand) 50%, var(--lr-color-surface))`) ŌĆö Background of a partially-selected entry's `[part="checkbox"]`.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Overrides the tree's computed accessible name. Wins over `label` and the localized
default. Attribute-reflects from a host-level `aria-label` so a plain-markup consumer
gets ARIA-name forwarding without setting a JS property.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: searchable
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: show-select-all
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

elements.name: lr-span-waterfall
elements.description:
`<lr-span-waterfall>` ŌĆö the horizontal-timeline projection of the same
`LyraSpan[]` `<lr-trace-tree>` consumes: a time axis, one row per span
in start order, status-toned bars (Langfuse timeline / Temporal
event-history style).

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `axis` | The time-ruler row, hidden when `hideAxis`. |
| `bar` | The interactive, focusable status-toned bar (`role` via `<button>`). |
| `bar-track` | The bar's positioning track. |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `duration` | The formatted duration text. |
| `empty` | The empty-state message shown when `spans` is empty. |
| `live-region` | The internal focus/status-announcement live region. |
| `meta` | Secondary row info (status/duration), shown inline under 480px. |
| `name` | The span's name (the row's name gutter). |
| `row` | One span's row. |
| `status-text` | The visible status label. |
| `tick` | One axis tick mark. |
| `tick-label` | An axis tick's formatted duration label. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-span-waterfall-name-width` (default: `8rem`) ŌĆö Width of the name gutter column.
- `--lr-span-waterfall-stripe-speed` ŌĆö Animation duration for a `running` span's striped bar; defaults to `--lr-transition-ambient`.
- `--lr-span-waterfall-row-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of the active (`activeSpanId`) row. Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()`, so the active row could otherwise only be restyled by hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token.

attributes.name: active-span-id
attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: hide-axis
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: view-end-ms
attributes.description:
Type: `number | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: view-start-ms
attributes.description:
Visible time window in trace-relative ms (same non-negative, trace-relative vocabulary as
`LyraSpan.startMs`/`endMs` -- never a wall-clock timestamp). Both `null` (the default) fits
the whole trace; a non-null NaN (e.g. an unparsable attribute) is normalized the same way as
`null` by `viewWindow()` rather than poisoning the axis/bar math with NaN.

Type: `number | null`  
Default: `null`

elements.name: lr-sparkline
elements.description:
`<lr-sparkline>` ŌĆö a zero-dependency inline SVG trend chart.
Mirrors the Web Awesome `<wa-sparkline>` API under the `lr-` prefix.

| --- | --- |
| `area` | The filled area under the line (type="area"). |
| `bar` | Each bar rectangle (type="bar"). |
| `line` | The stroked line path (type="line" and type="area"). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-sparkline-stroke-width` (default: `1.5`) ŌĆö Stroke width of the line/area path.
- `--lr-sparkline-area-opacity` (default: `0.15`) ŌĆö Fill opacity of the area under the line (type="area").

attributes.name: aria-label

- `--lr-spinner-track-width` (default: `var(--lr-border-width-medium)`) ŌĆö Thickness of the ring track.
- `--lr-spinner-duration` (default: `800ms`) ŌĆö Duration of one rotation. Not read under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, where the animation is disabled entirely.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the busy status, forwarded from a host `aria-label`. When unset, the
localized "LoadingŌĆ”" default provides the name.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: label-placement
attributes.description:
Type: `SpinnerLabelPlacement`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-split
elements.description:
`<lr-split>` ŌĆö resizable panels for dashboard layouts. Direct light-DOM
children are the panels; a divider is auto-inserted between each pair.

Optionally, one pane can opt in to responsive collapse via `collapse`
(`"start"`/`"end"`, default `"none"` ŌĆö no behavior change when unset): as
the split's own container narrows past `railBreakpoint` that pane clamps
to a fixed `railWidth`, and past the narrower `floatBreakpoint` it instead
becomes an absolutely-positioned overlay "floating card" above the other
pane. Both breakpoints accept a bare pixel number or a CSS length
(`px`/`rem`/`em`), and `collapseBreakpointBasis="viewport"` measures them
against the viewport via `matchMedia` instead of this component's own
allocation. This component only handles the width-collapse mechanics and
signals the current state ŌĆö via the `collapseState`-derived
`data-collapse-state` attribute (set on both the host and the collapsing
panel itself) and the `lr-split-collapse-change` event ŌĆö it renders no
icon-only/collapsed UI of its own; slotted content is expected to adapt to
its own clamped width (e.g. via its own container query).

`collapseState` is a public accessor with force/auto semantics mirroring
`<lr-app-rail>`'s `mode`: it's normally derived automatically from the
measured container width (via a `ResizeObserver` on `[part="base"]`)
whenever it crosses `railBreakpoint`/`floatBreakpoint`, but assigning it a
concrete `'wide'`/`'rail'`/`'floating'` value pins it there and stops that
automatic tracking ŌĆö useful for a consumer-driven toggle (e.g. a button
that forces `'floating'` regardless of width). Assigning the write-only
`'auto'` sentinel releases the pin and immediately re-derives the state
from the current measured width, resuming automatic tracking. `'auto'` is
never a value this getter returns.

The `'floating'` state is a hidden-by-default drawer, gated by `open`
(mirrors `<lr-app-rail>`'s mobile overlay): while `collapseState` is
`'floating'` and `open` is `false` (the default), the collapsing panel
renders nothing ŌĆö `hidden`, out of the accessibility tree, not just
visually hidden ŌĆö instead of the always-visible overlay card this state
rendered before `open` existed. Setting `open = true` reveals it as a
focus-trapped floating panel with a `[part="backdrop"]` scrim; Escape or a
backdrop click set `open` back to `false`. `open` is preserved (not reset)
while `collapseState` isn't `'floating'`, but no drawer chrome renders
until it is again ŌĆö except that leaving `'floating'` while `open` is
`true` (a breakpoint crossing back to `'wide'`/`'rail'`, or a forced
reassignment) also closes it, the same way `<lr-app-rail>` closes its
mobile overlay when leaving `'mobile'` while open.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Panels to arrange side by side (or stacked, when `orientation="vertical"`); each direct child becomes one resizable panel. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `backdrop` | The `'floating'` drawer's scrim. Only rendered while `collapseState === 'floating'` and `open`. |
| `base` | The flex layout wrapper (`position: relative`, so the `'floating'` collapse state can anchor to it). |
| `divider` | Each divider between two panels; carries `aria-disabled="true"` and is drag/keyboard-inert while its adjacent panel is collapsed (`'rail'`/`'floating'`). |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-split-overlay-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-overlay)`) ŌĆö The `'floating'` drawer scrim's color, applied to `[part="backdrop"]`.

attributes.name: collapse
attributes.description:
Opts a pane in to responsive collapse: `'start'` is the first light-DOM
panel (index 0), `'end'` is the last. Both are LOGICAL positions, same
as CSS `inset-inline-start`/`-end` ŌĆö see the `collapsingIndex` getter
for why that already resolves to the same physical index under RTL for
this component (panels are never re-`order`ed for RTL, only the drag
delta sign mirrors). Default `'none'`: none of the collapse behavior
below applies, and rendering/behavior is identical to before this
property existed.

Type: `SplitCollapseMode`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: collapse-breakpoint-basis
attributes.description:
Which box `railBreakpoint`/`floatBreakpoint` measure. `'container'` (the default) observes
this component's own `[part="base"]` inline size via `ResizeObserver`, comparing strictly `<`.
`'viewport'` instead evaluates `matchMedia('(max-width: <breakpoint>)')` for each of the two
thresholds, which is inclusive (`<=`) ŌĆö native `max-width` semantics, deliberately, so the
crossing point matches a CSS `@media` rule authored with the same length exactly. Switching
basis therefore shifts each crossing point by 1px (the same trade-off
`orientationBreakpointBasis` already makes).

Use `'viewport'` to collapse in step with a page-level responsive layout ŌĆö e.g. a shell whose
own `@media` rules restack at the same width ŌĆö rather than with this split's own allocation.
`'viewport'` also lets the browser resolve a `rem` breakpoint with real `@media` semantics
(against the *initial* font size, ignoring an `html { font-size }` override), keeping it in
step with such a rule.

Both bands are classified from both queries together on every change, so a fast resize that
crosses both thresholds at once still lands on one correct state and fires
`lr-split-collapse-change` once. Under `'viewport'` basis the first paint is already correct ŌĆö
no `ResizeObserver` round-trip ŌĆö and the initial state is not announced as a transition.

Default: `'container'`.

Type: `BreakpointBasis`  
Default: `'container'`

attributes.name: collapse-state
attributes.description:
The collapsing pane's current responsive state. Always one of the three
real states ŌĆö never `'auto'` ŌĆö reflecting either the live measured
width or, once forced, whatever was last assigned. See the class doc for
the full force/auto contract (mirrors `<lr-app-rail>`'s `mode`).

Type: `SplitCollapseState`

attributes.name: float-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Width below which the collapsing pane instead becomes an absolutely-positioned overlay above
the other pane (`'floating'` state). Same accepted forms, basis, and sanitization as
`railBreakpoint`; an unparseable value falls back to the `400` default.

Default: `400`.

Type: `number | string`  
Default: `400`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `10`

attributes.name: narrow-orientation
attributes.description:
Layout/resize axis used below `orientationBreakpoint`.

Type: `SplitOrientation`  
Default: `'vertical'`

attributes.name: open
attributes.description:
Whether the `'floating'` collapse state's drawer is shown. Only
meaningful while `collapseState` is `'floating'` ŌĆö the value is
preserved (not reset) while another state is active, but no drawer
chrome renders until `collapseState` is `'floating'` again. Defaults to
`false`: the collapsing pane renders nothing while floating until a
consumer opts in by setting this (or it's forced open programmatically)
ŌĆö see the class doc.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Type: `SplitOrientation`  
Default: `'horizontal'`

attributes.name: orientation-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Opt-in inline-size breakpoint for this component's *own* measured allocation. Below it,
`narrowOrientation` becomes effective. Unset by default ŌĆö the whole responsive-orientation
feature (and its `ResizeObserver`) is off, and `effectiveOrientation` just tracks
`orientation`.

Accepts a bare pixel number (`900`, `orientation-breakpoint="900"` ŌĆö the original form) or a
CSS length string: `'900px'`, `'56.25rem'`, `'3em'`. Under the default
`orientationBreakpointBasis="container"`, `rem` resolves against the *document root*'s
computed font size ŌĆö the rule a `@container` query follows, not a `@media` query's ŌĆö and `em`
against this element's own computed font size. The length is re-resolved on every measurement,
never cached at first render, so a root font-size change moves the crossing width with no
invalidation step. To stay in step with a sibling `@media (max-width: 56.25rem)` rule, use
`orientationBreakpointBasis="viewport"`, which hands the length to the browser instead; see
that property for why the two resolve `rem` differently.

Anything else ŌĆö `''`, `'auto'`, garbage, a non-finite number, and deliberately `%`/`vw`/`vh`/
`calc()` (which would mix reference boxes against an element-relative measurement) ŌĆö behaves
exactly as unset. Set `orientationBreakpointBasis="viewport"` for a viewport-relative
breakpoint instead.

Type: `number | string | undefined`

attributes.name: orientation-breakpoint-basis
attributes.description:
Which box `orientationBreakpoint` measures. `'container'` (the default) observes this
component's own `[part="base"]` inline size via `ResizeObserver`, comparing strictly `<`.
`'viewport'` instead evaluates `matchMedia('(max-width: <breakpoint>)')`, which is inclusive
(`<=`) ŌĆö native `max-width` semantics, deliberately, so the crossing point matches a CSS
`@media` rule authored with the same length exactly.

Use `'viewport'` when two siblings in one row must flip together at a shared breakpoint: a row
that stacks via a pure-CSS `@media` rule makes each sibling's own width non-monotonic across
the transition, so no self-measured threshold can express it. `'viewport'` also lets the
browser resolve a `rem` breakpoint with real `@media` semantics, keeping it in step with such
a rule across browser zoom and user font-size preferences.

Type: `OrientationBreakpointBasis`  
Default: `'container'`

attributes.name: rail-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Width below which the collapsing pane switches from its normal percent width to the fixed
`railWidth` (`'rail'` state). Must stay above `floatBreakpoint` ŌĆö an inverted pair is
sanitized by raising this one to match, which collapses the `'rail'` band away rather than
leaving a wide container reported as collapsed.

Accepts a bare pixel number (`640`, `rail-breakpoint="640"` ŌĆö the original form) or a CSS
length string: `'640px'`, `'68.75rem'`, `'3em'`. Under the default
`collapseBreakpointBasis="container"` this is compared against this component's own measured
`[part="base"]` inline size, strictly `<`, and `rem` resolves against the *document root*'s
computed font size (a `@container` query's rule, not a `@media` query's) while `em` resolves
against this element's own. The length is re-resolved on every measurement, never cached, so
a root font-size change moves the crossing width with no invalidation step.

Anything the grammar rejects ŌĆö `''`, `'auto'`, garbage, a non-finite number, and deliberately
`%`/`vw`/`vh`/`calc()`/`var()` ŌĆö falls back to the `640` default rather than switching the
feature off (unlike `orientationBreakpoint`, this breakpoint has a documented default to fall
back to). A negative length is floored at `0`, i.e. never crossed.

Default: `640`.

Type: `number | string`  
Default: `640`

attributes.name: rail-width
attributes.description:
Fixed CSS length the collapsing pane clamps to in the `'rail'` state.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'3.5rem'`

attributes.name: storage-key
attributes.description: Type: `string | undefined`

elements.name: lr-spreadsheet-viewer
elements.description:
Fetches and renders `.xlsx` and legacy `.xls` workbooks with virtualized rows and sheet tabs.

Adopts `DocumentAnchorTarget`: a `cell-range` anchor addresses one sheet's raw grid, 1-based, with
its (always-present) header row included -- matching how a spreadsheet app itself labels `A1`.
The target sheet resolves from the anchor's own `sheet` field (falling back to a `Sheet!`-prefixed
`range`, then the currently active sheet when neither is set); `scrollToAnchor()` switches
`<lr-tabs>`'s `active` tab first when the resolved sheet isn't already active, then scrolls the
addressed row into view via the virtualized list's `active-id`, then scrolls the first addressed
column horizontally into view. `highlights` paint as a focusable `part="cell-highlight"` on
membership, recomputed per row inside `renderRow()` so a row scrolled out and back in reconstructs
its highlight for free, with no persistent DOM to keep in sync. `search()` is a case-insensitive
substring match over every sheet's stringified cell values (the same stringification `cell()`
already renders), ordered sheet then row then column, switching tabs as navigation crosses sheets.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `cell` | One rendered cell. |
| `cell-highlight` | A cell covered by a `highlights` entry -- focusable, emits `lr-highlight-activate` on click or Enter/Space. |
| `data-row` | One virtualized data row. |
| `error` | The error message region. |
| `header-row` | A sheet's header row. |
| `rows` | The virtualized row list. |
| `sheet` | The wrapper around one sheet's header row and virtualized body. |
| `spinner` | The loading status region. |
| `tabs` | The sheet-switching `<lr-tabs>`, rendered only for a multi-sheet workbook. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-spreadsheet-viewer-highlight-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Outline color of a highlighted cell. The active highlight sets it inline to `var(--lr-color-warning, var(--lr-color-brand))`.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Source filename or display name, used as the viewer's accessible name.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
URL to fetch and parse.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-stack-trace
elements.description:
`<lr-stack-trace>` ŌĆö parses common V8/JS-TS, Firefox/Safari, and Python stack traces into a
leading message plus activatable frames, splitting chained/caused-by errors into separate
groups. Frames matching `internalPatterns` (`node_modules/`, `node:internal`,
`site-packages/`, ... by default) fold behind a count-labeled toggle. Falls back to verbatim
raw text when nothing parses. First-party invention (no Web Awesome equivalent).

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper; respects `max-height`. Drops its card chrome under `appearance="plain"`. |
| `copy-button` | The copy-to-clipboard button, only rendered while `copyable`. |
| `frame` | A single frame button; carries `data-internal` for internal frames. |
| `frame-function` | The frame's function name. |
| `frame-location` | The frame's `file:line:col` text. |
| `group` | One chained-error group of frames. |
| `internal-toggle` | The collapse/expand toggle for a run of internal frames. |
| `message` | The leading error message text for a group. |
| `raw` | The verbatim `<pre>` fallback when zero structured frames parsed. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-stack-trace-max-height` (default: `none`) ŌĆö Cap on how tall `[part="base"]` grows before it scrolls internally. `none` lets the component grow with its content; the `max-height` attribute sets this token.
- `--lr-stack-trace-font` (default: `var(--lr-font-mono)`) ŌĆö Font family for the parsed frames and the verbatim raw fallback.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled, padded box. `'plain'` removes the border, background, padding and corner
radius so a trace nested inside an `lr-result-card`/`lr-agent-run` (which already draws a
border) doesn't double the frame. The `max-height` scroll cap and the copy/frame affordances
are unaffected.

Type: `StackTraceAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: collapse-internal
attributes.description:
Folds runs of internal frames (matching `internalPatterns`) behind a toggle.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: copyable
attributes.description:
Shows a copy-to-clipboard button for the raw trace text.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-height
attributes.description:
Caps the rendered block size and enables an internal scrollbar once content exceeds it
(any valid CSS length, e.g. `'20rem'`). Empty string (the default) grows with content.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: trace
attributes.description:
The raw stack trace text to parse and render.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-stat
elements.description:
`<lr-stat>` ŌĆö a KPI/stat card. First-party invention consolidating the
"metric row" / "KPI card" pattern common to dashboard UIs.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Leading icon. |
| `caption` | Rich caption content (overrides the `caption` attribute). |
| `spark` | A sparkline (e.g. `<lr-sparkline>`) or other compact trend visual. `lr-stat` only reserves the slot; it doesn't render one itself. |
| `sub` | Rich sub-line content (overrides the `sub` attribute). |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The component's root wrapper (`<div>`, or a real `<a>` when `href` is safe). |
| `caption` | Container for the caption attribute/slot. |
| `icon` | Container for the leading icon slot. |
| `label` | The label text. Hidden (and collapsed) whenever `label` is empty, so a label-less stat doesn't leave a blank line above the value. |
| `row` | A single breakdown row (one per `rows` entry). |
| `row-label` | The label text of a breakdown row. |
| `row-value` | The value text of a breakdown row. Shows the row's `exactValue` (if any) as a hover/focus tooltip, same as the headline `value`, and is accessibly labelled by its `row-label` (via `aria-labelledby`) the same way the headline `value` is. |
| `rows` | Container for the `rows` breakdown list. |
| `spark` | Container for the `spark` slot. |
| `sub` | Container for the `sub` attribute/slot. |
| `trend` | The trend pill. |
| `unit` | The unit text. |
| `value` | The value text. Accessibly labelled by the `label` part (via `aria-labelledby`) whenever `label` is set, so tabbing directly to this (focusable when `exactValue` is set) control still announces which metric it is. |
| `value-row` | Wrapper around the value and unit. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-stat-trend-good-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-success)`) ŌĆö Text color of the trend pill when its polarity is "good". Independent of the headline value's `variant="success"` tint, which reads the shared `--lr-color-success` token directly.
- `--lr-stat-trend-good-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-success) 8%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of the trend pill when its polarity is "good".
- `--lr-stat-trend-bad-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-danger)`) ŌĆö Text color of the trend pill when its polarity is "bad". Independent of the headline value's `variant="danger"` tint, which reads the shared `--lr-color-danger` token directly.
- `--lr-stat-trend-bad-bg` (default: `color-mix(in srgb, var(--lr-color-danger) 8%, transparent)`) ŌĆö Background of the trend pill when its polarity is "bad".

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled, padded box that stretches to fill its parent; `'plain'` removes the border,
background, padding, corner radius and the `block-size: 100%` stretch so the stat can sit
inline in prose, a toolbar or a table cell. `plain` wins over `compact` when both are set
(nothing left to tighten), and it also drops `emphasis`'s accent edge ŌĆö that edge is card
chrome ŌĆö while `emphasis`'s brand value tint still applies. A `plain` stat with a safe `href`
swaps the card's border-color/lift hover affordance (invisible with no border) for an
underline on `[part='value']`; the `:focus-visible` ring is unchanged.

Type: `StatAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: caption
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter padding for constrained spaces ŌĆö same convention as `lr-empty`'s `compact`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: emphasis
attributes.description:
Visual emphasis (e.g. for a "headline" stat in a group) ŌĆö orthogonal to
the status `variant`; see the `[part='value']` selector below for how
the two combine.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: exact-value
attributes.description:
Exact value shown as a hover/focus tooltip on the headline `value` (e.g. `value="$1.2K"
exact-value="$1,204.37"`). Also makes `[part='value']` keyboard-focusable so the tooltip is
reachable without a pointer.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: good-direction
attributes.description:
Which trend direction counts as "good" ŌĆö inverts arrow/color polarity for
cost/latency/error-rate-style metrics where a decrease is the win.

Type: `StatGoodDirection`  
Default: `'up'`

attributes.name: href
attributes.description:
When set to a safe URL, renders the whole stat as a real anchor instead of a static div.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
Layout axis. `'vertical'` (the default) stacks label, value, trend, sub and caption.
`'horizontal'` lays label, value+unit, trend, sub and caption out on a single wrapping
baseline row; `[part='spark']` and `[part='rows']` have no sensible place on a text baseline
and stay stacked on their own full-width line beneath that row.

Type: `StatOrientation`  
Default: `'vertical'`

attributes.name: prose
attributes.description:
Renders `value` as smaller/lighter prose (e.g. a loading/status message) instead of the bold
numeric headline style, and hides `unit`.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: sub
attributes.description:
A secondary line distinct from `caption` (e.g. a comparison-period label), rendered between the
trend pill and the caption.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: target
attributes.description:
Native anchor target, used only while `href` resolves to a link. Setting this to `'_blank'`
(or any other target) automatically derives `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on the rendered
anchor -- there is no separately-settable `rel` property, so a consumer can't forget it and
leave the opened page holding a `window.opener` back-reference (reverse-tabnabbing). Matches
`app-rail-item.class.ts`'s pattern.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: trend
attributes.description:
Percentage/delta value for the trend pill (e.g. `-12.5` renders a down arrow at "12.5%").
`NaN` (the default, and what a removed `trend` attribute resolves to ŌĆö Lit's `type: Number`
converter maps a missing attribute to `null`, treated the same as `NaN` here) is a
deliberate sentinel meaning "no trend": it hides the pill entirely and is preserved as-is,
never coerced away. Any other non-finite input (e.g. a literal `trend="Infinity"` attribute)
is normalized via `finiteNumber` to a flat `0` instead of rendering a bogus
"Infinity%"/"-Infinity%" pill; genuine finite numbers pass through unclamped.

Type: `number`

attributes.name: unit
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
Type: `StatVariant`  
Default: `'neutral'`

elements.name: lr-stepper
elements.description:
`<lr-stepper>` ŌĆö ordered multi-step wizard/form navigation: label + index per step,
current/completed/locked/error state, click-to-jump. First-party invention (no Web Awesome
equivalent). Fully data-driven and controlled, like `lr-table`'s `columns`/`rows` -- it never
mutates `steps` itself; a click or Enter/Space on a non-disabled step fires a cancelable
`lr-step-select`, and the host decides whether/how `steps` changes in response (mirroring
`lr-dialog-close`'s cancelable-event convention).

An opt-in `orientationBreakpoint` (unset by default -- no behavior change) makes the effective
layout/navigation axis respond to a measured inline size instead of only the authored
`orientation`: below that width (a pixel number or a `px`/`rem`/`em` CSS length), `narrowOrientation`
becomes effective; at/above it, `orientation` does. This mirrors `<lr-split>`'s identically-named
`orientationBreakpoint`/`narrowOrientation`/`orientationBreakpointBasis` contract. Under the default
`orientationBreakpointBasis="container"` the breakpoint is measured on this stepper's own
`[part="base"]` inline size via `ResizeObserver`, so a stepper placed in a narrow split pane or
dialog still responds correctly even in a wide window; `orientationBreakpointBasis="viewport"`
instead evaluates `matchMedia('(max-width: <breakpoint>)')`, needed when the stepper has a fixed
width in a row that stacks at a shared breakpoint. The effective axis is exposed via the
`effectiveOrientation` getter, a `data-effective-orientation` host attribute (only present while
the breakpoint feature is active), and `lr-stepper-orientation-change`.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper. |
| `step` | A single step button. |
| `step-check` | The completed-checkmark glyph, shown for `completed` steps instead of `step-index`. |
| `step-icon` | Optional leading topic glyph supplied by the item's `icon` field; content may have a natural aspect ratio and is not restricted to a square icon. Rendered additionally to, never instead of, `step-index`/`step-check`. |
| `step-index` | The numbered index chip, shown for `pending`/`current`/`error` steps. |
| `step-label` | The step's label text. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-stepper-current-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color of the `current` step. Declared as an inline `var()` fallback (never on `:host`), so setting it on the element or an ancestor recolors only the current step without hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-text` token.
- `--lr-stepper-current-font-weight` (default: `var(--lr-font-weight-semibold)`) ŌĆö Font weight of the `current` step's label. `::part(step)[data-state='current']` is invalid CSS (an attribute selector cannot follow `::part`), so this is the only way to change just the current step's boldness without hijacking the library-wide `--lr-font-weight-semibold` token.
- `--lr-stepper-error-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-danger)`) ŌĆö Text color of an `error` step.
- `--lr-stepper-current-index-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Background of the `current` step's numbered index chip (`step-index`).
- `--lr-stepper-current-index-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Text color of the `current` step's numbered index chip.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the `role="tablist"` step strip. Attribute-reflects from a host-level
`aria-label` so a plain-markup consumer gets ARIA-name forwarding without setting a JS
property. Unset, the tablist renders without an `aria-label` (the role carries no localized
default name).

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: narrow-orientation
attributes.description:
Layout/navigation axis used below `orientationBreakpoint`.

Type: `StepperOrientation`  
Default: `'vertical'`

attributes.name: orientation
attributes.description:
`'horizontal'` (the default) lays steps out in a row (Left/Right, RTL-aware, to navigate);
`'vertical'` stacks them (Up/Down navigate instead, no RTL swap needed). The *authored* axis
used at/above `orientationBreakpoint` (or always, when that's unset) -- see
`effectiveOrientation` for the live axis actually in effect.

Type: `StepperOrientation`  
Default: `'horizontal'`

attributes.name: orientation-breakpoint
attributes.description:
Opt-in inline-size breakpoint, measured on `[part="base"]`. Below it, `narrowOrientation`
becomes effective instead of `orientation`. Unset (the default): no behavior change, the
authored `orientation` always applies.

Accepts a bare pixel number (`500`, `'500'`), an explicit `px` length (`'500px'`), a `rem`
length (`'31.25rem'`) or an `em` length (`'3em'`). Under the default
`orientationBreakpointBasis="container"`, `rem` resolves against the **document root**'s
computed font size -- the rule a `@container` query follows, and *not* a `@media` query's --
while `em` resolves against this element's own computed font size. The length is re-resolved
on every measurement, never cached, so browser zoom, a user font-size preference or an app
base-size token change are picked up with no invalidation step. To stay in step with a
sibling `@media (max-width: ŌĆ”rem)` rule, use `orientationBreakpointBasis="viewport"`, which
hands the length to the browser instead; see that property for why the two differ.

Any other value -- `%`, `vw`, `calc()`, `'auto'`, an unparseable string -- behaves exactly as
unset (no responsive observation at all), rather than as an armed breakpoint that can never
be crossed. Set `orientationBreakpointBasis="viewport"` for a viewport-relative breakpoint
instead.

Type: `number | string | undefined`

attributes.name: orientation-breakpoint-basis
attributes.description:
Which box `orientationBreakpoint` measures. `'container'` (the default) observes this
stepper's own `[part="base"]` inline size via `ResizeObserver`, comparing strictly `<`.
`'viewport'` instead evaluates `matchMedia('(max-width: <breakpoint>)')`, which is inclusive
(`<=`) -- native `max-width` semantics, deliberately, so the crossing point matches a CSS
`@media` rule authored with the same length exactly.

A stepper given a fixed width in a row layout cannot react to that row stacking by measuring
itself -- its own width never changes. That case requires `'viewport'`, which also lets the
browser resolve a `rem` breakpoint with real `@media` semantics.

Type: `OrientationBreakpointBasis`  
Default: `'container'`

elements.name: lr-stream-status
elements.description:
`<lr-stream-status>` ŌĆö a compact status indicator for a single streaming
connection (SSE, WebSocket, long-poll, ŌĆ”), with built-in heartbeat-aware
stall detection.

The host drives `phase` directly for `idle`/`connecting`/`streaming`, and
calls `recordActivity()` on every *semantic* frame received while
streaming ŌĆö a real content chunk, never a transport-level keep-alive ping.
This component has no payload-inspection logic of its own: "ignore
heartbeats" is entirely a call-site discipline ŌĆö the host simply never
calls `recordActivity()` for a ping, so pings never reset the stall timer
and a connection that's only sending keep-alives (no real content) for
longer than `stall-threshold-ms` correctly reads as stalled.

Internally, an inactivity timer runs only while `phase === 'streaming'`.
It's (re)armed whenever the phase transitions to `'streaming'` (directly,
or via `recordActivity()` recovering from `'stalled'`), on every
subsequent `recordActivity()` call while already streaming, whenever
`stall-threshold-ms` itself changes while already streaming (the new
value takes effect immediately, the same way `<lr-toast-item>`'s
`duration` re-applies mid-flight, rather than waiting for the next
`recordActivity()`/phase change), and whenever this element (re)connects
to the DOM while `phase` is still `'streaming'` (a disconnect always
disarms it, so moving the element elsewhere in the page ŌĆö disconnect then
reconnect with `phase` unchanged ŌĆö must resume detection rather than
silently disabling it for the rest of the streaming session). It's
disarmed the instant `phase` becomes anything else, including a
host-driven reassignment away from `'streaming'` ŌĆö so a stale timer can
never fire a stall transition after the host has already moved on. If it
ever fires, `phase` becomes `'stalled'` and `lr-stall` is dispatched.

`phase` remains a fully public, directly settable property at all times ŌĆö
a host can assign `'stalled'` (or any other phase) itself as a manual
override, and this component never fights that assignment. `lr-stall`/
`lr-recover` fire on *any* transition into/out of `'stalled'`
respectively, whether timer-driven or host-driven, and never for a
reassignment to the same value (Lit's default `hasChanged` already skips a
no-op set, same as every other reflected `@property` in this library).
Like `<lr-chat-message>`'s `status`, whatever phase this element happens
to *mount* with is never itself treated as an eventful transition ŌĆö only a
later change fires an event or an announcement.

Accessibility: phase transitions into/out of `'stalled'` are announced
through an internal `<lr-live-region>` (see that component for the
throttled/coalesced-announcement machinery this composes) rather than a
hand-rolled `aria-live` region. `recordActivity()` itself never announces
anything, no matter how often the host calls it ŌĆö only the *transition*
announces, exactly once per transition, which is the entire point of
routing through the throttled announcer instead of writing to a live
region on every call. Entering `'stalled'` announces with `mode="assertive"`
(a stall can need the user's attention, e.g. before they give up and
navigate away); leaving `'stalled'` always announces with `mode="polite"`
(good news doesn't need to interrupt), but the *wording* depends on where
it lands: `"Connection restored."` only when the destination is
`'streaming'` (a genuine recovery), or a neutral `"No longer stalled."`
when the destination is `'idle'`/`'connecting'` instead ŌĆö that's the host
giving up on the stream, not the stream recovering, and a screen-reader
user must never be told the opposite of what a sighted user sees on
screen. The decorative indicator dot is `aria-hidden` ŌĆö it's a
color/motion cue only, never the sole carrier of state.

Visual: `'stalled'` is styled as a warning, not a danger ŌĆö a stall is
usually recoverable (the stream may resume on its own, or the host's own
retry logic may kick in), so treating it as an actionable warning rather
than a hard failure keeps the tone proportionate. A host that wants to
escalate to danger styling after N stalls can scope its own CSS off
`[phase="stalled"]`, or simply stop rendering this component and show its
own danger-styled error state instead.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Custom copy shown only while `phase="stalled"` (e.g. "Taking longer than usualŌĆ”"). A sensible built-in default is used when nothing is slotted. |
| `actions` | A stop/retry button row. Always present in the template regardless of `phase` ŌĆö visibility is driven purely by whether anything is slotted, not by `phase`; what to put here, and when, is entirely the host's call. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `actions` | Wrapper around the `actions` slot. |
| `base` | The root layout container. |
| `indicator` | The decorative (`aria-hidden`) status dot. |
| `message` | Wrapper around the default slot; only rendered while `phase="stalled"`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-stream-status-dot-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text-quiet)`) ŌĆö `indicator` dot color. Swapped by the reflected `phase`: `var(--lr-color-brand)` for `connecting`/`streaming`, `var(--lr-color-warning)` for `stalled`.
- `--lr-stream-status-dot-opacity` (default: `0.35`) ŌĆö `indicator` dot opacity. Swapped by the reflected `phase`: `0.6` for `connecting`, `1` for `streaming` and `stalled`.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: phase
attributes.description:
Current connection phase. Settable directly by the host at any time ŌĆö
see the class doc for how that interacts with the internal stall timer.

Type: `StreamStatusPhase`  
Default: `'idle'`

attributes.name: stall-threshold-ms
attributes.description:
How long `phase` may stay `'streaming'` with no `recordActivity()` call
before this component declares it stalled.

Type: `number`  
Default: `10000`

elements.name: lr-streaming-text
elements.description:
`<lr-streaming-text>` ŌĆö a token-coalescing incremental text renderer for
streaming assistant output, with an optional blinking cursor and
auto-detected Markdown rendering.

The host is expected to assign the *entire* current text on every update
to `content`, not a delta -- this component does no accumulation or
ordering of its own. That matches how most streaming-UI state (SSE/
WebSocket token accumulation into a growing string in application state)
is already managed.

Token-by-token streaming can update `content` far faster than a human can
usefully perceive a re-render -- dozens of times a second for a fast
model. Re-rendering on every single assignment thrashes layout for no
visible benefit, so updates funnel through `Announcer`
(`../../internal/announcer.js`), reused here purely as the generic
"coalesce rapid calls, flush the latest" timing primitive its own class
doc says other components should reuse -- with none of its usual
DOM/ARIA plumbing (that's `<lr-live-region>`'s job, not this
component's). Within any `coalesce-ms` window, only the *last* `content`
value assigned actually reaches the rendered DOM. Two cases always bypass
the throttle and flush immediately instead of waiting out the window: the
very first `content` assignment after mount (so an already-complete
message never shows an artificial startup delay), and any transition of
`streaming` between `true` and `false` in either direction (so the final
chunk of a finished stream can never be left stranded mid-window, and a
stream restarting on a reused element can never keep showing the
previous stream's stale final content for the length of the window).

`markdown` is a tri-state property, not a plain boolean: left unset (the
default), it auto-detects via a lightweight regex heuristic (see
`looksLikeMarkdown`) run against whatever text is currently displayed --
good enough to route obviously-Markdown output through `<lr-markdown>`
without the host needing to know or declare it up front, at the cost of
an occasional one-time mode flip if Markdown syntax only appears partway
through a stream. Explicitly setting `markdown` to `true`/`false` always
wins over the heuristic. Rendering itself is never reimplemented here:
Markdown mode composes `<lr-markdown>` (`../markdown/markdown.js`)
directly, forwarding this component's own `streaming` through as that
component's own forward-compatible `streaming` hint prop; plain-text mode
renders into a `white-space: pre-wrap` span instead.

The blinking cursor (shown only while `streaming` is `true`) degrades to
a static, always-visible bar under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`
rather than disabling the animation into an invisible frozen frame --
the same pattern `<lr-typing-indicator>`'s own `cursor` variant uses. In
plain-text mode the cursor sits inline at the tail of the final
character, since the text is one continuous inline flow; in Markdown
mode it renders as its own trailing block below the rendered content
instead of attempting to splice into whatever nested block Markdown
happens to end with (a paragraph, a table cell, a list item, a fenced
code block, ŌĆ”) -- genuinely interleaving into arbitrary rendered Markdown
without reaching across `<lr-markdown>`'s own shadow boundary isn't a
tractable general solution, so this component doesn't attempt it.

Purely presentational: no events, driven entirely by its properties. It
also doesn't announce anything to assistive tech itself -- a host that
needs streamed text announced already has `<lr-live-region>` for that
(composed, for example, inside `<lr-chat-message>`).

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root container. |
| `cursor` | The blinking (or, under reduced motion, static) cursor bar. Only rendered while `streaming` is `true`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-streaming-text-cursor-width` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-125rem)`) ŌĆö Width of the `cursor` bar (also its border radius).
- `--lr-streaming-text-cursor-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-1em)`) ŌĆö Height of the `cursor` bar.

attributes.name: coalesce-ms
attributes.description:
Trailing-edge coalesce window, in ms, for `content` updates -- see the
class doc.

Default: `DEFAULT_COALESCE_MS`

attributes.name: content
attributes.description:
The full current text so far -- always the complete string, never a
delta to append.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: markdown
attributes.description:
`undefined` (default) auto-detects via `looksLikeMarkdown`; `true`/
`false` force that rendering mode regardless of content.

Type: `boolean | undefined`

attributes.name: streaming
attributes.description:
Shows the blinking cursor after the rendered text. Reflects, so a host
can also target `lr-streaming-text[streaming]` in CSS.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-suggestion-chips
elements.description:
`<lr-suggestion-chips>` ŌĆö starter prompts (empty thread) and follow-up suggestions (after a
response) as a horizontally scrollable chip row; activation hands the prompt to the host, which
decides whether to compose it into an input or send it directly. Never writes into a composer or
sends anything itself.

Streaming-friendly: chips render through a keyed `repeat()` on `id`, so replacing follow-ups
mid-conversation preserves focus on any chip whose `id` survives.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The labeled group. |
| `chip` | Each suggestion button. |
| `chip-detail` | The secondary line (only rendered when `detail` is set). |
| `chip-label` | The primary text. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-suggestion-chips-hover-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a hovered chip.
- `--lr-suggestion-chips-hover-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of a hovered chip.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the group. Defaults to the localized `suggestionsLabel`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: wrap
attributes.description:
Wraps into multiple rows instead of a single horizontally scrollable line.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-svg-viewer
elements.description:
Fetches and safely renders an inline SVG document.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root container. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the fetched-state content. |
| `error` | The error region. |
| `frame-content` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `frame-controls` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `frame-reset` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `frame-viewport` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `frame-zoom-in` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `frame-zoom-out` | Forwarded from the internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>` when `zoomable`. |
| `highlight-layer` | The wrapper around every rendered region highlight. |
| `region-highlight` | One region highlight (`data-tone`, `data-active`). |
| `spinner` | The loading region. |
| `svg` | The sanitized SVG document, once loaded. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-svg-viewer-max-height` (default: `none`) ŌĆö Maximum block size of the scrollable body before it scrolls internally. Also settable via the `max-height` property.
- `--lr-svg-viewer-active-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-warning, var(--lr-color-brand))`) ŌĆö Border color of the `[part="region-highlight"]` matching `activeHighlightId`. Distinct from the resting highlight border, so the active region can be recolored without touching the rest.

attributes.name: active-highlight-id
attributes.description:
Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max-height
attributes.description:
CSS length that caps the scrollable body.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the rendered SVG.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: src
attributes.description:
URL to fetch and render as sanitized inline SVG.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: zoomable
attributes.description:
Wraps the rendered content in an internal `<lr-zoomable-frame>`. `false` (the default)
preserves today's exact DOM -- an inline thumbnail (e.g. in a chat stream) must not
unexpectedly grow a focusable zoom-chrome viewport; an inspection surface opts in.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-swatch-picker
elements.description:
`<lr-swatch-picker>` -- a single-select picker over a small, fixed set of color swatches with
the WAI-ARIA APG `radiogroup` contract built in: `role="radiogroup"`/`role="radio"`, roving
tabindex, automatic activation (click or arrow-key move both select immediately, like a native
radio group), cyclic Arrow/Home/End navigation. Distinct from `<lr-color-picker>`'s freeform
native color input -- this picks exactly one of N designer-chosen named colors, the shape apps
otherwise hand-roll as a row of round accent-color buttons.

`mode="gemstone"` uses the shared faceted gemstone glyph for options carrying a `gemstone`
key and opts into the gemstone glow/shine defaults. The `options` array still controls display
order and `value` still controls the initial selection.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The `role="radiogroup"` root. |
| `swatch` | A single `role="radio"` color swatch's interactive hit target; sized via `--lr-swatch-picker-hit-size` (defaults to `--lr-size-2-5rem`, swapped per `size` tier and floored at 24px), independent of the smaller visible fill/icon rendered inside it. The selected one is `[part='swatch'][aria-checked='true']`. |
| `swatch-fill` | The compact filled circle rendered when the option has no custom `icon`. |
| `swatch-icon` | Optional custom shape supplied by the option's `icon` field; when present it replaces `swatch-fill` and the swatch renders unfilled/unbordered behind it. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-swatch-picker-selected-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Ring color drawn around the selected swatch, themeable independently of the focus ring and every other ring color.
