- `--lr-swatch-picker-selected-blur` (default: `0`) ŌĆö Blur radius of that same ring. 0 by default (a crisp ring); set a real length (e.g. 0.4rem) for a soft glow instead.
- `--lr-swatch-picker-shine-duration` (default: `0s`) ŌĆö Duration of a rhythmic brighten-and-settle "shine" on the selected swatch. `0s` (the default) is a no-op -- today's static look for every existing consumer. Set a real duration (e.g. 1.6s) for a pulsing shine; disabled outright under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. Independent of `--lr-swatch-picker-selected-blur` (a separate `filter: brightness()` animation, not `box-shadow`), so the two compose freely, and works identically for a plain color circle and an icon swatch alike.
- `--lr-swatch-picker-gemstone-selected-blur` (default: `var(--lr-size-0-5rem)`) ŌĆö Selected glow blur used by `mode="gemstone"` when `--lr-swatch-picker-selected-blur` is not overridden.
- `--lr-swatch-picker-gemstone-shine-duration` (default: `1.8s`) ŌĆö Selected shine duration used by `mode="gemstone"` when `--lr-swatch-picker-shine-duration` is not overridden.
- `--lr-swatch-picker-hit-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-2-5rem)`) ŌĆö Hit-area size (both min-inline-size and min-block-size) for the swatch button, swapped per tier and floored at 24px.
- `--lr-swatch-picker-fill-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-5rem)`) ŌĆö Visible fill/icon diameter for the swatch, swapped per tier.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the radiogroup, used when no visible label context
exists around it (e.g. no wrapping `<label>` or adjacent heading). Set
as `aria-label` on the `role="radiogroup"` element. A plain `aria-label`
attribute on the host itself is honored as a fallback when this is left
unset, matching `<lr-segmented>`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
Visual treatment. `swatch` preserves the plain-circle default; `gemstone` renders the shared
gemstone glyph for options with a `gemstone` key and enables its glow/shine recipe.

Type: `LyraSwatchPickerMode`  
Default: `'swatch'`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size ŌĆö scales the swatch hit-area and fill diameter proportionally, hit-area
floored at 24px (WCAG 2.5.8); not pixel-matched to `lr-input`'s row-height scale.

Type: `LyraSwatchPickerSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description:
The currently selected option's `value`, or `null` when nothing is selected.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

elements.name: lr-switch
elements.description:
`<lr-switch>` ŌĆö a boolean toggle-switch form control. Structurally the
same idea as a checkbox (form-associated via `ElementInternals`, click and
Space/Enter both toggle) but with switch semantics: `role="switch"` +
`aria-checked` read to assistive tech as an on/off state rather than a
checked/unchecked one, and there is no indeterminate state.

`checked` is not a plain string, so this attaches `ElementInternals`
directly and implements its own `updateValidity()` rather than using the
`FormAssociated` mixin (that mixin's `value` accessor assumes a string ŌĆö
see `<lr-combobox>` for the same direct-`ElementInternals` shape with a
non-string value).

Ships an opt-in `hint`/`errorText` form-control chrome (props + matching named slots +
`hint`/`error` CSS parts), mirroring `<lr-select>`'s pattern for those two pieces -- left
unset, neither renders. Deliberately no separate top-of-field `label` prop/slot/part mirroring
`<lr-select>`'s `form-control-label`: the default slot already *is* this control's visible,
clickable label (same as `<lr-checkbox>`), so a second label surface would be redundant.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Label text, rendered next to the track. Clicking it toggles the switch, the same as clicking a checkbox's associated `<label>`. If left empty, set `aria-label` on the host so the control still has an accessible name. |
| `error` | Custom error content. |
| `hint` | Custom hint content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The whole interactive control (`role="switch"`); wraps the track and label. |
| `error` | The error message. |
| `form-control` | The outer wrapper around the switch, error and hint. |
| `hint` | The hint message. |
| `label` | The wrapper around the default slot. |
| `thumb` | The circular knob that slides across the track. |
| `track` | The pill-shaped background. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-switch-track-inline-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-2-25rem)`) ŌĆö Inline size of the track, and (with the block size) the distance the thumb travels when checked.
- `--lr-switch-track-block-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-25rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of the track; the thumb's diameter is derived from it minus twice `--lr-switch-thumb-offset`.
- `--lr-switch-thumb-offset` (default: `var(--lr-size-2px)`) ŌĆö Inset of the thumb from the track's edges.

attributes.name: checked
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: error-text
attributes.description:
Error text below the switch (overridden by slotted `error` content). Unset: no error chrome
renders.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description:
Hint text below the switch. Unset: no hint chrome renders.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The form submission key, reflected synchronously for native form APIs.

Type: `string`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: value
attributes.description: Type: `string`

elements.name: lr-table
elements.description:
`<lr-table>` ŌĆö a presentational, sort/select-aware data table.

Header/row activation is delegated: one `click` and one `keydown`
listener on `<table>` resolve the target via `closest('[data-col-key]'
| '[data-row-key]')` and a keyŌåÆobject lookup map, instead of allocating
fresh per-column/per-row closures on every render. Both listeners guard
against nested interactive `cell()` content first (see
`INTERACTIVE_SELECTOR`) so a button/link/input inside a cell owns its own
activation instead of triggering `lr-row-click`.

Keyboard focus follows a roving-tabindex pattern (one `tabindex="0"` stop
among the header cells, one among the body rows ŌĆö see `focusedColKey()` /
`focusedRowKey()`), matching this repo's other `role="grid"`/composite
widgets. Left/Right/Home/End move within the header row; Up/Down/Home/End
move within the body; Down from the header enters the body's roving stop,
and Up from the body's first row returns to the header's roving stop.
Enter/Space still only sort/activate (see `activateColumn()` /
`activateRow()`).

Set `aria-label` on the host to give the `role="grid"` element an
accessible name; it's forwarded into the shadow DOM's `<table>`.

`columns[].priority` ('medium' | 'low') hides that column under
`[part='base']`'s `@container` breakpoints; `[part='reveal-columns-button']`
forces them all back into view. Rather than a *static* check of whether any
column merely declares a `priority`, the button (and the public
`columnsHidden` property, see below) reflects whether a `priority` column
is *actually* hidden right now ŌĆö measured via `ResizeObserver` on
`[part='base']` plus a post-render DOM check ŌĆö or the public `showAllColumns`
force-visible mode is currently active (so there's still a way to toggle
it back off). `showAllColumns` defaults to `false` and toggles itself on
`[part='reveal-columns-button']` activation with no external wiring
required, but is also settable up front (property or the reflected
`show-all-columns` attribute) to restore a previously-persisted
preference, and readable back ŌĆö directly or via the `lr-columns-revealed`
event ŌĆö to persist the current one. `columns[].sticky` pins a column's
header/cells to the inline-start (default/`true`) or inline-end (`'end'`)
edge while the table scrolls horizontally.

`expandedContent` (a table-level `(row: T) => unknown`, not a per-column
hook, since the resulting panel spans every column via `colspan`) makes
every row render a leading chevron-toggle cell before its data columns.
`canExpand` optionally gates which rows actually get an interactive
toggle ŌĆö a row that fails it still gets a blank leading cell for column
alignment. Which rows are currently open is fully consumer-owned via
`expandedKeys` (a `Set<string | number>` of row keys, per `rowKey`/
`keyOf()`) ŌĆö the table only reads it and emits `lr-row-expand-toggle`
on activation, mirroring `sortKey`/`selectedKey`'s existing
presentational-only convention.

Selection is opt-in through the `selectionMode` property. Use `single` or
`multiple` to self-manage row selection; the default `none` remains
presentational. `selectedKeys` contains the raw keys selected in multiple
mode.

`filterable` adds a compact search field above the grid. `filterText` is
controlled and emits `lr-filter-change`; `filter` can provide a typed
predicate, otherwise the row is matched against its JSON representation.
`pageSize` enables controlled pagination through the existing
`<lr-pagination>` primitive. Client mode slices `rows`; server mode
renders the supplied page unchanged while using `totalItems` for the
navigation summary. `loading` keeps the table shell busy; `loadingAppearance`
chooses how ŌĆö the default `'spinner'` replaces the grid with an indeterminate
spinner, while `'skeleton'` keeps the real `<colgroup>`/`<thead>` (and the
filter/pagination chrome) and fills the body with `skeletonRows` placeholder
rows, so column geometry survives the load instead of collapsing and
reflowing. Either way exactly one `role="status"` live region announces the
state ŌĆö every placeholder opts out of `<lr-skeleton>`'s own announcement.
Columns with `editable: true` open a native text/number editor on
double-click and emit `lr-cell-edit`; row mutation remains consumer-owned.
`editable: 'always'` instead renders that editor in every body cell of the
column from first paint ŌĆö a settings/rate-style column meant to be typed
straight into. Persistent editors are plain tab stops (no `tabindex` of their
own, exactly like the row-expand toggle) outside the header/row roving model,
so arrow keys still navigate the grid from a row's own tab stop and act as
caret movement once focus is inside a field. Enter commits and keeps focus;
Escape has nothing to cancel back to, so it is left uncancelled for an
ancestor dialog/popover. Their value binds as a content attribute, so once
the user has typed into one an out-of-band `rows` update to that same cell no
longer replaces the draft; an untouched editor still picks up a new value.
Focus is restored across a re-sort that moves the editor's node, and dropped
(never re-aimed at an unrelated row) when its row leaves the rendered page.
`spellcheck`/`autocapitalize`/`autoCorrect` forward to the filter input and, for a `'text'`
(the default) `editType`, the inline cell editor -- no effect on a `'number'` cell editor.
`groupBy` inserts non-focusable group header rows before each group; use
`groupLabel` when the raw group key needs custom content.

`columns[].heatValue` opts a column into heat-tint mode: its numeric return value is normalized
against a shared scale spanning every `heatValue`-defining column across every currently-rendered
row (auto-derived, or overridden via `heatTintScale`) and painted as a `color-mix()` background via
the retheme-able `--lr-table-heat-tint-lo`/`-hi` custom properties (matching `lr-heatmap`'s own
ramp-token convention). `rowTotal`/`grandTotal` add a trailing column mirroring `expandedContent`'s
leading one: `rowTotal(row)` renders per-row, `grandTotal(rows)` renders at its intersection with
the footer row (only when a column also defines `footer`) ŌĆö both share `footer`'s own
"consumer computes/renders" contract rather than assuming addition.

The built-in empty state is addressable rather than fixed: every `<lr-empty>` the table renders
carries `part="empty"` and re-exports its own inner parts as `empty-heading`/`empty-description`/
`empty-icon`/`empty-actions`/`empty-base`, the two *data*-empty branches (no rows at all, and
filtered/paginated down to zero) render it as the fallback content of a named `empty` slot so a
consumer can replace it wholesale, and `emptyCompact` overrides each branch's built-in `compact`
default. The no-columns branch is deliberately **not** slot-replaceable ŌĆö it reports a
configuration problem (`noColumnsHeading`), not "this query returned nothing", and a single slot
covering all three would collapse that distinction.

`layout` sets a floor on the `<table>`'s `table-layout`: `'fixed'` forces it even with no column
widths, while the default `'auto'` still resolves to `fixed` whenever a column declares a `width`
or a drag-resize is in flight (column resizing does not work under `table-layout: auto`).

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `empty` | Replaces the built-in empty state on the two *data*-empty branches (no rows at all, and filtered/paginated down to zero). Left unfilled, the built-in `[part='empty']` `<lr-empty>` renders as this slot's fallback content. The no-columns branch renders its own `noColumnsHeading` state and is not slot-replaceable. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper around the `<table>` and its footer controls. |
| `caption` | The `<caption>` element, rendered only when `caption` is set. |
| `cell` | Each body `<td>`. |
| `cell-editor` | The native inline cell editor: shown after a double-click on an `editable: true` cell, and rendered persistently in every body cell of an `editable: 'always'` column. |
| `empty` | The built-in `<lr-empty>` host, in all three empty states (no columns configured, no rows at all, and filtered/paginated down to zero rows). The two data-empty states render it as the `empty` slot's fallback, so it disappears once that slot is filled. Note that the no-columns and no-rows states return the empty element as the shadow root's own root, with no `[part='base']` wrapper around it ŌĆö `::part(base)` does not apply in those two states, only in the filtered-to-zero one. |
| `empty-actions` | Exported from the built-in `<lr-empty>`'s `actions` part. |
| `empty-base` | Exported from the built-in `<lr-empty>`'s own `base` part. |
| `empty-description` | Exported from the built-in `<lr-empty>`'s `description` part. |
| `empty-heading` | Exported from the built-in `<lr-empty>`'s `heading` part. |
| `empty-icon` | Exported from the built-in `<lr-empty>`'s `icon` part. |
| `expand-toggle-cell` | Each row's (and the header's) leading chevron-toggle cell, rendered only when `expandedContent` is set. |
| `expanded-cell` | The single `colspan`-spanning `<td>` inside `expanded-row`, containing `expandedContent(row)`. |
| `expanded-row` | The full-width panel `<tr>` rendered beneath a row whose key is in `expandedKeys`. |
| `filter` | The optional row-filter input. |
| `filter-label` | The `<label>` wrapping the filter input. |
| `foot` | The `<tfoot>`, only rendered when at least one column defines `footer`. |
| `footer-cell` | A single footer cell. |
| `footer-row` | The single footer row. |
| `group-cell` | The full-width group header cell. |
| `group-row` | A non-focusable group header row. |
| `head` | The `<thead>` element. |
| `header-cell` | Each `<th>` header cell. |
| `loading` | The loading-state wrapper. Under `loadingAppearance="spinner"` (the default) it is the visible block holding the spinner; under `"skeleton"` it is the visually-hidden `role="status"` node, since the placeholder rows are the visible affordance. |
| `more-button` | The "load more" control, shown when `hasMore` is true. |
| `pagination` | The optional pagination component. |
| `resize-handle` | The focusable separator used to resize a `resizable` column. |
| `reveal-columns-button` | The button that toggles `priority`-hidden columns back into view. |
| `row` | Each body `<tr>`. |
| `row-expand-icon` | The chevron icon inside `row-expand-toggle`. |
| `row-expand-toggle` | The `<button>` inside `expand-toggle-cell`, absent for a row that fails `canExpand`. |
| `row-total-cell` | Each body row's trailing `<td>` holding `rowTotal(row)`, rendered only when `rowTotal` is set. The corresponding footer-row cell (holding `grandTotal`) is a `footer-cell` instead, matching every other footer cell. |
| `skeleton` | Each `<lr-skeleton>` placeholder inside a `loadingAppearance="skeleton"` body cell. Its rows and cells reuse the ordinary `row`/`cell`/`row-total-cell` parts (that is what keeps them geometrically identical to real rows), so this is the part to target for the placeholder's own look ŌĆö e.g. `::part(skeleton) { --lr-skeleton-h: 2em; }`. |
| `sort-icon` | The chevron shown in the active sortable column's header cell. |
| `table` | The `<table role="grid">` element. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-table-resize-min-width` (default: `var(--lr-size-3rem)`) ŌĆö Default minimum width for a resizable column without an explicit pixel `minWidth`.
- `--lr-table-resize-handle-opacity` (default: `0.12`) ŌĆö Hover/focus opacity of the resize handle.
- `--lr-table-max-height` (default: `none`) ŌĆö Cap on the scroll container's block size, past which the table body scrolls.
- `--lr-table-heat-tint-lo` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Low endpoint of the heat-tint ramp used by `heatValue` columns.
- `--lr-table-heat-tint-hi` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö High endpoint of the heat-tint ramp used by `heatValue` columns.
- `--lr-table-heat-t` ŌĆö This cell's position on the heat-tint ramp, as a percentage string. Set inline by the component on each `[data-heat]` cell; not consumer-settable.
- `--lr-table-row-selected-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a row whose `aria-selected` is `true`. Shadow Parts forbids an attribute selector after `::part()`, so `::part(row)[aria-selected]` is invalid CSS and the selected row could otherwise only be restyled by hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token.
- `--lr-table-row-stripe-bg` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Background of alternating body rows. The token is read only on rows carrying the internal stripe marker, so it can be set on the table or an ancestor without affecting group, expanded, hover, or selected rows.
- `--lr-table-header-sorted-bg` (default: `transparent`) ŌĆö Background of the currently-sorted column's header cell (`[aria-sort]` other than `none`). Same rationale as `--lr-table-row-selected-bg`: `::part(header-cell)[aria-sort]` is invalid CSS, so this token is the supported way to recolor the sorted header without hijacking a library-wide token.
- `--lr-table-header-sorted-color` (default: `inherit`) ŌĆö Text color of the currently-sorted column's header cell.
- `--lr-table-sticky-offset` (default: `0`) ŌĆö Distance a `sticky` column pins from the inline edge. Measured and set inline per column by the component so multiple sticky columns stack instead of overlapping; falls back to `0` for the first one, or before the first measurement pass.

attributes.name: accessible-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the `role="grid"` ŌĆö a typed alternative to setting `aria-label` on the
host. When set it becomes the grid's `aria-label`; a host `aria-label` is used as a fallback
when this is unset. Consumer-supplied text, so it is NOT run through `this.localize()`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the same inputs' native `autocapitalize`. Empty string omits the attribute
(browser default).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the same inputs' native `autocorrect` (Safari/WebKit-specific). Empty string
omits the attribute (browser default). Named `autoCorrect` (capital `C`), not `autocorrect`,
to dodge a TS `lib.dom.d.ts` collision -- same fix as `<lr-textarea>`/`<lr-model-select>`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: caption
attributes.description:
Optional visible caption rendered as the table's `<caption>`. Also names the grid (via
`aria-labelledby`) when no `accessibleLabel`/host `aria-label` is set. Consumer-supplied
text, not localized.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: columns-hidden
attributes.description:
Whether a `priority` column is *actually* hidden right now by
table.styles.ts's `@container` rules, or `showAllColumns` force-visible
mode is currently active ŌĆö the same computed value that gates whether
`[part='reveal-columns-button']` renders at all (see `render()`), kept
in sync by `recomputeColumnsHidden()`. Computed/read-only by
convention: consumers may read it (and listen for
`lr-columns-hidden-change`), but setting it directly has no lasting
effect, since it's recomputed on the very next render or
`[part='base']` resize.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: empty-compact
attributes.description:
Overrides the built-in `[part='empty']` state's `compact` rendering. Leave `undefined` (the
default) to keep each branch's own built-in behavior: the whole-table states (no columns, no
rows) render spacious, while the in-table filtered/paginated-to-zero state ŌĆö which sits below
the filter field inside `[part='base']` ŌĆö renders compact. `empty-compact="false"` forces the
spacious rendering everywhere. Has no effect once the `empty` slot is filled.

Type: `boolean | undefined`

attributes.name: empty-description
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: empty-heading
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: filter-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: filter-placeholder
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: filter-text
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: filterable
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: has-more
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: hide-columns-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: layout
attributes.description:
Floor for the `<table>`'s `table-layout`. `'fixed'` forces the fixed algorithm even when no
column declares a `width`, so every column shares the available width evenly and long cell
content is clipped/wrapped instead of stretching its column. The default `'auto'` is only a
floor: it still resolves to `fixed` whenever a column declares a `width`, a column has been
drag-resized, or a resize gesture is in flight ŌĆö resizing does not work under
`table-layout: auto`.

Two consequences of the fixed algorithm are worth knowing before opting in: with no declared
widths the *first* row (header row included) determines every column's width, so revealing a
`priority`-hidden column via `[part='reveal-columns-button']` re-measures and changes all of
them; and `columns[].minWidth`/`maxWidth` are silently ignored by `table-layout: fixed`
(declare `width` instead when you need a specific column sized).

Type: `'auto' | 'fixed'`  
Default: `'auto'`

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: loading-appearance
attributes.description:
How `loading` renders. `'spinner'` (the default, unchanged output) replaces the whole grid
with an indeterminate spinner. `'skeleton'` instead renders the real table ŌĆö the same
`<colgroup>` (declared *and* drag-resized widths included), the same `<thead>`, the filter
field and the pagination footer ŌĆö and fills `<tbody>` with placeholder rows, so a cold load
sketches the grid's shape rather than collapsing to a spinner and reflowing when the rows
land. Kept separate from `loading` rather than widening it to a string union, so
`?loading=${ŌĆ”}` bindings and `el.loading === true` checks keep working.

Column *widths* only stay pixel-identical across the load if the browser isn't sizing them
from cell content: declare `columns[].width`, or set `layout="fixed"`. Under the default
`table-layout: auto`, placeholder cells have no intrinsic width, so the columns re-measure
when real content arrives ŌĆö exactly as they do between any two different data sets.

Type: `TableLoadingAppearance`  
Default: `'spinner'`

attributes.name: loading-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: more-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: no-columns-description
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: no-columns-heading
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: page
attributes.description:
Controlled current page used when `pageSize` is positive.

Type: `number`  
Default: `1`

attributes.name: page-size
attributes.description:
Set to a positive value to enable the controlled pagination footer.

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: pagination-mode
attributes.description:
Type: `'client' | 'server'`  
Default: `'client'`

attributes.name: reveal-columns-label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: selection-mode
attributes.description:
Type: `TableSelectionMode`  
Default: `'none'`

attributes.name: show-all-columns
attributes.description:
Forces `priority`-hidden columns back into view, overriding the
`@container` hide rules in table.styles.ts. Toggles itself on
`[part='reveal-columns-button']` activation by default ŌĆö no external
wiring is required for the button to work. Also settable from outside
(property or the reflected `show-all-columns` attribute) to restore a
previously-persisted preference, and readable back at any time ŌĆö or via
the `lr-columns-revealed` event, fired whenever the button toggles it
ŌĆö to persist the current one.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: skeleton-rows
attributes.description:
Number of placeholder rows rendered by `loadingAppearance="skeleton"`. `0` (the default)
derives the count instead: the normalized `pageSize` when pagination is on (capped at 20, so
a large page size can't emit thousands of placeholder cells), otherwise 3. Any positive value
is used verbatim and is not capped. Ignored entirely under the default spinner appearance.

Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: sort-dir
attributes.description:
Type: `'asc' | 'desc'`  
Default: `'asc'`

attributes.name: sort-key
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the filter input's, and (when the active column's `editType` is `'text'`, the
default) the inline cell-editor input's, native `spellcheck`. Defaults to `true`, matching
the native element's own default. `spellcheck="false"` is parsed as `false` (see
`spellcheckConverter` above). No effect on a `'number'` cell editor.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: storage-key
attributes.description:
Persists `showAllColumns` to `localStorage` across reloads when set. Namespaced as
`lr-table:${storageKey}` -- mirrors `lr-app-rail`'s identical `storage-key` pattern.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: total-items
attributes.description:
Total item count for server pagination; `-1` derives it from filtered rows.

Type: `number`  
Default: `-1`

elements.name: lr-tabs
elements.description:
`<lr-tabs>` ŌĆö a tab strip whose panels are direct light-DOM children,
each carrying `slot="<id>"` (the panel's stable id) and `label="<text>"`
(the tab button's text). One named `<slot>` is rendered per distinct
`slot` name found among the current children ŌĆö a child with no `label`,
or a name with no matching child, simply never produces a tab.

A tab button's *visible* content can carry a leading icon without ever
changing its *accessible name* (which always stays exactly `label`'s
text, nothing else): give a tab an extra direct-child sibling of
`<lr-tabs>` carrying `slot="<id>-icon"` (that sibling's own content --
an inline SVG, an emoji span, a custom icon element, anything -- is
entirely up to the consumer). It's rendered ahead of the label inside
that tab's button, wrapped in an `aria-hidden="true"` part so it's
excluded from accessible-name computation no matter what it contains. A
tab with no matching `<id>-icon` sibling renders no icon wrapper at all,
so existing text-only tabs are completely unaffected. (A named slot,
rather than a second attribute holding an icon-name lookup, was chosen
because this library's `internal/icons.ts` is a small closed set of
chrome glyphs for this library's *own* components, not a public
name-keyed registry -- a slot lets a consumer supply an arbitrary,
domain-specific icon instead of being limited to that internal set.)

Implements the WAI-ARIA APG tabs pattern with automatic activation:
Left/Right (swapped under RTL) move focus *and* selection together,
Home/End jump to the first/last enabled tab, and a roving `tabindex`
follows whichever tab is currently selected.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Direct children with `slot="<id>" label="<text>"` (and optionally `disabled`); one becomes each tab's panel. |
| `<id>-icon` | Optional sibling direct child supplying a tab's leading icon content; excluded from the tab button's accessible name. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper around the tablist and panels. |
| `panel` | A single `role="tabpanel"` wrapper (one per tab, hidden unless active). |
| `tab` | A single tab button. |
| `tab-icon` | The optional leading-icon wrapper inside a tab button; only rendered when that tab has a matching `<id>-icon` sibling. |
| `tablist` | The `role="tablist"` row of tab buttons. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-scroll-fade-size` (default: `2rem`) ŌĆö Width of the static fade at each horizontal scroll edge.
- `--lr-tabs-selected-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Text color of the selected tab. Scoped to `[aria-selected='true']` only, so it never repaints a hovered unselected tab (which is what hijacking `--lr-color-brand` library-wide used to do).
- `--lr-tabs-indicator-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Color of the selected tab's underline, themeable independently of its text color.
- `--lr-tabs-hover-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö Text color of a hovered, non-disabled tab. Independent of the selected-state props above.

attributes.name: active
attributes.description:
The active tab's `slot`/id. Falls back to the first enabled tab whenever the current value doesn't resolve to one.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the `role="tablist"` strip. Attribute-reflects from a host-level
`aria-label` so a plain-markup consumer gets ARIA-name forwarding without setting a JS
property. Unset, the tablist renders without an `aria-label` (the role carries no localized
default name).

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-tag
elements.description:
`<lr-tag>` ŌĆö a semantic alias for the compact badge treatment.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Badge content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The badge surface. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-badge-background` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö The badge's background. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's `-quiet` tint.
- `--lr-badge-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-border)`) ŌĆö The badge's border color. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's loud color.
- `--lr-badge-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-text)`) ŌĆö The badge's text color. Each non-neutral `variant` sets it to that variant's loud color.
- `--lr-badge-font-size` (default: `var(--lr-font-size-sm)`) ŌĆö The badge's label font size. Each `size` sets it to that step's font size.
- `--lr-badge-padding-inline` (default: `var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö The badge's inline padding. Each `size` sets it to that step's inline padding.
- `--lr-badge-min-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-1-25rem)`) ŌĆö The badge's minimum block size. Each `size` sets it to that step's minimum block size.
- `--lr-badge-radius` (default: `var(--lr-radius-pill)`) ŌĆö Corner radius of the badge surface. Does not vary by `size` tier.

Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`

elements.name: lr-task-list
elements.description:
`<lr-task-list>` ŌĆö a live, collapsible tracker for an agent's plan: ordered steps with
per-step lifecycle status and one level of nested sub-steps, embedded in the transcript.
`items` is controlled and never mutated by this component, mirroring `<lr-stepper>`'s `steps`
contract -- but unlike stepper's single-`current` navigation control, task-list is a read-only
status report: several steps may be `running` at once, there is no selection, and status changes
are announced through an internal `<lr-live-region>`.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `detail-<id>` | Dynamic, one per item id (e.g. `slot="detail-step-3"`). Rich detail under that item's label, after its `detail` text -- typically a `<lr-tool-call-chip>` or file `<lr-chip>`. Plain-HTML friendly, no render props. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `body` | The list of items, `hidden` while collapsed. |
| `header` | The clickable header (a `<button>` when `collapsible`, a plain heading otherwise). |
| `item` | One item row (`role="listitem"`); carries `data-status`, `data-id`, `data-depth` (`"0"` for a top-level item, `"1"` for a child). |
| `item-children` | The nested `role="list"` wrapper around a top-level item's children. |
| `item-detail` | The item's optional `detail` text. |
| `item-label` | The item's `label` text. |
| `label` | The `label` text. |
| `status-icon` | The per-item status glyph. |
| `summary` | The visible "N of M completed" summary, counting only top-level items. |
| `toggle` | The chevron indicator inside the header. Only rendered when `collapsible`. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-task-list-spin` (default: `1s linear`) ŌĆö Running-status icon spin animation duration/timing.
- `--lr-task-list-compact-header-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs) var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö `[part="header"]` padding while `compact`.
- `--lr-task-list-compact-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between `[part="body"]`'s item rows while `compact`.
- `--lr-task-list-compact-body-padding` (default: `var(--lr-space-2xs) var(--lr-space-s) var(--lr-space-s)`) ŌĆö `[part="body"]` padding while `compact`.

attributes.name: appearance
attributes.description:
Visual chrome, mirroring `lr-card`'s `appearance` vocabulary. `'card'` (the default) keeps the
bordered, filled box. `'plain'` removes `[part="base"]`'s border, background, and corner
radius, so a list embedded in the transcript inside a frame that already draws a border (an
agent-run panel, a message bubble) doesn't double it.

Type: `TaskListAppearance`  
Default: `'card'`

attributes.name: collapsible
attributes.description:
When `false`, the header renders as a static heading (no button, no toggle affordance) and
`expanded` can still be set programmatically by the host, just not toggled via the UI.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Tighter header/body padding and item gap for dense contexts (a plan tracker nested in an
already-padded transcript row) -- same convention as `lr-agent-run`/`lr-source-card`'s
`compact`. Defaults to `false`, i.e. the full padding. Purely a density knob: the border and
background stay, so use `appearance="plain"` instead to drop the chrome entirely.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Whether the body (item list) is currently shown. Defaults open -- this is a progress surface,
not a details disclosure a reader opts into.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Header text. Localized (`taskListLabel`) while at its default `'Tasks'`; any other value is
shown as-is.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Tasks'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

elements.name: lr-terminal
elements.description:
`<lr-terminal>` ŌĆö read-only ANSI console for streamed agent/tool output. Not a PTY: no
stdin/keystroke handling, no cursor-addressed full-screen apps.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `announcer` | The visually-hidden `role="status"` region used when `announce-output` is set. |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `copy-button` | The copy-to-clipboard button. |
| `download-button` | The download button. |
| `jump-to-latest` | The pill shown while `follow` is disengaged and new output has arrived. |
| `line` | One rendered line; carries `data-line-number`, `data-match`, `data-highlight-tone`. Rendered through `<lr-virtual-list>`'s `renderItem`, so it lives inside that element's own shadow root rather than this component's -- this component's own stylesheet reaches it via `lr-virtual-list::part(line)`, one hop of the standard CSS Shadow Parts selector. |
| `toolbar` | The header row, only rendered when copy/download are enabled. |
| `viewport` | The `role="log"` scrollable region wrapping the virtualized line list. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-terminal-height` (default: `var(--lr-size-20rem)`) ŌĆö Block size of `[part="viewport"]`, the scrollable log region. Not declared on `:host`, so it is inherited ŌĆö set it on the host or any ancestor.
- `--lr-terminal-highlight-accent-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of an `accent`-tone highlighted line. Decoupled from the shared `--lr-color-brand-quiet` token also used by `[part="copy-button"]`/`[part="download-button"]`'s hover state, and from any `::part('line')` override (the background is applied inline, so a stylesheet rule can't beat it without `!important`).
- `--lr-terminal-highlight-success-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-success-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a `success`-tone highlighted line.
- `--lr-terminal-highlight-warning-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-warning-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a `warning`-tone highlighted line.
- `--lr-terminal-highlight-danger-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-danger-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a `danger`-tone highlighted line.
- `--lr-terminal-highlight-neutral-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-surface)`) ŌĆö Background of a `neutral`-tone highlighted line.

elements.name: lr-test-results
elements.description:
`<lr-test-results>` ŌĆö a pass/fail suite summary with per-status counts, status filter
toggles, and per-test rows whose failures auto-expand by default and can host rich slotted
detail (e.g. a diff or code block) alongside the plain failure message.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `detail-{testId}` | Rich failure detail for that test, rendered after its plain message text once the row is expanded. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root wrapper; carries `role="group"`. Its `aria-label` defaults to the localized "Test results", but a host `aria-label` on `<lr-test-results>` itself wins over that default. |
| `count` | One status count; carries `data-status`. |
| `failure` | The failure-detail wrapper; hidden while collapsed. |
| `failure-message` | The failure's plain message text. |
| `filter` | The filter-toggle row. |
| `filter-toggle` | One status filter toggle; carries `data-status` and `aria-pressed`. |
| `suite` | One suite section. |
| `suite-header` | The suite's name row. |
| `summary` | The status-count strip. |
| `test` | One test row; carries `data-status`. |
| `test-duration` | The duration text. |
| `test-expand-toggle` | The expand/collapse button for a row's failure detail. Rendered for any failed test, or any test with slotted `detail-{testId}` content. |
| `test-name` | The activatable test-name button. |
| `test-status` | The status glyph and its visible status-word text; carries `data-status`. |

---

- `--lr-test-results-filter-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand-quiet)`) ŌĆö Background of a pressed (active) status filter toggle.
- `--lr-test-results-filter-active-border` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of a pressed (active) status filter toggle.
- `--lr-test-results-filter-active-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Text color of a pressed (active) status filter toggle. Restyling the pressed state otherwise requires overriding the library-wide brand tokens, since `::part(filter-toggle)[aria-pressed]` is invalid CSS.

elements.name: lr-textarea
elements.description:
`<lr-textarea>` ŌĆö a multiline plain-text input primitive, form-associated via the
`FormAssociated` mixin (see `<lr-chat-composer>`/`<lr-date-input>` for the same shape), so it
participates in native `<form>` submission/validation/reset like any other text control --
`name`/`value`/`disabled`/`required`/`checkValidity()`/`reportValidity()` all come from that mixin.

Ships an opt-in `label`/`hint`/`errorText` form-control chrome (props + matching named slots +
`form-control-label`/`hint`/`error` parts), mirroring `<lr-select>`'s exact pattern -- left
unset, the chrome remains hidden. A consumer preferring their own form-field layout can still
ignore these and wrap the element. A host `aria-label` is forwarded to the internal textbox;
external `aria-labelledby`/`aria-describedby` idrefs are not copied across the shadow boundary.

`minlength`/`maxlength` are forwarded to the internal native `<textarea>` and bridged into this
element's own `ElementInternals` as `tooShort`/`tooLong` by `updateValidity()`.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `error` | Custom error content. |
| `hint` | Custom hint content. |
| `label` | Custom label content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `error` | The error message. |
| `form-control` | The outer wrapper around label, textarea, error and hint. |
| `form-control-label` | The `<label>` element. |
| `hint` | The hint message. |
| `textarea` | The native `<textarea>` element. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-textarea-max-block-size` (default: `none`) ŌĆö Maximum auto-grown block size before the textarea scrolls.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name overriding the label/placeholder-derived default. Takes precedence over both
`label` and `placeholder` when set, matching `<lr-date-input>`'s `accessibleLabel`.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.name: autocapitalize
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the native `<textarea>`'s own `autocapitalize`. Empty string omits the
attribute (browser default).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
Native editing-assistance attributes forwarded to the wrapped textarea. Empty strings omit
the corresponding attribute and retain the browser default.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the native `<textarea>`'s own `autocorrect` (Safari/WebKit-specific). Empty
string omits the attribute (browser default).

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: enterkeyhint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: error-text
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: maxlength
attributes.description:
Upper counterpart of `minlength` (native `maxlength`/`tooLong`), with the same parsing and
the same default of `undefined`. Note that native `maxlength` also *prevents* typing beyond
the limit; it reports `tooLong` for values that arrive some other way (paste of a longer
value, a programmatic assignment).

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description:
Minimum text length, forwarded to the native `<textarea>`'s own `minlength` and reported as
`tooShort` by `updateValidity()`. Defaults to `undefined` (no lower bound). Like native
`minlength`, an empty value never violates it ŌĆö pair it with `required` to also reject
empty.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Type: `string`

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: required
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: resize
attributes.description:
Native CSS `resize` behavior for the textarea, plus `'auto'`: a `ResizeObserver`-driven
grow-to-content mode with no manual drag handle (mirrors `wa-textarea`'s `resize="auto"`).

Type: `TextareaResize`  
Default: `'vertical'`

attributes.description:
Visible text rows.

Type: `number`  
Default: `3`

attributes.description:
Forwarded to the native `<textarea>`'s own `spellcheck`. Defaults to `true`, matching the
native element's own default.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: wrap
attributes.description:
Forwarded to the native `<textarea>`'s own `wrap`.

Type: `TextareaWrap`  
Default: `'soft'`

elements.name: lr-thinking-panel
elements.description:
`<lr-thinking-panel>` ŌĆö a collapsible panel for an AI agent's
intermediate reasoning/"thinking" transcript, kept visually and
semantically distinct from its final response. Same collapsible
header-button-plus-region shape as `<lr-source-list>`; the default slot
is entirely free-form (a consumer-composed `<lr-streaming-text>`,
`<lr-markdown>`, or plain text) ŌĆö this component has no dependency on
either and imposes no structure on what's slotted.

`mode` (`'live'` while reasoning is actively streaming in, `'post-hoc'`
once it's complete and being reviewed after the fact) drives two concrete
behavior differences, not just a styling hook:
- **Header hint.** While `duration-ms` is unset, `'live'` shows a pulsing
  "ThinkingŌĆ”" placeholder in `[part="duration"]`; `'post-hoc'` shows
  nothing there instead (a finished review with no known duration has
  nothing useful to say in that slot). Once `duration-ms` is set, both
  modes show the same static `"Thought for ŌĆ”"` text ŌĆö a `'post-hoc'`
  consumer that captured a duration is free to supply it too.
- **Auto-scroll.** Only `'live'` mode auto-follows new content appended to
  the default slot while `expanded` (see below); `'post-hoc'` never
  scrolls on its own, since reviewing finished reasoning is expected to
  start from the top like reading any other completed document. This
  library otherwise defaults to *not* editorializing about a host's data
  (see `<lr-empty>`'s plain-`description` stance), but scroll position
  is presentation, not data, so this one behavior difference earns its
  keep rather than being left as a bare visual/semantic hint the host
  would have to reimplement identically itself.

Live-mode auto-scroll ("stick to bottom") is the classic chat-transcript
convention: while `mode="live"` and `expanded`, new content keeps the
panel scrolled to its latest line ŌĆö *unless* the user has manually
scrolled up to (re-)read earlier content, in which case their position is
never yanked away from them. This is tracked continuously via a `scroll`
listener on `[part="body"]` (not recomputed from the mutation itself,
which necessarily observes the DOM only *after* it has already changed):
every user-driven scroll records whether the body was left within
`NEAR_BOTTOM_PX` of its own max scroll position, and only a mutation that
arrives while that's still true triggers a follow-up scroll-to-bottom.
Opening an already-`'live'` panel (or one that later becomes `'live'`)
always resets this to "anchored" and jumps to the latest content, the same
way a chat app's own transcript does when you re-open it.

New content is detected via a `MutationObserver` on this element's own
light DOM (`childList`+`subtree`+`characterData`) rather than the default
slot's `slotchange` event, because `slotchange` only fires when the set of
top-level assigned nodes changes ŌĆö never for a text node mutating *inside*
an already-slotted element, which is the more likely shape for streamed
reasoning (a consumer appending chunks to an existing node's `textContent`
rather than re-slotting a whole new element per token). The one thing this
can't see is a mutation entirely inside a slotted custom element's own
shadow root (e.g. a `<lr-markdown>` re-rendering its shadow tree after a
`content` property change) ŌĆö Shadow DOM encapsulation blocks that by
design, and there is no way for this component to reach across that
boundary. A slotted element whose *own* internal updates should drive this
panel's auto-scroll needs to append/mutate visible light-DOM text itself
(as `<lr-streaming-text>` is expected to), or the host can call this
panel's own `scrollToBottom()` directly.

`aria-controls` linking the header to the body region uses `nextId()`
(`../../internal/a11y.js`) for a collision-safe id, the same convention
`<lr-source-list>` and `<lr-widget>` already establish for every
toggle-controls-region pairing in this library.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | The reasoning/thinking content. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The outer container. |
| `body` | The wrapper around the default slot, `hidden` while collapsed. Independently keyboard-focusable (`tabindex="0"`, `role="group"` named from `label`) since it's its own capped-height scrollable region. |
| `duration` | The formatted duration / "ThinkingŌĆ”" placeholder, when shown. |
| `header` | The clickable header (`<button>`) toggling `expanded`. |
| `label` | The `label` text. |
| `toggle` | The chevron indicator inside the header. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-thinking-panel-max-block-size` (default: `var(--lr-size-16rem)`) ŌĆö Cap on how tall the expanded reasoning transcript grows before `[part="body"]` scrolls internally.

attributes.name: duration-ms
attributes.description:
How long the reasoning took, in milliseconds, once known. Omitted
entirely (nothing rendered in `'post-hoc'`, a pulsing placeholder in
`'live'`) while unset.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: expanded
attributes.description:
Whether the reasoning transcript is currently shown. Starts collapsed,
matching `<lr-source-list>`'s default -- a consumer running `'live'`
reasoning sets this `true` itself to stream it in view as it arrives.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Header text. Localized (`thinkingPanelLabel`) when left at its default
`'Thinking'`; any other value is shown as-is.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Thinking'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: mode
attributes.description:
`'live'` while reasoning is actively streaming in right now; `'post-hoc'`
once it's complete and being reviewed after the fact. See the class doc
for the concrete behavior differences this drives.

Type: `ThinkingPanelMode`  
Default: `'live'`

elements.name: lr-thread-list
elements.description:
`<lr-thread-list>` ŌĆö the conversation sidebar: a grouped, searchable list of chat sessions with
pin/archive/delete/rename affordances. *Data mode* (non-empty `threads`, or empty `threads` with
nothing slotted) renders every row as a `<lr-conversation-item>` inside an internal
`<lr-virtual-list>` ŌĆö virtualized by construction, scroll position and per-row state survive a
`threads` replacement; zero rows renders the built-in empty state. *Slotted mode* (empty `threads`
*and* real slotted content) renders host-supplied `<lr-conversation-item>`s from the default slot
as-is: no grouping, virtualization, or row actions in that mode ŌĆö those are data-mode-only by design
(shadow DOM cannot inject group headers between slotted children).

No thread CRUD or persistence: every mutation (`lr-thread-pin`/`-archive`/`-delete`/`-rename`) is
a controlled event carrying the *requested* new state ŌĆö the host mutates `threads`.

Data mode: a host needing content with no home in `lr-conversation-item`'s own
`title`/`excerpt`/`meta`/`actions` surface sets `wrapRow` to wrap the already-built row. For
common row composition, `renderLeading`, `renderExcerpt`, `renderMeta`, and `renderRowContent`
provide focused virtualized render hooks -- `renderExcerpt` renders into the row item's own
`excerpt` slot (winning over the plain-string `excerpt` property) for rich per-row content such
as a highlighted search snippet. A host needing a fully custom
`actions` surface itself ŌĆö beyond `rowActions`'s closed `pin | archive | delete` set, e.g. a
`<lr-menu>` with Rename/Delete ŌĆö sets `renderActions` instead; its content is appended after any
built-in `rowActions` output in the same slot, and `wrapRow` continues to compose around the result.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| (default) | Slotted mode only: host-supplied `lr-conversation-item`s, rendered in order. Each top-level assigned element that doesn't already carry an explicit `role` is given `role="listitem"`, since `[part="list"]` is `role="list"` in this mode and `lr-conversation-item` deliberately doesn't self-apply that role (see its own class doc). |
| `empty` | Replaces the built-in empty state. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The root. |
| `empty` | The empty/no-matches state. |
| `group-header` | A date/custom group header in data mode. |
| `group-icon` | The decorative expand/collapse glyph. |
| `group-label` | The group label inside `group-toggle`. |
| `group-sticky` | `sticky-groups` only: the pinned copy of the current group's header, exported from the internal `lr-virtual-list`'s sticky layer. It wraps a full copy of the `group-header`/`group-toggle`/`group-label`/`group-icon` markup (which therefore styles both the real header row and the pinned copy), and carries `aria-hidden` ŌĆö style it for the pinned band itself, e.g. a shadow or a border under the band. |
| `group-toggle` | The controlled group expand/collapse button. |
| `list` | The list region. |
| `pin-glyph` | The small pin indicator shown in a pinned row's `meta` slot (data mode). |
| `row` | Exported from the internal `lr-virtual-list`'s `row` part (data mode). |
| `row-action` | A built-in pin/archive/delete icon button (data mode, when `rowActions` includes it). |
| `row-actions` | The wrapper around built-in and `renderActions` output. |
| `row-content` | The wrapper around `renderRowContent` output. |
| `row-excerpt` | The wrapper around `renderExcerpt` output, slotted into the row item's own `excerpt` slot. |
| `row-item-actions` | Data mode: the row item's `actions` wrapper. |
| `row-item-active-indicator` | Data mode: the row item's decorative active indicator, exported from `lr-conversation-item`. |
| `row-item-base` | Data mode: the row `<lr-conversation-item>`'s own `base` part ŌĆö the box whose padding sets row height. `row-item-*` parts are the item's *internals*; the `row-*` parts above wrap this component's own callback output and are a different surface. Styling row density here replaces the older `::part(row) { --lr-theme-*: ŌĆ” }` workaround, which retheme'd the whole row subtree (`renderActions` popups included). |
| `row-item-content` | Data mode: the row item's title/excerpt content column. |
| `row-item-excerpt` | Data mode: the row item's excerpt line. |
| `row-item-leading` | Data mode: the row item's `leading` wrapper. |
| `row-item-meta` | Data mode: the row item's `meta` wrapper. |
| `row-item-option` | Data mode: the row item's selectable `option` region. |
| `row-item-rename-button` | Data mode: the row item's pencil/rename affordance. |
| `row-item-timestamp` | Data mode: the row item's `<time>` element. |
| `row-item-title` | Data mode: the row item's title text. |
| `row-item-title-input` | Data mode: the row item's in-place rename `<input>`. |
| `row-leading` | The wrapper around `renderLeading` output. |
| `row-meta` | A wrapper around built-in or `renderMeta` metadata. |
| `row-wrapper` | The wrapper around `wrapRow` output (data mode, only when `wrapRow` is set). Row-only: group headers are never passed through `wrapRow`, so they never carry it. |
| `search` | The search field wrapper. |
| `search-input` | The `<input type="search">`. |
| `viewport` | The real scroll container, exported from the internal `lr-virtual-list`. It fills this component's height with no consumer CSS (and falls back to `lr-virtual-list`'s own 24rem `--lr-virtual-list-height` default when the container has no resolvable height). |

attributes.name: active-id
attributes.description:
Data mode: marks the matching row `active`/`aria-current` and scrolls it into view.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: compact
attributes.description:
Data mode only: forwarded to every row `<lr-conversation-item>`'s own `compact`, tightening
each row's padding and gaps in one place. Slotted mode is a deliberate no-op ŌĆö this component
renders host-supplied items as-is, so the host sets `compact` on its own items there, the same
division of responsibility slotted mode already has for every other row property.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: editable
attributes.description:
Forwarded to each data-mode row's inline rename.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: grouping
attributes.description:
Data mode: bucket rows under localized date headers, use `groupBy` with `'custom'`, or use
`'none'` for a flat list in host order.

Type: `ThreadListGrouping`  
Default: `'date'`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible name for the list region. Defaults to the localized `threadListLabel`.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: searchable
attributes.description:
Shows the built-in search field.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: show-archived
attributes.description:
Data mode: include `archived` threads (in their own trailing group under `grouping="date"`).

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: sticky-groups
attributes.description:
Data mode only: pins the current date/custom group's header to the top of the scroll viewport
while its rows are in view, pushing it off as the next group's header arrives. Group headers
are ordinary virtualized rows, so this renders a `aria-hidden` copy of the header into
`lr-virtual-list`'s sticky layer (`[part="group-sticky"]`) ŌĆö the real row keeps the
`role="heading"` semantics and the tab order, and the copy stays clickable so the pinned
toggle still requests a collapse. Default `false` leaves rendering exactly as it is without
this feature; `grouping="none"` has no headers to pin, so it is a no-op there.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

elements.name: lr-time-input
elements.description:
`<lr-time-input>` ŌĆö a native time-input alias with Lyra form chrome and events.

Adds no API of its own beyond re-typing the inherited `min`/`max` bounds for time values; every
other property, event, slot and part is `<lr-input>`'s.

---

**Slots**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `end` | Adornment after the native input and built-in actions. |
| `error` | Custom error content. |
| `hint` | Custom hint content. |
| `label` | Custom label content. |
| `start` | Adornment before the native input. |

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `clear-button` | The clear action, rendered for non-empty clearable text/search inputs. |
| `end` | Wrapper around the `end` adornment slot. |
| `error` | The error message. |
| `form-control` | The outer wrapper around label, input, error and hint. |
| `form-control-label` | The `<label>` element. |
| `hint` | The hint message. |
| `input` | The native `<input>` element. |
| `input-wrapper` | The row wrapping the native input and the password-toggle button. |
| `password-toggle` | The show/hide-password button, present only when `type="password"`. |
| `start` | Wrapper around the `start` adornment slot. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-input-control-min-height` ŌĆö Outer control height floor, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-input-control-height` ŌĆö Exact outer control height. Unset by default, which leaves `--lr-input-control-min-height` as a floor only; set it to a length to both floor and cap the control row (e.g. to pixel-match `<lr-select>`/`<lr-combobox>` in the same toolbar). Because it is never declared by the component itself, it can be set from an ancestor or an outer-tree rule as well as inline on the element.
- `--lr-input-padding-block` ŌĆö Block padding of the native input, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-input-padding-inline` ŌĆö Inline padding of the control row, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-input-font-size` ŌĆö Font size of the native input, scaled by `size`.
- `--lr-input-gap` (default: `var(--lr-space-xs)`) ŌĆö Gap between the start/end adornments and the native input in the control row. Unlike the size knobs above it does not vary by `size` tier. Override it to retune without a `::part(input-wrapper)` rule.
- `--lr-input-radius` (default: `var(--lr-radius)`) ŌĆö Corner radius of the control row. Does not vary by `size` tier.

attributes.name: aria-label
attributes.description:
Accessible name overriding the label/placeholder-derived default. Takes precedence over both
`label` and `placeholder` when set, matching `<lr-textarea>`'s `accessibleLabel`.

Type: `string | null`  
Default: `null`

attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocomplete
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: autocorrect
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: clearable
attributes.description:
Shows a built-in clear action for non-empty `text` and `search` inputs.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: enterkeyhint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: error-text
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: inputmode
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
Latest selectable time, in the same form as `min`, reported by the native input as
`rangeOverflow`. Defaults to `undefined` (no upper bound).

Type: `number | string | undefined`

attributes.name: maxlength
attributes.description:
Upper counterpart of `minlength` (native `maxlength`/`tooLong`), with the same parsing and the
same default of `undefined`. Note that native `maxlength` also *prevents* typing beyond the
limit; it reports `tooLong` for values that arrive some other way (paste of a longer value,
a programmatic assignment).

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
Earliest selectable time, as the native `<input type="time">` spells it (`HH:MM`, or
`HH:MM:SS` alongside a seconds-precision `step`). Forwarded verbatim to that native input,
whose own constraint validation reports `rangeUnderflow`. Defaults to `undefined` (no lower
bound). Unlike `<lr-input type="number">`'s numeric `min`, the attribute form is *not*
parsed as a number.

Type: `number | string | undefined`

attributes.name: minlength
attributes.description:
Minimum text length, forwarded to the internal native `<input>`'s own `minlength` and
consulted by that same native input's constraint validation (`tooShort`, see
`updateValidity()`). Defaults to `undefined` (no lower bound). Like native `minlength`, an
empty value never violates it ŌĆö pair it with `required` to also reject empty. Ignored by the
native input for `type="number"`/`type="time"`, exactly as the platform specifies.

Type: `number | undefined`

attributes.name: password-visible
attributes.description:
`type="password"` only ŌĆö whether the field currently reveals its raw text. Toggled by the
built-in `password-toggle` button; also settable by a consumer up front.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: pattern
attributes.description:
A regular expression the value must match in full, forwarded to the internal native
`<input>`'s own `pattern` and validated by it (`patternMismatch`). Defaults to `undefined`
(no pattern). Compiled by the browser with the `v` flag and anchored to the whole value, so
no `^`/`$` is needed; an empty value never violates it.

Type: `string | undefined`

attributes.name: placeholder
attributes.description:
Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: readonly
attributes.description:
Forwards native read-only behavior to the internal input and disables the clear action.

Type: `boolean`  
Default: `false`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size ŌĆö same `2xs`ŌĆō`xl` scale as `lr-select`/`lr-combobox`'s own `size`. `'2xs'` is
the tightest tier, for dense toolbar-embedded controls.

Type: `LyraInputSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: spellcheck
attributes.description:
Type: `boolean`  
Default: `true`

attributes.name: step
attributes.description:
Accepts `'any'` (the native way to disable step validation) in addition to a numeric step.

Type: `number | 'any' | undefined`

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
Type: `LyraInputType`  
Default: `'text'`

elements.name: lr-time-range
elements.description:
`<lr-time-range>` ŌĆö a two-handle brush/scrubber over a numeric domain.
Callers map their own time axis to `[min, max]`; no date logic lives here
(matches the no-date-library constraint used elsewhere in this library).

Optionally paired with a row of discrete presets (`presets`) ŌĆö e.g. "Last
7 days" / "Last 30 days" ŌĆö rendered above the track; picking one is just a
shortcut that sets both handles at once, the continuous brush underneath
is unaffected and both interaction modes coexist.

Form-associated only for the `<fieldset disabled>` cascade, not for a
submitted value: it attaches `ElementInternals` (like `<lr-combobox>`'s
minimal pattern, rather than the single-string-value `FormAssociated`
mixin, which doesn't fit a two-handle range) purely so an ancestor
`<fieldset disabled>` disables both handles and every preset button
through `effectiveDisabled`, the same way it would a native `<input>`,
without touching the consumer-facing `disabled` property/attribute itself.
Unlike `<lr-combobox>`, it never calls `internals.setFormValue()` and
has no `name` ŌĆö the selected range is not included in the owning form's
`FormData` on submit; read `start`/`end` directly (e.g. from `lr-change`)
instead of relying on native form submission.

Deliberately no label/hint/error chrome -- `startLabel`/`endLabel` here are per-handle
accessible-name overrides, not visible label text, the same carve-out `<lr-slider>` states for
its own single-handle `label`; a labeled-field consumer wraps this element in their own layout.

---

**CSS Shadow Parts**

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base` | The time-range wrapper. |
| `handle-end` | The end handle. |
| `handle-start` | The start handle. |
| `preset-button` | A preset button. |
| `presets` | The preset controls wrapper. |
| `range` | The selected range. |
| `track` | The complete range track. |

---

**CSS Custom Properties**

- `--lr-time-range-preset-active-bg` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Background of the active preset button (`[data-active]`, i.e. the preset whose range matches the current value). Declared as an inline `var()` fallback (never on `:host`), so setting it on the element or an ancestor recolors only the active preset without hijacking the library-wide `--lr-color-brand` token.
- `--lr-time-range-preset-active-border-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-brand)`) ŌĆö Border color of the active preset button.
- `--lr-time-range-preset-active-color` (default: `var(--lr-color-on-brand)`) ŌĆö Text color of the active preset button.
- `--lr-time-range-size-scale` (default: `1`) ŌĆö Unitless multiplier applied proportionally to the handle, track, and preset-button dimensions based on the current `size` tier; the drag hit-area is floored at 24px (WCAG 2.5.8).

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Type: `boolean`

attributes.name: end
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `100`

attributes.description:
Accessible name for the end handle, used as its `aria-label`.
Overridable for i18n/custom copy; defaults to the same literal text
this component always rendered before the property existed.

Type: `string`  
Default: `'Range end'`

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description:
Optional locale override. Otherwise the nearest `locale`/`lang` ancestor is used.

Type: `string`  
Default: `''`

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `100`

attributes.name: min
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Visual size ŌĆö proportionally scales the handle, track, and preset buttons via a single
multiplier; the drag hit-area never shrinks below 24px (WCAG 2.5.8). Not pixel-matched to
`lr-input`'s row-height scale -- this component's own dimensions aren't on that ladder.

Type: `LyraTimeRangeSize`  
Default: `'m'`

attributes.name: start
attributes.description:
Type: `number`  
Default: `0`

attributes.description:
Accessible name for the start handle, used as its `aria-label`.
Overridable for i18n/custom copy; defaults to the same literal text
this component always rendered before the property existed.

Type: `string`

attributes.name: step
attributes.description:
